event_title: Microlight Flying - Fly High Kiss the Sky
event_description:
Make your dream come true! Fly a Microlight, almost everyone has flown in an airplane. How do you feel about actually flying one? Turn your childhood dream into reality because here is your chance to fly a microlight plane and literally float in the clouds. You taxi your plane on the runway and if you are up for it you might even be allowed to do the landing as well. Nothing can give you the adrenaline kick like this one! A microlight is an aircraft that is designed to carry up to two people and has a maximum take-off weight not exceeding 450 kgs. These aircraft have relatively short take-off and landing capabilities making them ideal for use at smaller airfields for recreational purposes. Some countries differentiate between weight shift and 3- axis aircraft, calling the former 'microlight' and the latter 'ultralight'.

for bookings: https://adventuresome.in/explore/microlight-flying-bangalore/

or call on +91 8892203272

How to join?

event_title: Indiranagar Walk & Talk
event_description:
Start your Sunday with a long walk and curious conversations.

Bangalore Walk & Talk is a simple idea: we meet in the morning, walk together, and talk about interesting things. Books, fitness, work, technology, travel, economy, human nature, life in Bangalore, or anything else people are curious about.

Everyone is encouraged to briefly introduce themselves at the start and share something they’re currently curious about.

There’s no agenda and no formal networking. Just thoughtful people walking together and letting conversations flow naturally.

The walk will be roughly 4–5 kms at a relaxed pace. People naturally switch conversation partners along the way, so you’ll get to meet different people throughout the walk.

This is for people who enjoy long conversations, curiosity, and meeting interesting people outside the usual networking events.

Details
🕗 Start time: Sunday 7:30 am

🚶 Distance: ~5 kms

☕ Optional coffee after the walk

Come with comfortable shoes and curiosity.

event_title: Try Lacrosse - Lacrosse makes friends! 🥍
event_description:
Open for All Groups and Genders

Join us for Try Lacrosse Day and discover the excitement of this fast-paced sport. Whether you're young or old, male or female, experienced or a complete beginner, everyone is welcome!

Equipment: All equipment, including lacrosse sticks, will be provided.

A perfect time to learn the basics of lacrosse in a fun and inclusive environment. Our coaches will be there to guide you through every step, ensuring you have a great time.

Why Join Us?

* No experience required
– perfect for beginners!
* Free access to all necessary equipment.
* Open to all age groups and genders.
* A great way to stay active and meet new people.

Join Us and Experience the Fun of Lacrosse!

Bring your friends and family, and come enjoy a morning of lacrosse. We can’t wait to see you there!

event_title: Garvey Park Walk
event_description: Meet outside cafe at Garvey Park and walk to Bayswater dog park and back. Approx 8-9km at pace of 6km per hour. Dogs welcome, leave at 8am sharp. Coffee at the cafe afterwards.

event_title: Sunday Matilda Bay Walk
event_description:
Meet outside Bayside Kitchen at 8:15 AM for a 8:30 AM start.

We normally walk for about an hour.

We will walk from Bayside Kitchen to the playground and back then have coffee or breakfast at the Bayside Kitchen.

The walk is dog friendly.

event_title: Discover Dragon Boating: Join the Crew and Learn to Paddle!
event_description:
**Join the Perth Pirates Dragon Boat Club on the Swan River for an exhilarating paddle!**

Discover the thrill of dragon boating and meet the friendly team. Whether you're new to the sport or looking for a great full-body workout, this is the perfect chance to try something new in one of the most scenic spots in Maylands.

**Details:**

* **When:** 8:15 AM, Sundays
(Rock up 5 minutes earlier to sort registration)
* **Where:** Maylands Sports & Recreation Club, 50 Clarkson Rd, Maylands

Enjoy a **FREE tryout** and your first month **FREE (no obligation)**! Wear comfortable clothes, shoes that can get wet, and bring a water bottle. Don’t forget sunscreen on sunny days!

Any queries, contact : getpaddling@perthpiratesdragonboatclub.com.au

event_title: Bukit Kembara Trailhead @Ampang For the Girls!🥾✨(challenging : 4/5)
event_description:
We’ll be taking on a **challenging Level 4 hike** at Bukit Kembara via the **Kembara Hill – Lake Hut trail**.
This hike is designed **only for experienced hikers** with good fitness levels and prior hiking experience. Expect **steep inclines, uneven terrain, and a physically demanding climb**.
📍 **Date:** Sunday, 3rd May 2026
⏰ **Time:** 8:30 AM sharp
📸 You consent to being photographed or filmed during our events

Please arrive by **8:20 AM**. We will **start the hike at 8:30 AM sharp**, and there will be **no waiting for latecomers**. This is a tougher trail and timing is important for safety and group pacing.

### Hike Details

The **Kembara Hill – Lake Hut trail** is known for its **steep ascents and rewarding views**. This is not a beginner-friendly hike — participants should be comfortable with longer durations and elevation gain.
⏱ Estimated duration: **2.5 to 3.5 hours (including breaks)**
🔥 Difficulty: **Level 4 / 5 (Difficult)**
You can check out the full trail details here:
[https://www.alltrails.com/en-gb/trail/malaysia/selangor/bukit-kembara-pondok-tasik?sh=i7kzrv&utm_medium=trail_share&utm_source=alltrails_virality](https://www.alltrails.com/en-gb/trail/malaysia/selangor/bukit-kembara-pondok-tasik?sh=i7kzrv&utm_medium=trail_share&utm_source=alltrails_virality)

###

**What to Bring**

* At least **1.5–2L of water** 💧
* Light snacks / energy bars 🍫
* Proper hiking shoes 👟
* Cap, sunscreen & insect repellent ☀️
* Small backpack 🎒

**Transportation & Parking**
Transportation
If you’re taking public transport:

* Take the Kelana Jaya Line to **Ampang Park LRT Station** or **Cahaya LRT Station**
* From there, you’ll need to take a **Grab ride (approximately 10–15 minutes)** to the trailhead at Bukit Kembara

We recommend planning your journey earlier to ensure you arrive on time.
**Parking**
Parking is available near the trail entrance. Please come early to secure a spot.
***Ulu Kelang, 68000 Ampang Jaya, Selangor***

**Post-Hike Lunch**
After the hike, we’ll be heading for a **group lunch nearby** to relax, refuel, and socialize! 🥗✨
(Location will be shared in the WhatsApp group closer to the date.)

**Disclaimer**⚡
By joining this hike, you acknowledge that this is a **physically demanding activity** and you participate at your own risk. Please ensure you are fit and prepared.

event_title: Social Tennis
event_description: Hi All, we are playing on courts 5 & 6 on Sunday. Plan is for everybody to play three 25-30 minute doubles matches, but if the numbers don't add up, we play singles and American singles. Refer comments for gate access code. I have pre-paid for the courts. Cost is $8 per player which can be paid in cash to me 🙂 or via bank transfer to my account after we are finished. The 'overall' standard of tennis is pretty good. Amongst our regulars there is a mix of good, average and 'once a week sloggers' (me). Age range is 20-60+. My goal is to try and make sure that games are played with players of equal levels. This is sometimes not possible. If you have any questions please contact me or use the comments section.

event_description:

event_title: 🌎 (Online/Free) Free English Conversation Club with global people
event_description:
**📢IMPORTANT : CHECK THE SESSION TIME IN OUR WEBSITE(The time on the website and the meetup site may not match since we change it ocasionally)**

**Hi there! My name is Chloe.**
I'm a certified English teacher from United States.
I wanted to make an online language club where people can practice foreign languages by having real casual conversation!
I'm looking for people who want to practice conversational English by talking about interesting topics and hanging out with people from all over the world in a small online group.
**Here are some examples of what our weekly topics might be like:**
**"What would you do if you come across your crush?"**
**"Have you ever done the MBTI test? If so, what is your personality type?"**
**"Do you believe in love at first sight? Why or why not?"**
**"What are you most worried about right now?**
💎Here are some details💎
✔️ We meet via Zoom
✔️ The session will last for 1 hour and consists of 3 different rounds
✔️ Each round has different topics and discussion questions
✔️ Each round, the groups are randomly mixed, so you will have a chance to talk to different people every time
✔️ This isn't just about practicing English, but also about listening to different opinions and learning about different cultures
**✔️ The participation is FREE**
**❌ No credit card**
**❌ No Ads**

**📢IMPORTANT : CHECK THE SESSION TIME IN OUR WEBSITE(The time on the website and the meetup site may not match since we change it ocasionally)**

💎 How to Join 💎

1. [Click the link(here)](https://langclub.live/sessions)
2. [Sign up](https://langclub.live/signup)

3\. Click 'book' in the session that you want to participate\.
4\. Show up at the session time and click 'Join' in the session page
(The Zoom invitation will be sent to your e-mail once you apply too)
5\. Enjoy practicing English with people from all over the world\!

**📢IMPORTANT : CHECK THE SESSION TIME IN OUR WEBSITE(The time on the website and the meetup site may not match since we change it ocasionally)**

event_title: YOGA In The Park! HYDE PARK *Pay As You Feel*
event_description:
This easy to follow vinyasa yoga class is suitable for all levels & perfect for beginners! Calm your mind, release tension in your body and experience the joy of yoga 🧘‍♀️

Whether you're new to Perth, just travelling through or a local, this is a great place to try yoga and meet new friends in a relaxed and friendly atmosphere.

📍LOCATION: Hyde Park (opposite Chu Bakery) Click here for exact location: https://goo.gl/maps/xkmETtGQj3Z6P9xFA

✨PLEASE BRING: Water bottle & yoga mat or a towel (we’ll have a few spares - come 15mins early to grab one)

✨SCHEDULE: Classes run every Saturday & Sunday 9-10am from October-May with extra evening classes added during Jan-March.

✨WHAT TO EXPECT:
In this 1 hour beginner friendly Vinyasa Yoga class you’ll flow from one pose to the next, connecting movement with breath. You’ll work on your strength, balance and flexibility as well as practice mindfulness.

Don’t worry if you’ve never done yoga before, our classes are perfect for beginners! We’ll be demonstrating the whole class & giving lots of instructions so you can follow along and give it a go 🙂

✨NO PRE-BOOKING REQUIRED: You can just turn up!

✨COST:
All Yoga In The Park classes are ‘Pay As You Feel’ - please pay as you feel or can afford for the class, we accept cash & card payments either bedor or after class 🩷

🦋 Come along and be a part of the Blooming Happy Community :) Everyone’s welcome!

🤔 Have a question? Send us a message or post a comment :)

🙌 For class updates, follow us on INSTAGRAM:
www.instagram.com/bloominghappyyoga

——————————————————

*Your safety is your responsibility, if something doesn't feel right/good please ensure you come out of the pose, or take a variation*

www.bloominghappyyoga.com

event_title: 💻 Build Your AI Second Brain
event_description:
## 💻 Build Your AI Second Brain

This Weekend \| A Step\-by\-Step Workshop for Creators & Founders
***
## 💡 The Opportunity

We are all drowning in scattered notes, forgotten ideas, and endless cloud subscriptions. It doesn't have to be this way. With modern agentic coding tools ,you don't need a computer science degree to build your own "Local-First" life operating system. You can create a private, high-performance dashboard that tracks your habits, organizes your deep work blocks, and runs completely offline. The barrier to building private software is gone. Now is your chance to take back control of your data and build a system designed to last a decade.

***

## 🎯 What You'll Learn

✅ **The Agentic Paradigm:** Stop treating AI like a chatbot and learn how to direct it to build real software architecture.
✅ **Local-First Basics:** Why owning your data matters and how to run AI completely offline for free.
✅ **Zero-to-URL Live Build:** Watch step-by-step as we use natural language to scaffold a beautiful personal dashboard from scratch.
✅ **Prompting for Structure:** Learn how to force the AI to output exactly what you need without hallucinations. ✅
**Your Next Steps:** Discover how to turn this interface into a true AI assistant that can read and retrieve your private files.

***

## 🤝 Who Should Attend?

**Complete Beginners:** Step into the world of AI building without feeling overwhelmed by complex code.
**Founders & Creators:** Build a central hub to track your metrics and workflows without paying for another SaaS tool.
**Curious Minds:** See the future of local AI and agentic workflows in action.

***

## 📌 The Details

**Bring:** Just your laptop, your curiosity, and your messy notes.
**Bonus:** Get beginner system prompt cheat sheets and join the growing BagOfAI community.

event_title: Coffee and Interesting Conversation at The Balmoral
event_description:
We love events that are simple, easy, and fun to attend.

We are keeping it simple where we meet up and take turns using a die to randomly select a conversation starter question. Each person gets to answer and then you can discuss, explore, debate, or whatever you want to do, as long as it is done respectfully.

Subjects can range from interesting through to very enlightening. This event is for those people who are happy to discuss anything.

\*\*\* A few very important points to note \*\*\*

* If you RSVP, make sure you keep your RSVP up to date because, otherwise, people on the waitlist don't get notified and as such, they miss out. If you can't make it, please alter your RSVP as soon as you can
* Try to avoid altering your RSVP the night before or on the morning as the people on the waitlist may not see the notification that they are now attending and will miss out. Be respectful and keep your RSVP up-to-date
* Be aware that some of the subject matters given as conversation starters could be viewed as ‘full-on’ so make sure you come with an open mind.
* Remember that these are conversation starters and if you don't want to answer personally, just answer as a general response.

Come and enjoy some conversation that you wouldn't normally have and you may even learn a few things as well.

We'll see you there.

event_title: Melbourne -Sunday Free Online Guided Meditation: Learn, practice, relax and grow
event_description:
Meditate, meet and get motivated.
Meditation that you can use. Meditate for spiritual awakening, stress care, personal development, health...

How to join?
Please click on 'Attend Online' to get Zoom link

We recommend joining by laptop for better experience. All levels welcome.
You will find experience of this online class very real life like and in fact more attentive and personal

Facebook for more resources- more live events, recorded sessions, self learning units, mentorship, downloads..... Join now!

https://www.facebook.com/groups/WeMeditateGroup

What meditation technique is used?

The gentle and easy technique shared is known as 'Sahaja Yoga' meditation and is practiced in more than 100 countries and is always free. It was founded in 1970 by- Shri Mataji Nirmala Devi. When practiced for 10 minutes a day it can lead to dramatic positive change in your personal life - even after one week of practice!

More at:
https://sahajayogareview.wordpress.com/

Expected Outcome of class?

1. Learn how to meditate at home
2. Learn best practices and techniques
3. Experience the 'awakening' of healing energy within us that gives peace and balance
4. Sahaja Yoga meditation toolkit resources to apply techniques learnt in daily life

What happens after this class?

These recurring classes are meant to come together and meditate and learn little by little, so please plan to join them every week for growth in your meditative state and practice. You are equipped with knowledge to start practicing from day 1. As you join us for subsequent classes you are taken forward in meditation practice. In case you are unable to join a class, don't worry and rejoin any time and you can always start afresh.

Do share this event with you friends, colleagues, neighbors and loved ones. It may change their life and it may be the best gift they ever got :).

Need Help?

Please feel free to email or WhatsApp following in case you have more questions or face difficulties joining Zoom session. We will try to keep you informed about more meditation events.
Bhargava.potukuchi@gmail.com
+15132038961
Stay safe and keep meditating!

event_title: [Group Session] Exploring the Self: A Journey Within
event_description:
In the movement of everyday life, much of our attention is directed outward—towards responsibilities, roles, and the world around us. Yet within each of us exists a rich inner landscape shaped by our experiences, perceptions, emotions, and beliefs.

This workshop invites participants to pause and turn their attention inward. Through reflection supported by \*OH cards—\*participants will explore the different aspects of themselves that influence how they perceive life and relate to their experiences.

Often, insight arises not through analysis but through awareness. The imagery within the OH cards acts as a gentle mirror, allowing personal meanings, feelings, and perspectives to surface naturally. As participants reflect on what resonates with them, they may begin to notice patterns, inner voices, and stories that are part of their inner world.

This exploration is not about finding the “right” interpretation, but about **being present** with what emerges—observing one’s **own reflections** with openness and curiosity.
In a respectful and supportive space, participants will have the opportunity to explore their inner landscape, gain fresh perspectives, and deepen their awareness of themselves.

Sometimes, when we pause and look within, we begin to see ourselves—and our life experiences—with new clarity.
No prior experience is required, only a willingness to **explore with curiosity and openness.**

Kindly book the slot via whatsapp = +60197232112

event_title: CG Jung Society Library Day
event_description:
Library Day

The library offers the full spectrum of Jungian thought including current Jungian Journals as well as a wide range of Jungian topics by individual author. Books on mythology, symbols and alchemy as well as Reference books and Anthologies can aid in individual research and enjoyment.

In addition to the books, the library has a collection of audio tapes - mostly of lectures given locally. We also have a few videos. Members can borrow up to 4 books, 2 tapes and 1 video for one month. Renewal can be made by telephone (if the item is not required by another borrower). Please return the books on time.

Contact and visit
The Library, at Hawke House, 21 Ellen St, Fremantle, is open on the first Sunday of each month from 2pm - 5pm.
You do not have to be a Member to visit the library and have a look. Society and Library
Membership is required if you wish to borrow books.

Library Members unable to attend on the first Sunday of the month may visit the library by appointment.
Please contact Beth (Librarian) to arrange a mutually suitable time via email junglibraryperth@gmail.com

event_title: What’s Your Favorite Food?
event_description:
What’s that one dish you can never say no to? Or something you will never say yes to?😄

Maybe it’s something simple. Maybe it’s something from home. Or something you crave at the worst hours.

Or… that one food you tried once and said, “yeah, never again.”

Share:

* What did you eat?
* Where were you?
* Why do you love it (or hate it)?

Or just come and listen — you might discover foods you’ve never heard of… or tried 😄

No pressure. No expectations. No judgment.
Just easy conversations, good vibes, and a lot of laughter.

You’re welcome to share, or simply listen and laugh along.
You’ll be accompanied by a therapist and coach who are open-minded, supportive, and loves to have a great time so **come on in**!

Let’s spread the joy. After all, laughter is a language we all speak. 😃

Follow us on Instagram @therapistonvacation and @cindy.lee.stories

event_title: Paint, Fun ,Feel & Discover Yourself
event_description:
**Paint, Fun, Feel & Discover Yourself ( paid event )**
A Fun & Interactive Art & Psychology Experience

If letting go feels harder than it should, there’s usually a pattern behind it.

In this session, we go one step deeper.
Through simple, guided painting exercises, you’ll explore:

* what’s really happening inside you
* the patterns behind your reactions and relationships
* why some things are hard to release

No artistic skills needed. Just curiosity.

This is not a lecture. It’s an experience.
You’ll paint, reflect, and gain clear insights about yourself guided by psychologists .

🎁 At the end, two participants will be selected to receive a personal pattern reading with guided insights.

🎨All materials will be provided.
☕️Light snacks, coffee, and tea will be served.

Come as you are.
You may leave understanding something you couldn’t see before.

📣📣📣

Please note that this event is non-refundable and cannot be canceled or rescheduled.

We kindly ask everyone to respect the space, each other, and the workshop guidelines. Creating a safe and meaningful environment depends on mutual respect and consideration.

If these boundaries are not respected, you may be asked to leave the session.

event_title: Social Volleyball Game @ RKS Sports Centre (Ara Damansara) - RM15
event_description:
## 🏐 Social Volleyball @ Subang Jaya

### 🚨 【IMPORTANT】Fee & Payment

* **Amount:** 15 RM per person
* **Payment Method:** TNG eWallet / DuitNow QR
* **How to Pay:** To receive the QR code, please **join our WhatsApp** [What's App Group](https://chat.whatsapp.com/FIZusqhpIrc9I36yZZh7vd?mode=gi_t) or **message the organizer directly** [0169555794](https://wa.me/60169555794)
* **24-Hour Rule:** Please complete your payment within **24 hours of your RSVP**.
* *Note: If payment/screenshot is not received within 24 hours, your spot will be removed to allow others from the Waitlist to join.*
* **Confirmation:** After making the payment, please **send a screenshot of the receipt** to the organizer. Your spot is **ONLY officially confirmed** once the payment is verified.
* **System Note:** Meetup may show this event as "Free," but the 15 RM fee is required to cover court rental and organizing costs.

***

### 👋 Welcome Message

Hi everyone! Let's play volleyball! 🏐 I’ve organized this session for anyone who wants to enjoy volleyball, stay active, and meet new friends in Subang Jaya. Whether you are an experienced player or a beginner, come join us for a fun 2-hour session!

***

### 1\. Arrival & Warm\-up

* **Please arrive 10 minutes early.** We want to start the games right on time.
* Please **complete your stretching and warm-up individually** before the session begins so we can maximize our court time.

### 2\. Social Vibe \(Not Competitive\)

* This is a **Social Event**, not a professional tournament.
* All levels are welcome! Please be encouraging and respectful. Complaining or blaming others for mistakes is strictly prohibited. Let's keep it fun and positive!

### 3\. Cancellation Policy

* **More than 24 hours' notice:** Full refund / No charge.
* **Within 24 hours of the event:** **Non-refundable.** \* *Reason: If you cancel within 24 hours or do not show up, the fee still applies as the court cost is already fixed. Thank you for your understanding.*

### 4\. Waiver of Liability \(Safety First\)

* **Disclaimer:** By joining this event, you agree that you are participating at your own risk. The organizer is not responsible for any injuries, accidents, or loss of personal belongings that may occur during the session.

### 5\. Equipment \(Volleyballs\)

* **Bring your own ball (If possible):** While we provide one game ball, we highly encourage anyone who owns a volleyball to **bring it along!** \* Having extra balls will allow us to practice more effectively during warm-ups and ensures everyone gets more touches. Please make sure to put your name on your ball!

**🏐 Game Format & Rotation System**

To ensure a high-quality experience and keep the games energetic for the full 2 hours, we limit the session to **15 players**.
**1\. Why 15 Players? \(System & Backup\)**

* **Emergency Buffer:** We maintain this number to ensure we have sufficient players (at least 12) even if someone has a last-minute emergency. This guarantees the session will never be understaffed.
* **Optimal Energy:** With 15 people, everyone gets short breaks to hydrate and stay energized, preventing fatigue and injury.

**2\. How the Rotation Works \(7 vs 8\)** We will divide into two teams and use a **"Serve-Rotation"** system so everyone gets equal playtime:

* **6vs6 on Court:** Only 6 players from each team are on the court at a time.
* **The Flow:** Every time your team wins a point and gains the serve, the player in the serving position rotates **OUT**, and the next person waiting on the sideline rotates **IN**.
* **Referee & Score:** The person currently resting on the sideline will act as the **Referee (Whistle)** and manage the **Scoreboard**.

> **Result: No one sits out for more than a few minutes. You play, you rest/referee shortly, and you jump right back in!**

event_title: 🌍 English Practice: Meet & Learn (Daily & Free)
event_description:
## 🚀 **Stop searching for classes. Start speaking today!**

Welcome to our free worldwide community. Hosted by English teachers, our daily sessions are fun, supportive, and focused on *real* practice. Forget lectures—meet new friends and get guided feedback every day.

***

### 🔑 What to Expect:

✅ **Live 1-on-1 English Conversation** Forget boring lectures. Jump into real **conversation exchange** with different partners at your level. *The structure:* (3-5 min vocab warmup + 12 min **speaking practice**) x 4 rounds.
✅ **AI Matching + Feedback** Our smart system pairs you effectively for the best **english chat** experience and gives you instant tips on pronunciation, vocabulary, and grammar.
✅ **Come & Go Anytime** Stay for one round or all four—it's the most flexible way to **learn to talk english**.

***

### 💬 Why Learners Love This **Language Exchange**:

> “It’s like having English-speaking friends every night.”

> "In my perspective, I have no place to **practice english speaking** and share my ideas with other non native speakers. This platform made it happen."

> "This environment really helps people feel safe and comfortable, making it easier to engage in open **english conversation practice**."

***

### 📅 Details & How to Join:

We use our custom platform designed for speaking.
1️⃣ **RSVP now** on Meetup to show interest. 2️⃣ Go to 👉 **[speakduo.com](https://speakduo.com/)** to get today’s unique session link from the schedule.
Note: If you don't see the link here, just head straight to [speakduo.com](http://speakduo.com/). Yes, it's 100% free!

event_title: Social Pool 4PM session
event_description:
Please come and play **8-ball pool** on Sunday. The group is casual and has varying skill levels but games are about learning and having fun.
All are welcome.

Hot & cold drinks including alcohol are also available.
Teams rotate and split so you play with and against different people in teams of two.

Free parking - Park at CPP Cultural center on Roe Street for three hours free parking, and then charged at $4 per hour after that.
**Free Parking - the closest to Pot Black is #2**
https://www.cityofperthparking.com.au/whats-new/3-hour-free-parking-weekends-public-holidays

Transport - The Perth train and bus ports are within 5 minute walks.

Cost - $7 cash. Thanks to the venue we have subsidised tables for up to two hours of pool. Stay as long or short as you like. Please bring cash and change is provided.

We meet at the table and chairs at the entrance inside the building at 3.50 - 4pm. Just ask the staff for the Meetup group.

Rules: Since there are so many variations of the rules around the world, here is what we play:

8 Ball rules:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=c-4URzvawhs&t=236s
(International rules, with the exception of calling shots. Flukes welcome)
https://docs.google.com/document/d/1s-LCRkCKjvz-jer0K3NQD4FiyvLjvhYJb-5zUTC8fSk/edit?usp=drive_link

***By registering for this event, please take note of the following concerning attendance;***

* Please make sure to turn up when your name is reflected on the "Going" list.
* If you require to pull out for any reason, please ensure to do so 24hrs before the session (4pm, Saturday) to give enough notice to the people on the waitlist.
* Failing to do so and being absent on Sunday will be met with a warning and a strike. Receiving 3 strikes will result in a ban from attending 3 future sessions counted from the week following the third strike.
* If you are registering onto the waitlist, please check back after 4pm on Saturday the day before to see if you have been bumped up onto the "Going" list.
* On the day of the session, organisers will be grouping participants for play at 4pm sharp. If you are on the "Going" list, but late, you will not be given priority to jump onto the active tables. In the event we do not have enough tables subject to venue conditions, you will be asked to wait.

event_title: ✨ Daily English Conversation | Safe Practice & Community
event_description:
🚀 **Stop searching for classes. Start speaking today!**
Welcome to our free worldwide community. Hosted by English teachers, our daily sessions are fun, supportive, and focused on *real* practice. Forget lectures—meet new friends and get guided feedback every day.

***

### 🔑 What to Expect:

✅ **Live 1-on-1 English Conversation** Forget boring lectures. Jump into real **conversation exchange** with different partners at your level. *The structure:* (3-5 min vocab warmup + 12 min **speaking practice**) x 4 rounds.
✅ **AI Matching + Feedback** Our smart system pairs you effectively for the best **english chat** experience and gives you instant tips on pronunciation, vocabulary, and grammar.
✅ **Come & Go Anytime** Stay for one round or all four—it's the most flexible way to **learn to talk english**.

***

### 💬 Why Learners Love This **Language Exchange**:

> “It’s like having English-speaking friends every night.”

> "In my perspective, I have no place to **practice english speaking** and share my ideas with other non native speakers. This platform made it happen."

> "This environment really helps people feel safe and comfortable, making it easier to engage in open **english conversation practice**."

***

### 📅 Details & How to Join:

We use our custom platform designed for speaking.
1️⃣ **RSVP now** on Meetup to show interest. 2️⃣ Go to 👉 **[speakduo.com](https://speakduo.com/)** to get today’s unique session link from the schedule.
Note: If you don't see the link here, just head straight to [speakduo.com](http://speakduo.com/). Yes, it's 100% free!

event_title: Listeners' Circle - Edition 111 - Tell Your Story
event_description:
Hello Listener! Please get your pass from the below link!

[https://in.bookmyshow.com/events/listeners-circle-tell-your-story/ET00330970](https://in.bookmyshow.com/events/listeners-circle-tell-your-story/ET00330970)

Listeners’ circle is now back after a pause, more committed to the cause than ever. Listeners’ Circle in association with Dialogues now happening again at the venue!! Speak your heart out without fear of judgment and listen without judging!
​
Every story matters. Every life matters. Every feeling matters. But do we share our stories, struggles, and experiences with others openly? What stops us? Isn't it the fear of judgment? And how often we listen to others
patiently without judging them?
​
Think about how it would feel to take an hour out of your fast-paced life and tell your story to people who listen to you without judging? Also, listen to the stories of others without judging.
​
Listeners' circle, Bangalore is an initiative to make that happen. Started in May 2018 at as a not for Profit Initiative, we have hosted 100+ editions until now, impacting more than 500+ Speakers and 3000+ Listeners. ​
​
The previous editions had some exciting stories to listen to. ​
​
An Entrepreneur from IIT Madras shared his life-changing near-death experience and how it transformed his
complete life.​
​
A Management Profession shared how being just ‘Ordinary’ all along his life has helped him and his circles. He articulated that being ‘Ordinary’ is the new difference.​​

A founder of a theatre group shared how she bravely stood up to the sexual harassment of a fellow passenger in a bus moving through the middle of the forest in midnight. ​
​
A thirty-something professional shared how she is in a journey of re-discovering love after five years of separation and being single. ​
​
Someone shared how an experience in a cab on his first journey in Japan, made him understand Japan and honesty.​
​
Everyone has such stories to share. Do you have something to share? ​
​
Be a part of the Listener's circle - Edition 92
​
Speak your mind without being judged. Speak your mind without any boundaries. ​
​
Would you like to just listen? We have a place for you too.​
​

Get ready to be mesmerized by powerful verses, thought-provoking pieces, and captivating performances in our open mic session. Come join our community of poetry enthusiasts and immerse yourself in an evening of creativity, inspiration, and mutual appreciation. Let your soul speak through the whispers of poetry.

event_title: Web3 group meeting
event_description:
Join fellow Web3 enthusiasts, developers, entrepreneurs, investors, and traders for an evening of insightful discussions and networking at the vibrant Dome Cafe in KLCC! Whether you're deep into blockchain development, exploring NFTs and DeFi, or just curious about the decentralized web, this meetup is your chance to connect with like-minded individuals in Kuala Lumpur's growing Web3 community.

**What to Expect:**

* **Open Discussions:** Topics include the latest trends in Web3 (e.g., layer-2 scaling, Web3 gaming, and real-world applications in Malaysia).
* **Guest Spotlights:** If you're working on something exciting, sign up to give a quick 5-minute pitch (optional—let us know in the comments!).
* **Project ideas:** present your ideas, and find like-minded people to develop together.
* **Casual Networking:** Mingle over coffee and share your latest projects or ideas.
* **Beginner-Friendly:** No prior experience required; come learn and ask questions.

This is a great opportunity to build connections in a small, comfortable setting. Bring your laptop if you want to demo something, and let's shape the future of the internet together!
**Why Attend?** Web3 is booming in Malaysia—events like Malaysia Blockchain Week and local crypto gatherings show the momentum. Don't miss out on expanding your network in the heart of KL.

event_title: VIRTUAL BOOK CLUB 2026
event_description:
Virtual Book Club is spurred by our introverted love of good books, and the awareness that if we don't schedule it, it won't be a priority.

**WHO IT'S FOR:**

Anyone who:

* Loves reading
* Wants to get into a consistent habit of reading
* Can't get to our in-person book club in Melbourne

**HOW IT WORKS:**

* Book club runs every Sunday from 5-6pm.
* Starts: Sunday 11 January.
* Drop in when you can. Pay only for the session you attend.
* No prescribed book - bring what you feel like reading
* Read in silence with audio off (40 minutes)
* Share what you read and what you loved / learned (15 minutes)

**WHAT IT COSTS:**

There is an organizer fee of $5 for the session.
You only pay if you show up, so if you can't make it on the day for any reason, you don't have to pay.
You can PayID me at 0403 413 056 (Serena Low).

**Call:** 9606735383

https://www.instagram.com/thefunboardgames/

**\*** **Say no to boring weekends** and **get started with this joyful hobby.** Don’t think twice — just come over to our events where **fun is guaranteed.** We offer a wide range of carefully chosen board games **(50+ board and card games)** to **rekindle the child in you** and **bring back those joyful days you experienced as a kid.** It’s a **perfect way** to **relax from** your **hectic weekday schedules.**

**\*** Join us at The Fun Boardgames event for an unforgettable board gaming experience every weekend! It’s **a place to have fun, socialize, make new friends**, and discover the **joy of board gaming**.

**\*** Our events are **Absolutely Beginner-Friendly** and **Introvert-Friendly** — perfect for those who are **new to board games** and looking for a welcoming environment to **start their board gaming journey**!

**\*** Our events feature a variety of board games, with a focus on creating a fun and inclusive atmosphere. Around **80–90% of the games are light to medium-level, fun-themed board games**. Here are some of the board games we feature at our event: **Dune imperium uprising, Wingspan, Catan, Shasn, Sagrada, Project-L, Cascadia, Carcassonne, Camel Up, Dixit, Azul, Splendor, King of Tokyo, Sequence, Ticket to Ride Multi-Map Collections, Codenames, social deception games (Coup, Saboteur),** and **many many more**.

**\* You can come alone**; in fact, many members do so. We group them together and explain the rules. You’ll quickly find yourself surrounded by friendly faces who share a love for board games. **If you come with your friends or as a couple**, we will help you find the right group to join and play board games.

**\*** Disconnect from digital screens and immerse yourself in **face-to-face interactions, laughter**, and **friendly competition**.

**\* Don’t worry about not knowing how to play board games** — our Gamemasters will be there to help you learn the basics and you can enjoy playing unlimited games until the end of the event.

\* **Note for those who are registering for the event**, you can come and go anytime between the start and end of the event:
**-** **You can Come Late** (Example — The event starts at 5:30pm: You can come by 6 pm or 7 pm or 7:45 pm or anytime at your convenience).
**-** **You can Leave Early** (Example — The event ends at 10 pm: You can leave by 7 pm or 8 pm or 9:15 pm or anytime at your convenience).

**\*** Come and discover the joy of board gaming with us! Join our vibrant community of board game enthusiasts in Bangalore and **make memories that will last a lifetime**. See you at our next event!

**Note:** Photos and videos will be taken during the event and shared on our Instagram board game community page. We aim to capture the fun and celebrate our shared experiences — please attend only if you're comfortable being photographed or recorded. Thank you!

Please join us on Sundays for teachings and a guided meditation at the Diamond Way Buddhist Centre, Perth.

* Diamond Way Buddhist Centre Perth

event_title: 9 Questions You Should Ask Before Joining Any Company (But Nobody Tells You)
event_description:
What if I told you there are exactly 9 things that decide whether a workplace will make you thrive — or burn you out?
Not the "we're like a family" pitch. Not the Glassdoor rating.

**Nine specific, science-backed psychological dimensions that tell you the truth about a company's culture before you sign the offer letter.**

At this meetup, **we're giving them away — for free.**

These are the same **9 dimensions VMS CultureLabs uses to diagnose culture health for companies**.

**Built on research from Gallup, Edmondson (Harvard), and Maslach's burnout research.** The kind of frameworks companies pay consultants lakhs to access.

You'll leave with a **checklist you can use immediately**

— whether you're evaluating your next job, or wondering if you should stay at your current one.

**What you'll learn:**
The 9 dimensions like
**Psychological Safety, Burnout Risk, Expectations vs Reality and 6 more.**

What each one means. **How to spot it**. **The questions that reveal the truth.**

**This is for you if:**

* You're job hunting and tired of "great culture" companies that turn toxic in 2 months
* You've experienced burnout and never want to repeat it
* You're in HR or hiring and want to understand what talent is really evaluating
* You're a founder losing people and can't figure out why

**Why this is backed by real science:**
77% of employees globally are disengaged (Gallup).

Psychological safety is the #1 predictor of high-performing teams — not talent, not strategy (Edmondson, Harvard).

Burnout is now classified by the WHO as an occupational phenomenon.

Companies with top-quartile culture see 4× higher revenue growth (McKinsey).

**Your mental health at work isn't a luxury. It's non-negotiable. Come learn how to protect it.**

**About the host:**
**Shobhit Khandelwal is the founder of VMS CultureLabs,** a psychometrics-based culture intelligence service that uses behavioral science to tell companies exactly how much their culture problems are costing them — in ₹, not feelings.

event_title: Popularity & Fame – A Philosophical View
event_description:
Popularity and Fame may not be concepts which are immediately associated with Philosophy, but when we look closer into it, we will find a lot to philosophise. Issues of what do we value in us and others, why are we influenced by social status, what qualities create popularity and what makes us follow some people more than others? All those issues and more come to mind.

**Popularity**: the state or condition of being liked, admired, or supported by many people.
**Fame**: the state of being known or talked about by many people, especially on account of notable achievements. Fame is a widespread, extended form of popularity.

**Benefits of Popularity and Fame**
Whilst popularity and fame can come with problems, they also offer real benefits—socially, psychologically and financially. Popular people have it easier to find access to positions, resources and even sexual and relationship partners. They simply have greater choice.

Popularity open doors that are normally closed to most people. Well‑known and popular individuals often receive career opportunities, invitations to exclusive events and access to influential networks. It can give higher income, endorsements, sponsorships and long‑term financial security.

Popular people receive more respect and attention. Once they have received this higher social status, they do not always have to prove ongoing qualifications for it. A status can carry them over a longer period of time.

Popular or famous people seem to have a greater power to wield influence over others. We often experience that entertainer or actors feel qualified to give political and social advice, simply because they have a large audience, not necessarily because they are truly qualified.

In short, popularity and fame offer opportunity, wealth, influence, recognition, access and opportunity for inspiration. Psychology notes, that they can elevate a person’s life in extraordinary ways—"as long as the individual stays grounded and maintains a strong sense of self.”
The big question: What does it mean to stay grounded?

**Disadvantages**
Popularity and fame can come with real psychological, social, and even physical costs. The core problems fall into a few major categories, each backed by research and real-world examples.

The loss of privacy can curtail somebody’s life quite dramatically. Living under constant surveillance comes with a price. Intense scrutiny and public judgment are often a by-product of popularity and fame.
Popular people often experience distorted or fragile relationships.

Friendships and relationships become selfish and fake with a hidden agenda to gain social or monetary benefits by living close to a prominent and popular person. True friendships become rare or hard to judge.
Psychology knows of the addiction to attention. Once experienced a high level of it, it becomes difficult to live with a lesser, normal level of recognition and social appreciation.

There is also a certain pressure to maintain an image, however, to some degree that applies to all of us, although mostly to a lesser degree.

We know that many famous people struggle with their mental health. It appears paradoxically that many well-known successful and very rich entertainers suffer addiction, depression and often early mortality. Particularly musicians show significantly higher early death rates than the general population, often linked to substance abuse and lifestyle instability.

In general, it is problematic to depend on other people’s approval on a very intense and frequent level. Popular folks end up performing instead of living. So-called identity crises are therefore very likely to occur. This creates a fragile identity that can collapse the moment the attention fades.
Lastly, popular people meet a great deal of envy, jealousy and rivalry.

**Reasons why we admire popular and famous people**
We admire popular or famous people because our nature, our social instincts and our culture are wired to. The pull towards fame and popularity is deeply human. But we may want to ask why.

Evolutionary Psychology suggests that humans have always admired leaders, heroes, and high‑status individuals. Admiring and following powerful or skilled people helped early humans learn how to survive and thrive. Modern celebrities simply replace ancient leaders or heroes.
In addition, popular people act as models for our own values, lifestyles and aspirations. In many ways they are tent-setters because their followers want to be like them.

**Our questions** could be, what makes people popular and why do we as humans have the desire to be popular? We should also reflect on the aspects of admiration, which is the very basis of popularity or fame. What do we admire, why do we admire others and why do we act in favour of people who are popular? Are we wired to be led? And most importantly, should we be careful to adopt famous people as models and influencer for all sort of aspects of life, including political, social and economical values?

event_title: Bharat Food Addicts in Recovery Anonymous (Hindi)
event_description:
**Having trouble controlling the way you eat?**

**Are you overweight? Underweight?**

**Obsessed with food, weight, or dieting?**

**You are not alone. There is a solution.**

**Food Addicts in Recovery Anonymous (FA)** is a program based on the **Twelve Steps of Alcoholics Anonymous (AA).**

There are **no dues, fees, or weigh-ins** at FA meetings.

FA is a **fellowship of individuals** who, through **shared experience and mutual support**, are recovering from the disease of **food addiction**.

FA members are **people of all ages**, from every part of the world.

FA includes people who were **morbidly obese**, **substantially underweight**, or even at a **normal weight**.

Regardless of their size, they were **tormented by cravings, dieting, bulimia, and/or an obsession with exercise**

event_title: Parlons Français ONLINE ! French Conversations !
event_description:
Salut la compagnie !

Want to practice your French and pick up some new expressions for casual conversation? Come online this week and join us at Parlons Français ! – French Conversations !

Please note: This is a conversation group, NOT A BEGINNER LESSON. You will need to be able to have a basic conversation to participate effectively.

Parlons Français ! – French Conversations ! is free meetup event. At The Language Lovers, we practice a peer-teaching/peer-sharing approach, through which individuals learn languages from others who are willing to share their knowledge of those languages. At the same time, we make great friends from all over the world.

Since this is a conversation group, participants should have at least basic French speaking and listening skills such as the ability to introduce themselves and talk about their day.

All our language-learning meetup events are free of charge.

WE WANT FRENCH SPEAKERS TOO

event_title: Join the Calm Collective - Holding the Light Together
event_description:
Dear friends,

Across the world, many are holding a quiet yet powerful intention for peace, healing, and clarity in our shared human family. In that spirit, we warmly invite you to join the **Calm Collective** — a simple gathering where meditators, faith leaders, and well-wishers from all backgrounds pause together in silent intention for ten minutes.

*These short moments of shared stillness offer a chance to reconnect with presence and hold a pure intention of much-needed calm, compassion, and hope for our world.*

Everyone is welcome. There is a power in a gathering of shared intent.

Our Calm Collective meditations will be **every Sunday** for the next three months — March, April and May, from **9:00–9:10 pm AEST**.

You are warmly invited to join in — wherever you are — to contribute, connect, and also to take support from these collective pauses each week.

Whether you join once or return regularly, your prayers, meditations, and presence contribute to the quiet field of intention we are creating together.

Please register to attend on any Sunday (you only need to register once).

**Dates:** Every Sunday in March, April and May
**Time:** 9:00–9:10 pm AEST
**Zoom Link:** [tiny.cc/calmc](http://tiny.cc/calmc)
**Meeting ID:** 968 1016 7515
**Passcode:** calm

Each month we hold a different shared intention.

**Intention for March:**
*"As steady lights of peace, may calm and compassion guide our minds and hearts."*

With gratitude and in peace,

The Calm Collective Team

event_title: Speak Thai for Tourists in 2 Hours Fast Track Series (Online)
event_description:
# **Speak Thai for Tourists in 2 Hours**

## **Fast Track Series (Online)**

**Before you explore Thailand…**
**prepare yourself properly.**
**Designed for travelers staying 1–4 weeks,**
**this exclusive 2-hour intensive session gives you exactly what you need — and nothing you don’t.**
**Because when you travel, you shouldn’t:**
**• Feel shy speaking to locals**
**• Get overcharged**
**• Take the wrong taxi**
**• Order the wrong food**
**• Feel unsure how to say “No”**
**You deserve to travel confidently.**
**You deserve to stay in control.**
**You deserve to enjoy Thailand without stress.**

# **What You’ll Master in Just 2 Hours**

**A carefully structured, high-impact session covering:**

### **✔ Essential Survival Thai**

**Speak naturally from day one using 40–50 high-frequency phrases.**

### **✔ Real Travel Conversations**

**Order food clearly**
**Communicate with taxi drivers smoothly**
**Ask prices & bargain politely**
**Respond confidently in everyday situations**

### **✔ Smart Traveler Awareness**

**Avoid common tourist scams**
**Understand emergency numbers**
**Handle uncomfortable situations**
**Avoid cultural mistakes**
**No grammar overload.**
**No textbook memorizing.**
**Only practical Thai you can use immediately.**
**This is not just language learning.**
**This is travel preparation at a professional level.**

# **Exclusive Bonuses Included**

**1️⃣ Practical Thai PDF Guide (ready-to-use phrases)**

**3️⃣ Confidence-focused speaking correction**
**4️⃣ Free Offline Practice Class (2 Sundays/month)**

**6️⃣ 15-Minute Insider Travel Briefing with a Professional Tour Guide**
**Discover:**
**• Must-visit highlights**
**• Hidden local gems**
**• What to skip**
**• Smart planning strategies**
**Travel like an informed insider — not just a tourist.**

# **Meet Your Instructors**

## **Ms. Aiyada Tongngoen**

**Lead Thai Language Instructor**
**Founder of Fast Track Series**
**• Specialist in Practical Thai for Travelers**
**• Confidence-based speaking methodology**
**• Small-group interactive coaching**

**Helping travelers communicate clearly, confidently, and respectfully in real Thai environments.**

## **Kru Kaew**

**Professional Tour Guide**

**• Deep cultural & destination expertise**
**• Real-world tourism knowledge**
**• Safety & travel strategy specialist**

# **💎 Why This Class Is Worth It**

**In just 2 hours, you gain:**
**✔ Language confidence**
**✔ Cultural awareness**
**✔ Scam prevention knowledge**
**✔ Practical survival skills**
**✔ Local insider insight**
**✔ Real speaking experience**
**For less than the cost of one tourist dinner.**

event_title: MMM - Magic Moves Monday
event_description:
See the event in the Facebook group: https://facebook.com/events/s/mmm-**magic**-moves-monday/954174810127475/

MMM will have......

Dario and Fab Skate X track
Slalom
Beginner help
Kids fun
Smiles

All for free with smiles, laughter, music and more. We will have plenty of skates and safety gear for you to try if you dont have your own.

We are happy for complete BEGINNERS to come along, providing tips on skating, stopping and turning, this will get you out rolling and usually we can get you to this stage in one session. POR is a social group, not a skating school, we do not provide formal lesson, just skaters helping skaters. If you have your own helmet, please bring!

We normally set up cones for slalom practice and skaters practice whatever they are keen to learn - normally lots of advice on offer too.

This is a social event, so everyone welcome, we don’t leave this area, so no issues if you are not confident yet, we will be close by. We tend to get 200+ skaters most weeks, normally 5-10 new skaters too, so don’t be shy, come and join us.

IMPORTANT - this is a casual social event and you participate at your own risk.

Protective gear is strongly recommended, if you dont have protective gear and want to come along, maybe ask in the group, we do have spares.

Photos and videos maybe taken at the event, and shared in the closed group.

Please let us know beforehand if you want to be excluded, your privacy will be respected.

event_description:

### **Mondays: Yin Yoga \| 6\.30 \- 7\.30pm**

A meditative practice focused on deep stillness. We use long-held floor poses to target the connective tissues and quiet the mind.

* **Switch** from "fight-or-flight" to deep rest.
* **Release** deep-seated tension in connective tissues.
* **Calm** the nervous system through mindful stillness.

### **Thursdays: Gentle Flow Yoga \| 6\.30 \- 7\.30pm**

A rhythmic, fluid practice that connects breath with motion. This class uses somatic movement to release daily stress and build inner strength.

* **Soothe** the mind with rhythmic, fluid movement.

event_title: Monday Night Write Club
event_description:
**Ever told yourself you’d start writing comedy but never quite found the time?**

**Monday Night Write Club** is a casual weekly gathering at the Bassendean Hotel where comedians and aspiring comedians sit down and actually do the work. Some people arrive with notebooks full of half-finished ideas. Others come to workshop a new bit, punch up a joke, or simply carve out an hour in the week to focus on writing.
You can join a small group and peer-review jokes, bounce ideas around, or head off to a quiet corner and write on your own. The goal is simple: be around people who are doing the same thing. The energy of a room full of people thinking about comedy tends to get the brain moving.
It’s also a comfortable entry point for people who have been curious about stand-up but haven’t taken the leap yet. You can listen, contribute, ask questions, and get a feel for how comedians build material before stepping on stage.
Write Club attendees also get the chance to run one of their jokes live. Each week there is an allocated segment in the 7:30pm comedy show at the Bassendean Hotel where Write Club members can test a line or two in front of a real audience. It’s a low-pressure way to see what works.
Stick around afterwards and you’ll also see working comics trying new material, along with the occasional touring act or headliner dropping in to test fresh ideas.
Whether you’re writing your first joke, sharpening your act, or just looking to be around people who enjoy the craft of comedy, Monday Night Write Club is a good place to start.

event_title: Ride from one country to another on two wheels and a saddle
event_description:
Day 1: Saigon - My Tho - Cai Be - Can Tho (Approx. 50 Km.)

Pick up at Tan Son Nhat airport and transfer to My Tho, the gateway to the Mekong Delta. In My Tho, we unload our bikes and start cycling on quiet country roads along Tien River to Cai Be. Along the way, we pass through the heart of rural Mekong along narrow lanes, passing banana plantations, sugar cane fields and the lush rural landscape of the Delta. After Cai Be, we cross Dinh Khao ferry to Cho Lach. We can cycle for a couple of kilometers if we wish and then shuttle the rest way to Can Tho. Overnight in Can Tho. Meals: Lunch, Dinner.

Day 2: Can Tho - Chau Doc (Approx . 70 Km.)

An early morning short walk to Ninh Kieu pier to board our private motorized sampan for a leisurely cruise to explore the hustle bustle of Cai Rang floating market packed with fruits and colourful goods some like cigarettes from Cambodia that are smuggled. We return to the hotel for breakfast before driving out of town to catch the Can Tho Ferry. From the pier we transfer by bus for 2 kms before starting our cycling day. We head off to a quieter road to enjoy biking along the exotic Hau River. Enjoy rural Vietnamese scenery including vast stretches of rice paddies and picturesque lotus ponds. Enjoy a tasty lunch in a simple local restaurant and then continue through the countryside until we reach Cho Moi town and along the Tien River we approach the small town of Phu Tan. We cross the river by ferry before loading our bikes on to the support van for a 40km transfer to Chau Doc via Phu My. Dinner and overnight in Chau Doc. Meals: Lunch, Dinner.

Day 3: Chau Doc - Ha Tien (Approx. 80 Km.)

After breakfast, enjoy a full day on saddle. Today will be a mixture of on-road and off-road. First cycle to Sam Mountain with dozens of pagodas and temples. Visit Lady Chua Xu: founded in the 1820s, this pagoda stands facing Sam Mountain. From top of Sam Mountain we catch the panoramic view over the Cambodian border. Ride down hill before we hit the flat terrain to Ha Tien enjoying evergreen countryside dotted with local homes and orchards. We break journey at Ba Chuc Temple, considered as Vietnamese killing fields during Pol Pot’s regime. We then continue to Ha Tien, a beautiful beach and market town situated on the Vietnam Cambodian coastal border and offering a diverse spread of Vietnamese life with many Khmer people living here. It has fantastic beaches and superb coastline. Overnight Saigon Ha Tien hotel. Meals: Lunch, Dinner.

Day 4: Ha Tien - Kep (Approx. 40 Km.)

We leave Ha Tien and head out to Cambodian border at Xa Xia (10km from town). A short pleasant ride on the quiet scenic roads to the border crossing. This rural crossing used mainly by local traders makes for a pleasant stop while we complete all the immigration formalities. After 15 km ride, leave your bike in Vietnam and walk across the border to Cambodia where your new bike awaits. We then enjoy a gentle ride through stunning rural areas introducing Cambodia to you with the night’s destination being the tranquil seaside town of Kep. Meals: Breakfast, Lunch.

Day 5: Kep - Sihanoukville (Approx. 80 Km.)

Following breakfast, enjoy a full day on saddle. Today's ride takes us along easy riding tarmac roads that pass through many rural sights with occasional glimpses of the Gulf of Thailand. Cycle through Kampot and its Damrei Mountains and stop off to admire this provincial capital with its well preserved French colonial building. Continue to Cambodia's premier beach resort, Sihanoukville, where we spend our night. Meals: Breakfast, Lunch.

Day 6: Sihanoukville - Phnom Penh. (Approx. 25 Km.)

Morning at leisure on the beach before transferring from Sihanoukville by road to Cambodia's capital, Phnom Penh. In the afternoon, ride or transfer by minibus to the Tuol Sleng Museum and the infamous Killing Fields. Meals: Breakfast, Lunch.

Day 7: Phnom Penh - Kampong Chnang (Approx. 90 Km.)

Following breakfast, we commence cycling from hotel and head towards Oudong, the former capital of Cambodia, passing magnificent scenery and friendly locals. Climb to the top of Phnom Oudong for sweeping views of the countryside before pedaling on tarmac roads towards Kampong Chhnang. This quiet, riverside town is known for producing some of the country's best pottery. Meals: Breakfast, Lunch, Dinner.

Day 8: Kampong Chnang - Siem Reap (Approx. 34 Km.)

An early start today with 6 hour boat ride on Tonle Sap Lake. Arrival in Siem Reap around lunch time. In the afternoon, Embark on a 10km ride from Siem Reap to Angkor Wat, stopping to explore the temple complex and to enjoy a picnic lunch. In the afternoon, cycle an additional 17 to 24km on the Small Circuit between Angkor Wat and Phnom Bakeng, with stops at the Death Gate, Victory Gate, Terraces of Elephants and Leper King, and Srah Srang. Watch the sunset from Phnom Bakeng. Meals: Breakfast, Lunch.

Day 9: Siem Reap (Approx. 52 Km.)

After breakfast, take a shuttle bus to the rose sandstone temple of Banteay Srei, then explore the temple’s magnificent carvings and embark on the 31km Grand Circuit to the ruined, jungle temple of Preah Khan. Bike to Neak Pean Temple, famous for its sacred pools, breaking for lunch before riding another 21km to Banteay Kdei and Siem Reap via picturesque country lanes. Overnight in Siem Reap. Meals: Breakfast, Lunch.

Day 10: Departure Siem Reap.

Free time for leisure before transfer to the airport. Meals: Breakfast

event_title: Rivervale Meditation Class
event_description:
Don’t miss out on these free, casual meditation classes! The meditation classes cater for people of all experience levels and are run in a relaxed environment. No stretches or physical exercises are involved in learning this simple yet effective technique.

**Effortless Meditation**
The word ‘Sahaja’ can be translated as ‘Spontaneous’ or ‘Simple’, and is at the outset a hypothesis that meditation can come easily for those who are willing to practice regularly (even briefly each day).
Sahaja Yoga defines meditation as ‘thoughtless awareness’, a state where you are completely conscious but at the same time completely without thought. In this state, we gain a unique perspective and insight into the beauty of our own innate nature.

Physical and mental health benefits have also been attributed to regular meditation practice through comprehensive Australian research (Learn more in the book ‘silence your mind’ or at www.beyondthemind.com).

Sahaja Yoga meditation was founded by HH Shri Mataji Nirmala Devi in 1970, and is based on kundalini awakening. Shri Mataji felt that meditation is an essential part of our spiritual growth. She felt that because meditation is so essential, a free and simple method to achieve it must be made available for everyone. Sahaja Yoga is now practiced in over 52 countries worldwide.

event_title: Mindful Well-Being Meditation
event_description:
**Find your sanctuary within: A Mindful Well-Being Journey**

**Feeling like the hamster on a work wheel?**
You’re not alone. Juggling deadlines, relationships, and endless to-do lists leaves us stressed, drained, and yearning for more. It’s not just about coffee and weekend binges. We’re talking about unearthing inner peace, boosting focus, and reclaiming your life. Sound unrealistic? It’s not.

**Welcome to Mindful Well-Being Membership.**
This isn't a quick fix. It's a 4-week transformative journey designed by, and for, working professionals. Through powerful, non-religious, and accessible practices, our experienced practitioners will help you:

#### **Melt away stress & find deep relaxation**

Learn simple techniques to **breathe easy**, let go of tension, and **discover calmness amidst the storm**.

#### **Restore balance & reconnect with your life**

Find harmony in the **midst of busyness**, **rekindle** your relationships and **rediscover** your purpose.

#### **Sharpen your focus & achieve more**

Break through **mental clutter**, prioritize with clarity, and get things done **without feeling overwhelmed**.

#### **Build resilience & face challenges with ease**

**Gain** inner strength, **navigate obstacles** with grace, and **make decisions with confidence**.

**Forget escaping to the Himalayas. Integrate mindfulness into your everyday life, one step at a time.** Imagine feeling calm, focused, and energized, even when your inbox explodes or your boss loses it. **That’s Mindful Well-Being.**
• 4-week program online meditation subscription
• 8 classes per month
• Every Monday & Thursday 8 - 9pm

For more details: [https://thegoldenspace.com.my/mindful-well-being/](https://thegoldenspace.com.my/mindful-well-being/)
Or WhatsApp +6011 4058 2178

There's a one-time trial session too. Ask us!

event_title: Mindset Mondays - Group Mindset exercises & guided Meditation/Visualisation
event_description:
Welcome to Mindset Mondays!

We meet every Monday at 8pm AEST/AEDT (Melbourne time) on Zoom for a 30 Minute mindset and personal development enhancing session.

Each week has a different theme such as Confidence, Overcoming Fear, Inner Peace, Personal Growth, Decision Making, Forgiveness etc.

The session layout is:

* 5 Minute talk about the topic of the week.
* 10 Minutes of journaling exercises to explore the topic.
* 15 Minutes guided meditation/visualisation based on the topic.

After the 30 minutes you can either leave or stay on for a little bit to discuss anything that comes up or just to chat. We have some great discussions!

**IMPORTANT NOTE**: I do not let people join after 8:05pm. As the sessions have a running structure designed to go deep into a topic, if you join late you will have no idea what we're doing and I can't re-explain what we're doing or bring you up to speed quickly as it breaks the flow for everyone else. To participate and get the most out of the session, be on time.

You don't need to have your camera on or say anything.

As we all have busy lives, and there are many distractions fighting for your attention, it's good to develop a practice honing the most valuable tool you have, your mind. This way you can keep your attention where you want it.

Each of the sessions is designed to make you think, to explore some new ways of thinking and to explore ways to bring calmness to your mind.

Listening to inspiring ideas, journaling to explore your mind and direct it, and meditation, are all great ways to become the master of your destiny and not a victim of your circumstances.

Each session is led by me, David Keith Turner, and I am a Mindset Transformation Coach. I help Ambitious Optimists set and achieve worthy goals through mindset shifting and habit building.

As I've been in many paid group courses, programs, coaching sessions and events, I know the power a weekly call with other likeminded people can be! Especially those that are guided and serve a purpose.

It's my absolute pleasure to run these sessions and I hope to see you there :)

David Keith Turner

P.S. Please message me with any questions or feel free to check out more information at...

www.davidkeithturner.com/mindsetmondays

event_title: Walnut Creek, California Food Addicts in Recovery Anonymous
event_description:
**Having trouble controlling the way you eat?**

**Are you overweight? Underweight?**

**Obsessed with food, weight, or dieting?**

**You are not alone. There is a solution.**

**Food Addicts in Recovery Anonymous (FA)** is a program based on the **Twelve Steps of Alcoholics Anonymous (AA).**

There are **no dues, fees, or weigh-ins** at FA meetings.

FA is a **fellowship of individuals** who, through **shared experience and mutual support**, are recovering from the disease of **food addiction**.

FA members are **people of all ages**, from every part of the world.

FA includes people who were **morbidly obese**, **substantially underweight**, or even at a **normal weight**.

Regardless of their size, they were **tormented by cravings, dieting, bulimia, and/or an obsession with exercise**

event_title: 🌍 JUST GUTSY — Solo Travelers Meetup (Kuala Lumpur 4PM local time)
event_description:
**No pressure. No plans. Just new friends ✨**
Whether you’re visiting Kuala Lumpur for the first time, just arrived, or simply love meeting curious, open-minded people, this bi-weekly **Just Gutsy** meetup is an easy way to share stories, make friends, and explore the city together.

**Who it’s for?**
• Solo travelers
• Expats & digital nomads
• Locals who love meeting travelers

**What to expect?**
✨ Relaxed, **self-led** gathering
🤝 Small group energy
🌍 Travel stories, tips & shared adventures
🍹 Grab a drink or snack if you like

**How it works?**
This is a self-led meetup, meaning I set it up for you but the magic happens between you.
To make sure everyone finds each other smoothly, please follow these simple coordination steps:

1️⃣ **RSVP** Thoughtfully
If you’re planning to come, tap “Going.”
It helps others feel confident joining and builds momentum before the meetup.
Even if you’re 80% sure, RSVP.
If your plans change, please update your RSVP so others aren’t expecting your attendance.

2️⃣ **Confirm 2 Hours Before** (Essential)
To ensure the meetup runs, please comment on this event page at least 2 hours before the start time:
“Confirmed for today 😊”
This allows everyone to see, ahead of time, who is actually coming.
The meetup moves forward when at least 2 people confirm 2 hours before the event.
If fewer than 2 confirmations are posted by then, attendance may be low that day.
This avoids anyone showing up uncertain.

3️⃣ **Signal Your Arrival**
When you arrive, comment on this event page:
“Arrived” + where you’re sitting + what you’re wearing
Example:
“Arrived. Sitting outside near the entrance. Wearing a black t-shirt.”
This ensures everyone can easily find each other.
Recognition Tip
Having a clear profile photo on Meetup is highly encouraged — it makes recognition effortless and helps the group form faster.

**When & Where?**
📅 Day: Every Monday,Tuesday, Wednesday & Saturday
🕓 Time: **4:00 PM local time**
📍 Meeting point: In front of Feeka Coffee Roasters.
**Arrive on time,** smaller groups make it easier to connect.

**Why join Just Gutsy?**
• Meet open-minded people who genuinely want to connect
• Feel at home anywhere in the world
• Be part of a growing global solo travel community
• Early members get lifetime premium access to the future Just Gutsy app 🚀 Join here 👉 https://justgutsy.com/

**About Just Gutsy**

**Just Gutsy** is a global community for solo travelers who are free spirited, love exploring the world believe that real connections make the best memories.

Hi, I am Syrine,
I started solo traveling in 2018 and it changed my life, from introvert to extrovert. Along the way, I loved meeting people, but also felt how hard it can be to find connection when traveling alone.
I’m building Just Gutsy so no one has to feel alone on the road — and so anyone who’s still shy to take that first step has a welcoming place to start. 💛

event_title: Manning Meditation Class
event_description:
Don’t miss out on these free, casual meditation classes! The meditation classes cater for people of all experience levels and are run in a relaxed environment. No stretches or physical exercises are involved in learning this simple yet effective technique.

**Effortless Meditation**
The word ‘Sahaja’ can be translated as ‘Spontaneous’ or ‘Simple’, and is at the outset a hypothesis that meditation can come easily for those who are willing to practice regularly (even briefly each day).
Sahaja Yoga defines meditation as ‘thoughtless awareness’, a state where you are completely conscious but at the same time completely without thought. In this state, we gain a unique perspective and insight into the beauty of our own innate nature.

Physical and mental health benefits have also been attributed to regular meditation practice through comprehensive Australian research (Learn more in the book ‘silence your mind’ or at www.beyondthemind.com).

Sahaja Yoga meditation was founded by HH Shri Mataji Nirmala Devi in 1970, and is based on kundalini awakening. Shri Mataji felt that meditation is an essential part of our spiritual growth. She felt that because meditation is so essential, a free and simple method to achieve it must be made available for everyone. Sahaja Yoga is now practiced in over 52 countries worldwide.

Learn more here
https://youtube.com/playlist?list=PLpn47Y9bqAkucSPCMYJqONCaytIUlvQcX

event_title: Dinner (6:15) + Hokum (7:00) at Willeton (Cheap Tuesday)
event_description:
**Please join me for the latest film from writer/director Damian McCarthy, and starring Adam Scott. McCarthy's last two films *Oddity* and *Caveat* showed he has a knack for unsettling imagery and creepy scenes. Hokum premiered at SXSW 2026 to rave reviews, heralding it as a terrifying contender for "the Best Horror Movie of 2026."**
**We'll meet at Pepper Lunch for dinner and a chat at 6:15pm before the film at 7pm. [Tickets available here](https://www.hoyts.com.au/orders/tickets?cinemaId=SOUTHL&sessionId=284215). My seat is E6. They're all recliners.**

*Haunted by his mother's violent childhood death and the looming end of his bestselling trilogy, cantankerous author Ohm Bauman (Adam Scott) seeks closure by scattering his parents' ashes at the remote Irish inn where they honeymooned. Arriving on Halloween weekend, he finds Bilberry Woods Inn populated by eccentric guests and staff who share one strict rule: the Honeymoon Suite stays locked (or so goes the legend) to keep an ancient witch imprisoned inside.*

[Trailer](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=jP2nDyQWBOU).

"*An atmospheric & soulful haunt. McCarthy delivers an eerie tale of a haunted hotel, but is more focused on peeling back the layers of his lead. Between his chilling frames & Scott's spot-on work, Hokum succeeds as both a great scare and character piece.*" -- Perri Nemiroff on Youtube.

"*I enjoy how McCarthy mixes human evils with the supernatural. With a great atmosphere and well-placed scares, Hokum is worth checking out.*" -- Jazzmin Crawley, Horror Movie Blog.

People of all ages and backgrounds are welcome - so don't be shy if you haven't met any of us before. And if you've got any questions just leave a comment or feel free to shoot me a message. Please also remember to keep your RSVP up to date.

About Hoyts Southlands

* Located upstairs at the Southlands shopping centre on Burrendah Blvd, Willeton. There is free parking.
* They have a [free rewards program](https://www.hoyts.com.au/rewards) that gives you discounted tickets. All seats are recliners.
* Pepper Lunch is a fast service Japanese restaurant that does sizzling curries, piping hot rice & meat dishes, teppan pasta, etc.

event_title: 🍔 Burgers & Games at Grill’d East Vic Park
event_description:
Come along to our traditional fortnightly games night at Grill’d Vic Park!

The event starts at 630 but feel free to arrive early and head to the long table at the back before the booths. You can order at the counter or by QR code on the table at anytime, then settle in and make some space for the games.

As there can be a lot happening during the event we may need to move places so that a game can flow smoothly, sometimes if you aren’t playing it can be good to swap spots with someone who is so they can be closer to the action. The table closest to the booths is often a place to chat or on the bench table closest to the counter.

Feel free to bring along your own games but be aware that it may be hard to teach something too complicated, and please nothing that could be offensive. Easy to learn stuff is best.

This can be a lively event with a large turnout in a public restaurant, there is noise and a bit happening, it may push people out of their comfort zone which can be exciting but also challenging, please take care of yourself and feel free to bring a support person.

This can be a challenging event for host too, so please respect that hosts are autistic volunteers and have their limits, any assistance in bringing games and leading games is helpful.

There is free parking around Vic Park at the IGA and the Park Centre Shopping Centre, you can get a ticket if parking on the street.

So come along and join us on Tuesday night for Burgers and Games at Grill’d Victoria Park.

Happy Days

**Accessibility**
Door width & pinch point: wide doors, tables and chairs can be moved.
Lighting: pretty soft lighting
Noise level: moderate to high
Activity: moderate to high
Flat/rough surfaces: flat
Disability toilet access: yes
Contact host for more information

-== About SAGE ==-

SAGE is a social group for people who identify as being on the autistic spectrum, or people who suspect that they are. You do not need a diagnosis to come along to events.

Our core values are respect, acceptance, and community, and by holding to these values we aim to create a friendly and safe place for members to relate to others and have some fun.

New members are always welcome and we understand that it can take courage to attend events if your social confidence isn’t high. Feel free to to message event host for details or assurance, we want to meet you.

SAGE is non-political, independent, and entirely volunteer operated. SAGE was established in February 2020.

If you would like to donate to support SAGE go to:

https://gofund.me/9d1e18e7

Thank You
