event_title: Recovering from Religion - Support Group Meeting (Monthly)
event_description:
The Perth Recovering From Religion support group meets on the first Tuesday of each month 6:30pm.

Recovering from Religion offers hope, healing and support to those who've been harmed by religion.

The primary focus of a Recovering From Religion support group is to provide ongoing and personal support to individuals as they let go of their religious beliefs. This transitional period is an ongoing process that can result in a range of emotions, as well as a ripple effect of consequences throughout an individual’s life. As such, the support meetings are safe and anonymous places to express these doubts, fears, and experiences without biased feedback or proselytizing.

We are here to help each other through this journey.

There is no cost to attend this support group but, if you feel so inclined, donations can be made at [recoveringfromreligion.org.au/donate/](https://recoveringfromreligion.au/donate/)

RfR has an immense collection of well-curated resources available as you go through your journey. The page includes resources for mental and physical crises, working with relationships, for issues stemming from specific religions, coming out stories, and much more. Head to [www.recoveringfromreligion.org/resources](http://www.recoveringfromreligion.org/resources) for these helpful resources.

If you find you need to talk with someone before the meeting date, RfR has a hotline and online chat for that express purpose. Call +61 3 4505 2402 to speak with someone or go to [www.recoveringfromreligion.org](http://www.recoveringfromreligion.org/) to chat online.

If you are in need of professional help, we can offer the Secular Therapy Project to provide options to connect with a professional therapist. All therapists have been thoroughly vetted by our organization and offer only evidence-based and non-religious treatment. Connect with them at [www.seculartherapy.org.](http://www.seculartherapy.org./)

Like what RfR does? Why not volunteer! There are quite a few options. Head over to [www.recoveringfromreligion.org/volunteer](http://www.recoveringfromreligion.org/volunteer) for more info.

event_title: [HYBRID] Tuesday Evening Practice Session: Total Relaxation
event_description:
You are invited to join us for this Tuesday evening practice session either online or in person.

**Session Agenda**

* Sitting Meditation
* Total Relaxation
* (In-person Only) Walking Meditation & Touching the Earth
* Dharma Sharing

**For in-person gatherings:** Please arrive no later than 6.45 pm to give yourself time to get settled before the sitting meditation begins.

**Virtual Session**
6.55 pm to 8.30 pm AEST (approximate)

To support the feeling of togetherness and safety, please have your video on during the session.

The Zoom Link is accessible on the right side of this page.
Zoom Password: breathe

**In-Person Session**
6.45 pm to 9.00 pm AEST

E-Vam Institute
673 Lygon St
Princes Hill VIC 3054
https://maps.app.goo.gl/bYySuFNqCTiN2LZY7

**Newcomers**
If you will be joining us for the first time, we ask that you please arrive 5 minutes before the practice session for a short orientation and welcome to our community. Please reach out to us at breathe@melbourneriversangha.com.au before the session to make arrangements.

**Dana Paramita (Giving)**
Giving (dana) is an essential practice of generating generosity, practising letting go, and developing our capacity to spread our compassion and love. Our practice sessions are offered freely, but if you would like to offer a financial contribution to support the sangha to continue beautifully into the future, then please do so using our bank details below. A suggested donation is $10 per practice session, but please give the amount that speaks to your own heart. No one is turned away for lack of funds.

Name: MELBOURNE RIVER SANGHA
BSB: 633000
Account number: 232117564

**This Week’s Practice: Total Relaxation**
*“Many of us are over-scheduled. Even the lives of our children are over-scheduled. When we can allow ourselves to rest and relax, healing becomes possible. There is no healing without relaxation.”* \- Plum Village

Total relaxation is the practice of completely stopping, lying down, and following a guided relaxation or doing a ‘body scan” to create the conditions needed to restore ourselves through true rest. [Learn more about the practice of total relaxation](https://youtu.be/oTmSjYkH8Fg?si=qwNSr4o38t_9e4Jy).

**Core Practices**
Sitting Meditation \| Mindful Breathing
Mindfulness is always mindfulness of something. In sitting meditation, we practice mindful breathing, the most foundational mindfulness practice. In this simple, but deep, practice, we bring awareness to our in-breath as we are breathing in and to our out-breath as we are breathing out. We learn to feel our breath, either by becoming aware of it passing through our nostrils or by feeling the rise and fall of our belly. We do not control our breathing in any way; we practice simply being aware of it and relaxing any tension in our bodies. Following our breath, we come back to the present moment and arrive in our body here and now. [Learn more about the practice of sitting meditation](https://youtu.be/pwjfeeuf_Ig?si=_re9tn5HaSV0ouF9).

Dharma Sharing
During dharma sharing, we come together as a community to share our experiences, joys and challenges, or our questions, insights, and experience of the practice. We share what is in our hearts rather than speaking about ideas or theoretical concepts. We also have the opportunity to practice deep listening while others are speaking. Dharma sharing is confidential. By sharing our experiences, we contribute to the collective insight and understanding of the sangha and begin to see our shared difficulties or aspirations and recognise our connections to each other. [Learn more about the practice of Dharma sharing](https://youtu.be/nz7QQ391F7k?si=mlCqCMOT62rar72x).

Walking Meditation
Walking meditation allows us to practice arriving fully in the present moment. We focus completely on each step, becoming aware of our feet touching the ground and the flow of our breathing. As we walk, we harmonise our breathing with our steps in a natural way - taking 2 or 3 steps as we breathe in and 2 or 3 steps as we breathe out, depending on our natural rhythm and comfort. Through walking meditation, we release our thinking about the future, the past, our projects, and worries, and learn to become 100% present. [Learn more about walking meditation](https://youtu.be/CY0Ro-Kg5Yc?si=plc6-DMwJW7eqsY0).

We look forward to practising with you.

Breathe and smile,
Melbourne River Sangha

event_title: Open Heart Meditation & Reiki Tummo Healing
event_description:
Hello Everyone!

Come & join us for a relaxing Open Heart meditation & Reiki Tummo healing. You will be guided to be connected to your spiritual heart again, to feel the calmness, peace, joy, and love from your Heart.. to experience more of this quality in our daily lives.

The only thing you have to bring is a yoga mat to lie on for receiving the healing at the end of the session, your heart and your smile, feel free to bring your friends, family member, neighbors, colleagues..

Please park at Thirsty Camel secure parking and walk towards the building. Ring 601 at the intercom and walk towards the lift, press 6 on the lift.

See you there!

:)LL
Christine/ Grace/ Sam
+61 413 264 386

We ask for a $5 donation to help with running costs. Feel free to call or message Grace with your queries. Make sure to also connect with our community on Facebook:
https://www.facebook.com/groups/openheartmeditation

INTRODUCTION
On the surface level Open Heart Meditation is a very simple yet effective way to shut the busy mind down, relax and unwind, and to reconnect with peace. Many people come as a quick way to recharge for the week and to find inner peace.

On a deeper level Open Heart Meditation is about opening the heart to know your True Self and to connect with our Creator - the ultimate source of love and life - what we call "True Source". The divine energy from True Source (the Reiki energy) is a beautiful restorative energy of healing, joy, love and blessings. It's suitable for people from all religions because it's non denominational and helps people to better connect with their chosen spiritual path - experientially through the heart (rather than through the head with more thinking and theory).

When you are connected to this abundant source of life energy every day, you will in turn receive life more abundantly. It will improve your overall happiness, health, and general well-being. You will find yourself more calm and less reactive; your choices in life will be more sound and centred; and you will be more in line with your True Self - all beyond the limited intelligence of the mind, other worldly wisdoms, and fluctuating emotions.

The greater purpose of this practice for those who wish to pursue it beyond relaxation and healing is to return home - back to our Creator from where we all originated from - the ultimate source of life and unconditional love.

Open Heart Meditation is therefore very different to many popular types of meditation, such as Mindfulness Meditation for the following reasons:
• It does not rely on the mind or observation, or on emotions, but rather it is focused on the heart. We teach you how to easily shut the mind down, to open the heart, and sense from it.
• Instead of practicing detachment, we teach you to attach to the source of abundant life first. This will then help you detach from all forms of negativities naturally without any effort. In doing so, this will gradually iron out negative reactive responses, smoothing your life's path.
• This blessing, is a gift of Grace and cannot be earned by greater effort. The only thing one can do is to just receive this gift from True Source with an open heart, in gratitude.
This group is dedicated to those who would like to learn, practice and share about Open Heart Meditation. Everyone is welcome to join, learn and contribute in a positive, safe and caring environment. :-)

For more information about Open Heart Meditation:
https://naturalwayofliving.com/wellness-programs/open-heart-meditation/

PERTH TESTIMONIALS
"We are so incredibly blessed with Open Heart Meditation - the truth that we've learnt and discovered for ourselves; the lovely community; and what it is doing for our soul permanently. So grateful for this huge blessing. Thank you all for what is coming to my first year with you smiley lot." Ronnie Chua, Perth Australia

"Isn't it lovely when you intend to pray for something and immediately feel the love pour down like a bucket of water..Then your heart remembers that the love is everywhere and so you feel like you're swimming in it instead..Now you can't even remember what you were going to pray about because the only 2 words left are 'thank you'" Yalda, Perth, Australia

"Before learning about the heart, when I prayed to True Source, it felt flat, and I was never sure if my prayers were heard. I remember how I prayed only when I had problems in my life, or because at school or at church a bunch of us were being told to pray together. Words came out of me without any feeling; I was simply reading prayer lines. Now it is completely different: prayers have become beautiful, and I am able to go into a deep state of connection. I feel how I am communicating with True Source-how my prayers reach up to True Source, Lovingly listening, and are accepted with the beauty of Love." Sunny Tjandrakesuma, Perth, Australia

event_title: Bassendean Meditation Class
event_description:
Don’t miss out on these free, casual meditation classes! The meditation classes cater for people of all experience levels and are run in a relaxed environment. No stretches or physical exercises are involved in learning this simple yet effective technique.

**Effortless Meditation**
The word ‘Sahaja’ can be translated as ‘Spontaneous’ or ‘Simple’, and is at the outset a hypothesis that meditation can come easily for those who are willing to practice regularly (even briefly each day).
Sahaja Yoga defines meditation as ‘thoughtless awareness’, a state where you are completely conscious but at the same time completely without thought. In this state, we gain a unique perspective and insight into the beauty of our own innate nature.

Physical and mental health benefits have also been attributed to regular meditation practice through comprehensive Australian research (Learn more in the book ‘silence your mind’ or at www.beyondthemind.com).

Sahaja Yoga meditation was founded by HH Shri Mataji Nirmala Devi in 1970, and is based on kundalini awakening. Shri Mataji felt that meditation is an essential part of our spiritual growth. She felt that because meditation is so essential, a free and simple method to achieve it must be made available for everyone. Sahaja Yoga is now practiced in over 52 countries worldwide.

**Learn more here**
https://youtube.com/playlist?list=PLpn47Y9bqAkucSPCMYJqONCaytIUlvQcX

the mind doesn’t quite stop. It keeps going.

So when something feels off, our instinct is familiar. We try to fix it.
Push through it. Do a little more.

But sometimes, what we actually need is not more effort.
It is a moment to return.

In this online Superhumans meetup, Nadam will guide you through a simple, direct meditation experience designed to help you slow down, settle your system, and reconnect with yourself.

event_title: The journey to production AI: A practical guide for platform engineers and SREs
event_description:
Most teams are experimenting with AI agents. Few are running them reliably in production. This session breaks down how to move from first agent to trusted, repeatable workflows with real telemetry, strong context, and control over cost, accuracy, and scale.

Getting an AI agent to work in a demo is easy. Getting it to run reliably in production at scale, with real telemetry, without hallucinating or torching your infrastructure, is a problem entirely different. This talk maps the journey from ad hoc experimentation to trusted Production AI: build your first agent, deploy a real use case, engineer your context, and get control of your data. We'll show how AURA, an open-source agentic harness, eliminates 80% of agent-building boilerplate via simple config and how pairing it with Active Telemetry cuts investigation costs by up to 25x by delivering curated, just-in-time context instead of information bloat.

We'll cover:

* Why common agent frameworks break down at scale
* How to define repeatable agentic workflows your whole team can trust
* Why context engineering is the unlock for reliable Production AI
* A practical roadmap: from first agent to autonomous ops

event_title: Stop Writing Code like it's April 2026!
event_description:
Software is no longer written by people alone, so why are we still running our SDLCs as if it is?

In this talk, Aidan Morgan will draw on twelve months of hands-on experimentation with agentic engineering teams assembled for an AI-first delivery model.

He’ll introduce a mental model for AI-first delivery, share how he’s using deterministic tooling to create fast, automated backpressure to keep agents on track, and challenge some long-held assumptions about software delivery.

If you’ve been wondering how to move beyond “AI-assisted coding” and into genuinely agentic software delivery, this one’s for you.

**About Aidan**
Aidan Morgan is a four-time startup VP/CTO, founder, and seasoned technology executive with more than two decades of experience leading high-impact engineering teams and scaling organisations. He has driven innovation across startups, scale-ups, and enterprise environments in technologies spanning embedded systems, cloud platforms, and autonomous robotics.

Aidan is currently Chief Engineer at Bankwest, where he is helping guide the bank’s transition to a digital-only future while driving modern engineering and agentic engineering practices across teams and platforms.

He is also an Industry Fellow at AITAI, supporting research and industry engagement initiatives in advanced AI and engineering, and mentoring founders and technology leaders in the adoption of emerging AI capabilities.

**Agenda:**

* **7:30 am**: Shameless plugs and announcements. Introduce yourself or announce your startup to the Perth startup community!
* **7:45 am: Stop Writing Code like it's April 2026 with Aidan Morgan**
* **8:30 am**: Networking & delicious breakfast treats
* **9:00 am**: Finish

**Live stream**
This event will also be live streamed by the amazing folks at [[Perthvideo.com.au](http://perthvideo.com.au/)]([https://perthvideo.com.au/](https://perthvideo.com.au/))

Access the live stream and all our post-event recordings here:

event_title: The Execution Gap: Why Capability Is the Missing Layer
event_description:
Join Fatimah Abbouchi for a focused virtual session examining why many well-designed strategies still struggle to translate into reliable execution — and the capability gaps that sit behind that problem.

Most organisations do not lack strategy.
They lack the structural capability required to deliver it consistently.

This session is about execution architecture, not project activity.

Drawing on delivery experience across PMOs, transformation offices, and executive teams, the discussion examines recurring execution challenges seen across major programs and change initiatives, including:
• planning models built around capacity rather than capability
• capability being embedded within individuals, functions, or projects
• delivery programs rebuilding cross-functional structures repeatedly
• governance models that increase activity but not execution coherence

The session focuses on how organisations can design capability structures that support reliable execution, including approaches that:
• anchor strategy in clearly defined organisational capabilities
• separate capability, roles, functions, and capacity
• reduce delivery fragmentation across programs and teams
• strengthen the structural foundations behind transformation initiatives

What This Session Will Cover
• Why strategy rarely fails — but execution consistently does
• The difference between capability, capacity, functions, and roles — and why conflating them creates delivery risk
• How capability fragmentation drives governance complexity and program fatigue
• How to anchor delivery in capability to reduce rebuilding and improve execution reliability
What You'll Leave With
• A clearer way to look at your own delivery model — and where execution pressure is likely coming from
• Language to distinguish what your organisation actually needs versus what it currently has
• A starting point for the structural conversation most transformation programs never have

Who This Session Is For
Designed for executives, programme leaders, and transformation leads who are tired of rebuilding delivery capability from scratch

Event Details
📅 Date: Wednesday 6th May 2026
🕛 Time: 12pm AEST
⏱ Duration: 60 minutes
💻 Platform: Zoom
🎟 Cost: Free (registration required)

event_title: Event-Driven Architecture in the Age of Agentic AI
event_description:
**Event-Driven Architecture in the Age of Agentic AI**
**Tales from the Trenches**

Every day, organisations generate thousands of signals — a transaction completed, a sensor triggered, a customer action taken. The ability to respond to these moments intelligently and instantly is what separates modern, adaptive organisations from those still catching up. This is the promise of event‑driven architecture, and when combined with agentic AI, it opens up a whole new world of possibility.

In this session, we welcome speakers Jamie Craig and Ronald Inglesby to explore how these two approaches come together, and what it means in practice for the people and organisations working with them.

We’ll explore:

* What event‑driven architecture is and why organisations adopt it as a design pattern, brought to life through real customer use cases.
* What it really means to go agentic, with AI moving beyond chatbots to become digital co‑workers that extend what people can do rather than replace them.
* Why agentic AI works better with event‑driven design, and how combining the two unlocks faster, smarter, and more responsive systems.
* Stories from global organisations already putting this into practice, and a look at where the technology is heading next.
* The ongoing role of humans in the loop — why people remain central to this evolution, and how the relationship between human and agent will continue to develop for the foreseeable future.

Whether you work in technology, lead organisational change, or are simply curious about where AI is taking us, this session will give you a grounded and human view of what it looks like when people and intelligent systems work side by side.

There will be time for questions and discussion after the presentation, followed by networking over drinks and pizzas.

Registration is essential, and we hope to see many new and familiar faces on 6 May!

\-\-\-
*Thank you to our supporters who make these events possible: our venue supporter [Spacecubed](https://spacecubed.com/), and our principal sponsor [Rmkble](https://rmkble.com.au/?utm_source=meetup&utm_medium=event-page-may&utm_campaign=dtp-sponsorship-2026), the home of JourneyOne, ProcessPath and DataDivers.*

\-\-\-
**Meet our speakers:**

**Jamie Craig**
*Sales Director, Solace*

Jamie is a senior technology leader at Solace with nearly four decades of experience guiding organisations through complex digital transformation.

A trusted advisor to C-suite executives across mining, retail, energy, and utilities, Jamie specialises in event-driven architecture and real-time integration, helping businesses turn data into competitive advantage through strategic partnerships with companies including SAP and leading iPaaS providers.

**Ronald Inglesby**
*Senior Solution Engineer*

Ronald is an experienced senior solution engineer at Solace, with over 30 years’ experience working in the information technology industry. He is skilled in integration, event-driven architecture, data engineering, development and support across a broad spectrum of industries.

He applies his experience and knowledge to solve enterprise computing challenges. By employing event-driven architecture, he helps organisations get data moving seamlessly between applications, IoT devices and microservices across cloud, edge, and on-premises environments.

He holds Solace certifications as a developer practitioner and solutions consultant, and the SAP certifications Modernize Integration with SAP Integration Suite and Your HR Journey to the Cloud (2023).

He has a Bachelor of Science Honours degree, majoring in computer science, from the University of Cape Town.

event_title: LGBT Perth Book Club - We Used To Live Here
event_description:
The next book club pick is [We Used To Live Here by Marcus Kliewer](https://app.thestorygraph.com/books/af8ba3c2-9225-420a-b31d-9a14dbe2a940)! It'll be held on the 6th of May at 6pm, Gino's in Fremantle. Here's the [Storygraph buddy read](https://app.thestorygraph.com/buddy_reads/9b6346ab-945d-4ab7-91af-b2779d129991). Discussion questions are [here](https://app.thestorygraph.com/book_clubs/2a843925-2aa5-4fe0-aad5-becaadf4e38c/meetings/4bf3965c-e54d-4bb4-8f2f-860429e5ff9f). You don't have to answer them at the book club. They're just there to give a jumping point for anything people would like to discuss.

Libraries in the Cities of Canning, Armadale, Rockingham, Bayswater, Stirling, Melville, Victoria Park, Joondalup, Cockburn and Kwinana have copies. You can request a purchase or use inter-library loans to have a copy sent to your local library. Keep in mind a purchase might not arrive in time.

You can access audiobook copies through Borrowbox and Libby and an eBook copy through Borrowbox. Audiobook and eBook copies are available through a subscription to Everand. Spotify has the audiobook.

If you'd like to purchase a copy, [Booko](https://booko.au/w/10120817/we-used-to-live-here) provides a price comparison for certain sites. Other stores that have copies are [Boundless Books](https://shop.boundlessbooks.com.au/p/we-used-to-live-here-2831701) and [Boffins Books](https://www.boffinsbooks.com.au/books/9781804995587/we-used-to-live-here).

Here are some interviews with the author, warning for spoilers:

* [Marcus Kliewer - Author Interview](https://armedwithabook.com/marcus-kliewer/)
* [Marcus Kliewer on writing horror and the intensity of "We Used To Live Here"](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=8zIginJslGM) (YouTube)
* [WE USED TO LIVE HERE with Marcus Kliewer](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=trq9WGqQvas) (YouTube)
* [Interview with Marcus Kliewer! | We Used To Live Here Pt. III](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=9gN-A78AvlU) (YouTube)

You don't have to have read or finished the book to attend the book club, but we will be discussing spoilers at a certain point.

I'll have the book on the table to help you find me. If you have any questions or need to update me on your arrival, feel free to message me!

event_title: Perth Weight Loss Support Group - Wednesday Evening Meetings
event_description:
People who attend support groups lose more weight than those who go it alone.

You don’t have to lose weight all by yourself - in fact it’s probably best that you don’t.

Research shows that having support on your weight loss journey enhances self-esteem and provides motivation to keep going.

A study published recently in the American Journal of Medicine found that participants who attended weekly support meetings, lost on average, five times more weight than those who relied solely on the information and advice that they were given.

So, if you’ve tried losing weight on your own with little or no success, it might be time to join a supportive group of like-minded people who are on the same journey.

The Perth Healthy Weight Support Group meets Every Wednesday night from 7:00pm to 8:00pm

event_title: Conscious Communicators Speakers’ Meeting
event_description:
**Join Us at Conscious Communicators Toastmasters!**

Are you ready to transform your communication skills, boost your confidence, and become a persuasive leader? Look no further! Conscious Communicators Toastmasters is the most efficient, enjoyable, and affordable way to achieve these goals and more.

**Why Toastmasters?**

At Toastmasters, you'll thrive in a friendly and supportive environment. Our self-paced program allows you to build confidence each time you speak, and constructive, positive evaluation is at the heart of what we do. Whether you’re giving a prepared speech or participating in a meeting, your strengths will be celebrated, and you'll receive valuable suggestions for improvement.

**What Can You Expect?**

- **Enhanced Communication Skills**: Become a more persuasive and confident speaker in your personal, social, and professional life.
- **Leadership Development**: Gain practical leadership training to unlock your full potential.
- **Supportive Community**: Join a network of like-minded individuals who are all working towards similar goals.   **Why Conscious Communicators Toastmasters?**  - **International Group and Online**: You’ll meet amazing people from all over the world who are growing, supporting, and cheering each other on and you can join from the comfort of your own home or if you are travelling. - **Communication with a Calm Presence**: You’ll not just learn clear communication! You will experience a little meditation, some philosophy and even a joy session.

**Curious? Come as Our Guest!**

Experience firsthand the welcoming atmosphere and supportive community at Conscious Communicators Toastmasters. We meet every 1st and 3rd Wednesday of each month online at:

** https://us02web.zoom.us/j/83750150082?pwd=FP07YvHH6RUFK7MwG49lKdPdPkfKWN.1

Bring your beautiful face and see what Conscious Communicators Toastmasters is all about. We can't wait to meet you!

**Want to Know More?**

Visit our club website for more information:

event_title: Does belief in intrinsic values indicate a radical misunderstanding of value?
event_description:
Workshop # 7, Intrinsic Value, Series 12, (S7,17)
This event begins 7.30 pm S'pore & WA time, 12.30 pm UK, 7.30 am NY.
An AI summary of the meeting will be in the relevant Youtube comments.

**You will need to view the relevant podcast** below on YouTube before joining the meeting in order to participate in the discussion.
[https://youtu.be/OiYMmoAo07U](https://youtu.be/OiYMmoAo07U)
I know it's stupid, but please click 'like', as it promotes circulation.
**Join this group** at meetup.com/philosophy-of-value-workshops
And **buy my book** *The Pursuit of Value* from Amazon Books

The workshops include of a prior presentation of the topic by myself on YouTube that you need to watch. The meeting itself consists of a brief review of the topic followed by questions and discussion. The weekly topic is posted a week before the event, together with a suggested reading from my work The Pursuit of Value, available through Amazon Books or myself. Transcripts of the Youtube presentation are available by email.

**THIS WEEK**: The attribution of intrinsic value to material objects and abstract principles is a widespread practice that can not only be strongly argued to be erroneous, but also indicating a pre-suppositional error that reveals both; a radical misunderstanding of value, and how that misunderstanding can be rectified. That is, the understanding and ‘practice’ of value as ontologically subjective, rather than objective, offers a new direction for human existence. But can we apprehend and live this new direction? Reading, The Pursuit of Value: Ch. 2, Scn. ix.

event_title: Find Your Purpose
event_description:
## Find Your Purpose

**A Guided Online Meet-Up for Clarity & Direction**
Many of us are doing “all the right things”, working hard, staying responsible, moving forward, yet still feel disconnected or unsure about why we are doing what we do.
This guided online meet-up is designed to help you pause, reflect, and gain **clear direction**, not through motivation or advice, but through **structured self-inquiry**.

## What to Expect

This is a **facilitated, reflective session** where you will:

* Explore your **long-term purpose direction**
* Identify what truly matters to you in this phase of life
* Translate clarity into **short-term, actionable priorities**
* Leave with a grounded understanding of where your energy belongs next

The session is thoughtful, respectful, and practical, not motivational or spiritual in nature.

## Agenda & Flow

**1\. Setting the Context**
Why purpose feels confusing today and what clarity actually means
**2\. Long\-Term Vision \(Ikigai Framework\)**
Understanding purpose through:

* What you love
* What you are good at
* What the world needs
* What you can be valued for

**3\. Short\-Term Direction \(Passion Test\)**

* Identifying current priorities
* Converting purpose into 90-day focus areas

**4\. Integration & Reflection**
Connecting long-term direction with short-term action
**5\. Closing & Q&A**
Personal questions will be addressed toward the end

## You Will Walk Away With

* A clear **purpose direction**
* Priority focus for the **next 90 days**
* Reduced confusion in life and career decisions
* A grounded action plan you can realistically follow

## What to Bring

* A notebook or digital notes
* Openness to reflect honestly
* Willingness to engage when invited, no pressure to overshare

No prior knowledge or preparation is required.

## Session Guidelines

To keep the space respectful and focused:

* Personal comments are avoided during the session
* Personal questions will be taken at the end
* Please avoid side or private chats
* Participation in reflections is encouraged when invited

## How to Join

* This is an **online live session**
* The joining link will be shared with registered attendees before the event
* Please join on time to get the full experience

**Facilitated by:**
**Anubhuti Sharaf**
Life & Leadership Coach \| Founder\, *Swayantran*
If you want clarity that leads to action, you’re welcome to join.

event_title: Depression Support Group
event_description:
**Group Structure:**

* **6 Zoom Sessions \| Every Wednesday at 8:00 PM IST \| 75 minutes**
* **Confidential \| Small Group\, 6–8 Participants**
* **Led by a Clinical Psychologist**

**A supportive, confidential space to navigate depression through guided reflection, storytelling, and strength-based practices building connection, compassion, and clarity along the way**

**Register at:** https://www.soulup.in/products/support-groups-depression-support-group

### **Goals of the Group**

1. **Understand Your Depression :-** Explore the emotional, physical, and relational dimensions of depression with honesty and support.
2. **Rebuild Daily Life :-** Reconnect with simple routines, pleasure, mastery, and small moments of meaning.
3. **Strengthen Emotional Tools :-** Learn grounding techniques, self-soothing strategies, and practices for emotional regulation.
4. **Reclaim Connection & Direction:-** Reflect on relationships, life goals, and values to foster a renewed sense of purpose.
5. **Practice Self-Compassion:-** Gently shift from self-neglect to self-kindness—and hold space for gratitude, hope, and healing.

### **Who is the Group For?**

This group is for adults living with depression who are seeking a space to feel seen, heard, and supported. Whether you're newly diagnosed, in recovery, or simply feeling disconnected, this group offers practical tools, shared stories, and a path forward—one gentle step at a time.

**Register at:** https://www.soulup.in/products/support-groups-depression-support-group

We’ll also show real examples and simple workflows you can understand and apply.

**Who Should Attend**

event_title: Unleashing your team is a challenge of culture, not collaboration
event_description:
“Unleashing your team is a challenge of culture, not collaboration” explores a critical shift in how organizations think about productivity and performance. While many teams invest heavily in tools and collaboration platforms, true success lies beyond technology. This session highlights that having the right tools is not enough—what truly drives impact is the culture in which those tools are used.
Drawing on Atlassian’s vision to “unleash the potential of every team,” the session will dive into how organizations can move from tool-centric thinking to a culture-first approach. It will address common challenges such as tool fatigue, lack of alignment, and unclear ownership, and explain why teams often struggle even when they have access to the best collaboration solutions .
Participants will gain insights into building a strong “system of work,” where teams are aligned to shared goals, empowered with clarity, and enabled to use tools effectively. The session will also emphasize the importance of user enablement, strategic tool adoption, and fostering behaviors that drive accountability, transparency, and continuous improvement.
By the end of the session, attendees will understand how to create an environment where collaboration tools truly add value—supported by a culture that enables teams to perform at their best, deliver faster outcomes, and achieve higher impact

This is a global online meet & greet. People join from all over. Come with curiosity. Stay for the experience and connection.

### **What to Expect**

• Small group breakout sessions - everyone will get to share!
• 1 simple prompt per round - life, physical, mental, emotional, spiritual....
• 10–15 minute rotations
• Easy questions that anyone can answer

The questions we ask matter as they shape the conversations we have and the connections we build.

The prompts are designed to help you:
• get to know people beyond surface level
• find others who share similar values
• feel seen and understood for who you are

This is where you start to build new connections with people who *get you*, because the conversation actually meant something.

Meet your host:

Want more? Let's connect on YouTube & IG @TherapistOnVacation😊

event_title: Python WA May Meetup
event_description:
Are you interested in using AI and agents? Are you concerned that AI and agents are going to take your job? Well, we've got you covered because **Sam Bishop** and **Aiden Morgan** will be covering both subjects at our May meetup.

**Thanks again to our friends at** [Bankwest](bankwest.com.au) **are hosting us for our May meetup!**

* **5:30 - Doors open for guest registration (please bring valid photo ID to comply with Bankwests' building access security policy**)
* **5:30-6:00 - Networking. Bar service and light refreshments provided**
* **6:00-7:20 - Introductions, Bankwest and Python WA keynote speakers**
* **7:20-7:40 - Networking**

**NB:** ***Due to limited capacity, please RSVP and/or kindly update your availability where required, by Tue 28 April. Bankwest and Python WA thank you for your cooperation and understanding 🙏***

**From COBOL to Copilots: Why "No-Code" Never Kills the Coder**
Every few years somebody swears the newest tool to magically avoid needing developers will boot developers out of a job. Yet here we are, still coding, still shipping. Come along for a quick tour from COBOL’s “English you can compile” to today’s LLM powered vibe coding agents and see why each wave of “code without coders” crashed on the same rocks. You’ll leave with a pocketful of history, a nose for hype, and concrete ways to pair AI helpers with your hard-won craft instead of handing the keys over.
*Sam Bishop is a professional software developer and snake charmer a.k.a. Python problem solver. They are an amateur content creator and rocket scientist who likes working on their software and hardware projects, playing games, and 3D printing things.*

**Building Fully Automated Agents**
Software isn’t being written by humans anymore—so why do our delivery models still assume that it is?

This talk distils a year of hands‑on experimentation building agentic engineering teams that generate Python systems. Aidan will share a practical AI‑first delivery model, show how deterministic tooling creates fast automated backpressure, and explain how we keep agents reliably producing correct, maintainable Python without re‑introducing human bottlenecks.
If you want to move past AI‑assisted coding and into truly agentic Python software delivery, this session is for you.
*Aidan Morgan is a four‑time startup VP/CTO, founder, and seasoned technology executive with more than two decades of experience leading high‑impact engineering teams and scaling digital product organisations. He has driven innovation across startups, scale‑ups, and enterprise environments, with a career spanning embedded systems, cloud platforms, autonomous robotics, and large‑scale digital transformation.*
*Aidan currently serves as Chief Engineer at Bankwest, where he is helping guide the bank’s transition to a digital‑only future, driving modern engineering and Agentic Engineering practices across teams and platforms.*

*He also works as an Industry Fellow at AITAI, actively supporting research and industry engagement initiatives in advanced AI and engineering. His role includes mentoring founders and technology leaders in the adoption of emerging AI capabilities—reflected in ongoing collaborative AI discussions recognised by AITAI’s leadership.*

**A special thank you our food, drink and generous venue sponsor,** [Bankwest](bankwest.com.au)**, for making our May meetup possible 🙌**

**We look forward to welcoming you at Level 1, Bankwest Place (Podium Rooms) Perth 🏦!**

event_title: Floreat Speakers Rostrum Club 34
event_description:
Public speaking can be really scary.

The best place to learn the skill, build your confidence and overcome the fear is to join a friendly, supportive club where everyone has been through exactly the same fears. We have lots of fun - it's not all serious and that's what makes it really enjoyable and creates the environment where you learn fast.

What we do:
Rostered speakers present their speaking activities and receive coaching. Visitors don't need to speak. You can participate if you wish but it is entirely up to you and there will be absolutely no pressure.

What to bring:
Nothing but a sense of humour and a desire to build your confidence and speaking skill

Important to know:
No-one is asked to speak if they don't want to. You are welcome to come and just see what we do.

Meeting goes from 6:15pm to 7:30pm. Arrive about 10 minutes early to grab a seat and have a chat prior!

Contact us on [rostrum@davidprice.com](mailto:rostrum@davidprice.com) if you have any questions.

event_title: Slow Down & Rebalance Yoga (Weekly)
event_description:
Step away from the noise and return to center with a practice dedicated to your nervous system. These sessions are designed to help you move out of "survival mode" and into a state of deep, restorative peace.

### **Mondays: Yin Yoga \| 6\.30 \- 7\.30pm**

A meditative practice focused on deep stillness. We use long-held floor poses to target the connective tissues and quiet the mind.

* **Switch** from "fight-or-flight" to deep rest.

A rhythmic, fluid practice that connects breath with motion. This class uses somatic movement to release daily stress and build inner strength.

* **Soothe** the mind with rhythmic, fluid movement.
* **Release** daily stress through gentle somatic flow.
* **Build** resilience and inner peace through breath.

event_title: Practical Philosophy Meetup - Perth 🇦🇺
event_description:
**🏛️ WHAT'S PRACTICAL PHILOSOPHY ABOUT?**

Practical Philosophy is a weekly meet-up that brings critical thinkers together for an opportunity to dive deep on a topic, practice communicating, and building a community. All with no ‘official’ philosophy knowledge required! 😎

Practical Philosophy is based on the idea that philosophy should be accessible to all, and not something reserved only for academics. Each week we choose a topic, and the goal is to use the conversation to develop our critical thinking and communication as we explore that topic.
The goal is to help develop our Critical Thinking, Communication, and Community 🙌

**📓 HOW DOES IT WORK?**

Each week, we pick one topic and discuss it. The topic for the week is shared, generally in the [WhatsApp group](https://tally.so/r/vGegL4), along with jump-off points to get the conversation going.
When groups get bigger than 7-8 people, it’s important that we break into smaller groups. This way we are able to maintain a conversational flow as opposed to having our meetups feel like a discourse or lecture.

**General Meeting Agenda**
Each Practical Philosophy meet-up follows this general timeline:

* 19:00-19:15 - People arrive and chat, get to know each other before the official start.
* 19:15-19:25 - Meeting introduction, explanation of Practical Philosophy and the topic for the week, read the guidelines and the overview so attendees know what to expect in terms of timelines.
* 19:25-20:45 - Break into small groups - introduce yourselves and general thoughts on the topic, open discussion afterwards. At this point the group can review the ‘jump-off’ questions as needed, but they are just there to help guide the conversation. It’s not obligatory to answer them.
* 20:45-21:00 - Bring everyone back together to discuss conclusions of the topic - each group gives a short 1-minute summary. Organizer concludes the meeting and everyone takes a group photo.
* 21:00 - The official meeting is over but it’s a great opportunity to get to know people in a non-structured environment, so we often stick around, chat, and get to know each other.

**🧧PRICING?**

Practical Philosophy Club is FREE to attend! We just ask that if we are meeting in a café, buy a beverage! Some chapters rent a space for us to meet, and they may ask for donations to help contribute to the cost of rent. Donations will always be 100% voluntary though!

**💛 WHAT PEOPLE SAY ABOUT PRACTICAL PHILOSOPHY**

**🗣** “I feel like Philosophy Club helps someone build their own belief system through a series of perspectives while not promoting what’s right and wrong” - Ekam

🗣 “I got hooked from day 1 (the topic was authority) and felt it was the right place for me. I feel it is a place where people can go to learn and discuss new perspectives and increase critical thinking and community by interacting with people who might challenge your point of views but at the same time encourage you to have openness and mind flexibility.” - Daniel

**🤓 FAQ**

* **​What will we talk about? What's the topic?**

**​**The topic for the week is shared every Sunday in the Whatsapp group. We try to add the topic to the comment section on this event page as well but if you don’t see it, the topic and the jump-off points will be in our [WhatsApp group](https://tally.so/r/vGegL4).

* **Do I need to study or read anything to attend?**

No preparation is necessary. This isn’t a study of other thinkers. We want to know what YOU think about the topic, not what an old philosopher thought. If you have a perspective from a religion or a school of thought to share, throw it on the table and we’ll discuss it, but it's not required.

* **Is there a specific philosophy that Practical Philosophy is focused on?**

The goal of our meet-ups is to not have dogmatic discussions, and flex our critical thinking muscles. Because of this, we want to hear from every realm of thought, and don't study a particular school.

* **Is it mostly men that attend?**

Surprisingly, no! Although philosophy is seen as a Candelabra affair in a dark room with a bunch of guys, our Practical Philosophy meetups are generally 50/50 between genders, and we don’t meet in any dark rooms.

* **Can I come alone?**

Of course you can, we encourage it :)

* **Can I be late?**

Please don't be! We do have a 15 minute grace period where we allow people to funnel in while we hang out, but after that if you arrive too late, it disrupts the flow of the conversation.

* **Where do you meet?**

The location is posted in Meetup and our [WhatsApp group](https://tally.so/r/vGegL4) group for this location. It is HIGHLY RECOMMENDED that you join the [Whatsapp group](https://tally.so/r/vGegL4)! If you want to know what other cities have a Practical Philosophy Club, you can see our chapters on our [website](https://www.practicalphilosophy.club/).

🤝**POLICY**

To respect our members privacy, we ask that no one privately message a member without first getting explicit consent. (This looks like speaking in person and being asked to message privately.) Contacting or texting other participants without prior consent is not permitted and may result in removal from the group. Practical Philosophy reserves the right to enforce this policy at its discretion to maintain a safe and respectful environment.

**PRACTICAL PHILOSOPHY CLUB - Philosophy, everywhere. ✨**

We started in Practical Philosophy back in 2021 and there are now 50+ chapters in over 25 countries, including Canada, Spain, Japan, Mexico and many more! 🗺️
**\*\*\***
**Join our [WhatsApp group](https://tally.so/r/vGegL4) 👈**
**Follow us on [Instagram](https://www.instagram.com/practicalphilosophyclub/) 👈**
**Our [website](https://www.practicalphilosophy.club/) 👈**

event_title: Fremantle East Meditation Class
event_description:
Don’t miss out on these free, casual meditation classes! The meditation classes cater for people of all experience levels and are run in a relaxed environment. No stretches or physical exercises are involved in learning this simple yet effective technique.

**Effortless Meditation**
The word ‘Sahaja’ can be translated as ‘Spontaneous’ or ‘Simple’, and is at the outset a hypothesis that meditation can come easily for those who are willing to practice regularly (even briefly each day).
Sahaja Yoga defines meditation as ‘thoughtless awareness’, a state where you are completely conscious but at the same time completely without thought. In this state, we gain a unique perspective and insight into the beauty of our own innate nature.

Physical and mental health benefits have also been attributed to regular meditation practice through comprehensive Australian research (Learn more in the book ‘silence your mind’ or at www.beyondthemind.com).

Sahaja Yoga meditation was founded by HH Shri Mataji Nirmala Devi in 1970, and is based on kundalini awakening. Shri Mataji felt that meditation is an essential part of our spiritual growth. She felt that because meditation is so essential, a free and simple method to achieve it must be made available for everyone. Sahaja Yoga is now practiced in over 52 countries worldwide.

**Learn more here**
https://youtube.com/playlist?list=PLpn47Y9bqAkucSPCMYJqONCaytIUlvQcX

event_title: Raising Financially Confident Children
event_description:
**Duration:** 1 Hour \| **Format:** Virtual Interactive Session
Click Link to Register --> https://www.jotform.com/form/252052067992460
(Google link would be sent to registered participants 1 day before event)
**Hosted by:** Saravanan Mano, Founder of *Junior Investor Protocol* & CEO, *Global Edushift Pte. Ltd.*
**Description:**
In today’s fast-changing world, financial confidence is one of the most valuable life skills a child can develop — yet it’s rarely taught early enough. This engaging 1-hour session is designed for parents, educators, and mentors who want to help children build a lifelong foundation of financial wisdom, discipline, and confidence.
Join **Saravanan Mano**, an investment adviser with 15+ years of experience and creator of Singapore’s first structured financial literacy program for children — the *Junior Investor Protocol (JIP)* — as he shares practical tools, real-world examples, and engaging stories on how to make money concepts come alive for kids aged 6 to 16.
**In this session, you’ll learn:**

* How to raise children who are confident and responsible with money.
* The psychology of financial habits — and how they form in early years.
* Simple, actionable methods parents can use daily to teach saving, investing, and giving.
* How financial literacy builds not just wealth, but resilience and self-belief.
* Proven frameworks from the *Junior Investor Protocol* that families can apply immediately.

**Who should attend:**

* Parents who want to nurture financially independent children.
* Educators interested in integrating financial literacy into learning.
* Youth mentors and community leaders empowering the next generation.

**Takeaway:**
Every participant will receive a FREE *Family Money Confidence Starter Kit* — a practical guide to begin money conversations at home today.

event_title: Connect Your Body Language Through Movement Part 3
event_description:
**Connect Your Body Language through movement //**
**Build Confidence, Presence, and Authentic Connection**
**Part Three - Connect to the Movement Quality**

Welcome to our Thursday night online meetup, where we explore how we express ourselves in body language! Join us for a one-and-a-half-hour conscious movement session that beautifully weaves the principles of
movement therapy with insights from Rudolf Laban's philosophy.

At each event, we’ll explore and learn the movement qualities through engaging activities. Our sessions will include a warm-up to get our bodies flowing, an inspiring theme, and lively group discussions to share our experiences.

We’ll also incorporate journaling and grounding activities to help you reflect and connect with your feelings. This is a welcoming space for everyone to learn, grow, and connect through the power of movement.

**Small dedicated group of 8 as max.**
**Zoom Link will be shared in April.**

\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-

**Who is the facilitator?**

Chara Yang is a qualified movement therapist with the DTAA and a multidisciplinary dance artist whose work explores the intersection of body language, emotional literacy, and expressive movement.

She has facilitated movement experiences in both artistic and community contexts, supporting people from all backgrounds to explore the unspoken language of the body. Alongside her facilitation work, she actively performs a range of dance styles across diverse platforms.

Improv or Improvisation is exactly what it sounds like. A type of performance art that is created at the moment, spontaneously — on the fly — without any preparation at all.

event_title: Dating & Relationship Advice for Singles from a Psychologist
event_description:

**Dating & Relationship Advice — Live Interactive Session (Zoom)**
Are you tired of confusing dating experiences, mixed signals, emotional ups and downs, or repeated relationship patterns? This interactive session is designed to help you understand love, relationships, and yourself at a deeper level — in a simple, practical, and relatable way.
In this open session, you will learn how your emotional patterns, past experiences, and mindset influence the kind of relationships you attract — and how you can shift them to create healthier, more fulfilling connections.
**In this session, we will explore:**

* Why modern dating feels confusing and emotionally draining
* Common mistakes people make while choosing partners
* Understanding emotional availability and compatibility
* How past heartbreak affects present relationships
* Building self-worth, clarity, and confidence in love
* How to stop chasing and start attracting healthy love
* Practical relationship insights you can apply immediately

This will be a safe, open, and insightful space where you can reflect, learn, and gain clarity about your love life.
**Who should attend:**

* Singles navigating dating
* People healing from heartbreak or past relationships
* Anyone seeking clarity, emotional balance, and healthy love

**Format:** Live on Zoom \| Interactive \| Practical \| Insightful
**CTA:**
RSVP to reserve your spot and connect on WhatsApp for updates and joining details.

event_title: WEBINAR "Build Real-Time AI Agents with Context Engineering on Google Cloud"
event_description:
**Add to your calendar -** [https://hubs.ly/Q0469-0j0](https://hubs.ly/Q0469-0j0)

​AI agents are only as good as the data they can access, yet legacy architectures often fail to provide the real-time context agents require. This session explores context engineering: the foundation for ensuring data freshness and quality. We will demonstrate how to unify streaming and low-latency serving on Google Cloud using the Model Context Protocol (MCP) and Vertex AI.

​Learn how combining Confluent’s real-time data with Google Cloud enables agents to take accurate, autonomous action without the lag of traditional data stacks.

​**Speaker#1:** Dr. Sean Falconer is Confluent’s AI Entrepreneur in Residence, driving AI strategy and thought leadership. Sean has built and launched products that have impacted millions. As the founder of [Proven.com](https://proven.com/), he led engineering, product development, and go-to-market strategies, successfully guiding the company to acquisition. At Google, Sean worked on enhancing developer experience and advancing conversational AI technologies.

​He holds a Postdoc in Bioinformatics at Stanford University, where he collaborated with the World Health Organization to modernize the International Classification of Diseases.

## ​**Useful Links**

* ​​​**Free access to more talks/trainings:** [Ai+ Training platform](https://hubs.li/H0Zycsf0)
* ​​​**ODSC blog:** [https://opendatascience.com/](https://opendatascience.com/)
* ​​​**Slack Channel:** [https://hubs.li/Q03SyZP80](https://hubs.li/Q03SyZP80)
* ​​​**Code of conduct:** [https://odsc.ai/code-of-conduct/](https://odsc.ai/code-of-conduct/)

event_title: Introduction to AI and Machine Learning on Google Cloud (1 Day)
event_description:
**CloudAssist is Google Cloud Authorised Training Partner.**
This course introduces the AI and machine learning (ML) offerings on Google Cloud that build both predictive and generative AI projects. It explores the technologies, products, and tools available throughout the data-to-AI life cycle, encompassing AI foundations, development, and solutions. It aims to help data scientists, AI developers, and ML engineers enhance their skills and knowledge through engaging learning experiences and practical hands-on exercises.
**Objectives**

* Recognize the data-to-AI technologies and tools provided by Google Cloud
* Build generative AI projects by using Gemini multimodal, efficient prompts, and model tuning
* Explore various options for developing an AI project on Google Cloud
* Create an ML model from end to end by using Vertex AI

**Prerequisites**
Having one or more of the following:

* Basic knowledge of machine learning concepts
* Prior experience with programming languages such as SQL and Python

**Audience**
Professional AI developers, data scientists, and ML engineers who want to build predictive and generative AI projects on Google Cloud.

Pls register for this training below.

event_title: AIConnect: Global Virtual Job & Career Networking Event for AI Specialists
event_description:
Are you an AI engineer, data scientist, machine learning researcher, or aspiring innovator in the world of artificial intelligence? **AIConnect** is your dynamic **AI networking event online** designed to foster real-time conversations, career growth, and collaboration opportunities — all within a vibrant and self-led virtual space.

Whether you're exploring job roles in AI, hoping to demo your latest project, or searching for your next co-developer or data scientist partner, **AIConnect** offers a one-of-a-kind experience to connect with the global **machine learning community meetup** — anytime, from anywhere.

👉 [https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=bH3uNrkUTuw](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=bH3uNrkUTuw)

**💡 Why Join AIConnect?**

This is not just another virtual event. It’s a **collaboration platform for AI specialists** where you can grow your professional network, showcase your AI/ML portfolio, exchange ideas, and chat directly with fellow participants, recruiters, and service providers.
Designed for **AI career fair virtual** seekers and builders alike, the platform supports live 1-1 **video or text chats**, and offers open access to diverse community-led channels tailored to your interests.

**💬 Brainstorming & Discussion Channels Include:**

* **General** – Your go-to hub for announcements, orientation, and casual community-wide conversation.
* **Intros** – Introduce yourself, your research, your startup, or just share your journey in AI.
* **Networking** – Showcase your AI/ML projects, portfolios, startup pitches, or job aspirations.
* **Help Wanted** – Looking for team members, mentorship, freelance gigs, or offering support? Post here.

Whether you’re a beginner or a pro, a researcher or a recruiter — this space empowers growth through **project demos, career discussions, and research conversations.**

event_title: So You Think You Can Write
event_description:
It is a casual meetup for anyone who likes to write. We will meet and have a pleasant time together.
We are a mixed group in terms of experience, backgrounds, and languages, but we are also very supportive of each other in everything from goals to reading to publishing.
Please bring your writing piece (short story less than 2k words) or just come to discuss others' writing.
Inkwell is a creative non profit organization dedicated to connecting writers from around the world. Founded by Dr. Ryan Thorpe, based out of Shanghai, Inkwell has grown across China to start numerous workshops where writers are both challenged and supported to hone their craft. Inkwell offers workshops in fiction, reading, nonfiction, poetry, screenwriting, and translation as well as hosts writing retreats to bring together writers from across the globe. It also releases work through its literary journal, Visions, to help support new creative writing voices. Inkwell offers free reading and writing opportunities for both locals and expats at all levels of experience to foster a new generation of creative talent.

event_title: India Food Addicts in Recovery Anonymous
event_description:
**Having trouble controlling the way you eat?**

**Are you overweight? Underweight?**

**Obsessed with food, weight, or dieting?**

**You are not alone. There is a solution.**

**Food Addicts in Recovery Anonymous (FA)** is a program based on the **Twelve Steps of Alcoholics Anonymous (AA).**

There are **no dues, fees, or weigh-ins** at FA meetings.

FA is a **fellowship of individuals** who, through **shared experience and mutual support**, are recovering from the disease of **food addiction**.

FA members are **people of all ages**, from every part of the world.

FA includes people who were **morbidly obese**, **substantially underweight**, or even at a **normal weight**.

Regardless of their size, they were **tormented by cravings, dieting, bulimia, and/or an obsession with exercise**

event_title: Gokarna Beach Trek & Honnavar Tour | Plan The Unplanned
event_description:
Discover Gokarna — Coastal Bliss, Unplugged
Welcome to Gokarna, Karnataka’s sun-kissed seaside paradise. This small temple town has quietly become a backpacker’s haven, thanks to its stunning coastline, laid-back vibe, and hidden beaches.
Now picture this: you’re walking along the coast, looking out at endless blue waters meeting the sky, discovering beach after beach — each with its own charm. And while you're at it, you're making new friends, sharing stories, and creating memories that stay long after the trip ends.
**Why Travel With Plan The Unplanned?**
**1\. Premium Comfort**
AC dorms, private rooms, and comfy AC tempo travellers or mini-buses. Yes, this is an adventure — but without compromising on rest, hygiene, or travel quality.
**2\. Thoughtfully Curated Itinerary**
We don’t rush you through locations. From beaches and hidden waterfalls to quiet sunsets and boat rides, everything is set to offer the best vibe, not just photo ops.
**3\. The PTU Tribe Vibe**
It’s more than a trip — it’s a community. Whether you come solo or with friends, you’ll feel at home. You’ll laugh, bond, and probably plan your next trip together before this one ends.
Come for the Views. Stay for the Vibe.
From rock-lined beaches to the scent of coastal spice, from ancient ruins to playful waves — Gokarna has a little something for every kind of traveller. And with Plan The Unplanned, you won’t just see it — you’ll truly experience it. So whether it’s your first trek or your fifth, pack your bags and join us. You might just fall in love with the coast — or at least find a version of yourself that’s more alive than ever.
Who knows, this might just be the first of many.

## Trek highlights

* Depart from Bangalore at night and arrive at the scenic shores of Gokarna the next morning
* Begin your trek with a scenic trail covering Belekan Beach, Paradise Beach, Half Moon Beach, Om Beach, Dolphin’s Point, and Kudle Beach
* Spot dolphins at Belekan Beach
* Catch a magical sunset at Half Moon Beach
* Play a game of beach volleyball or shop for souvenirs at Om Beach
* Indulge in local cuisine at cafes on Kudle Beach
* Choose to explore the Gokarna town area on your own — temples, streets, and sea views await
* Prefer to relax? Chill at the campsite with your new tribe
* Enjoy a beachside dinner and sleep to the sound of waves under the stars
* On Day 2, continue the adventure — visit Apsarakonda Falls, explore the Honnavar Mangrove Boardwalk, Eco Beach, and enjoy a sunset boat ride on the Honnavar backwaters
* End the day with dinner and an overnight journey back to Bangalore

**Brief Itinerary**
Day 0
Depart from Bengaluru by 6:30 pm
Day 1
Arrive at the stay at 9:00 am

* Freshen up and breakfast
* Beach trek covering:
Paradise Beach, Half Moon, Om & Kudle Beach
* Return to stay by walk or auto (self-sponsored)
* Dinner
* Bonfire (subject to permissions)
* Overnight stay at the campsite
Day 2
Wake up early and freshen up
* Breakfast & checkout
* Visit Apsarakonda Falls in Honnavar
* Lunch ( Self-sponsored )
* Mangrove Board Walk
* Eco Beach
* Sunset boar ride at Honnavar
* Dinner en route (self-sponsored)
* Overnight drive to Bengaluru

Day 3 — Monday
Arrive in Bengaluru by approximately 6:00 am
**INCLUSIONS:**

* Round-trip via AC tempo traveller or minibus (based on group size) with push-back seats
Cosy shared accommodation in AC Dorms & Rooms
Separate zones for men and women
2× Breakfast • 1× Dinner
Entry fees & sunset boat ride
Trained outdoor leader from Plan The Unplanned & a local guide
The overall experience that you gain during the trek

**Exclusions**

* Any meals beyond those listed
Any kind of personal expenses
Auto in case of emergency or travel back to stay from Kudle Beach
Any kind of insurance (health, medical, life and accidental)
Anything not expressly mentioned in the inclusions

event_title: Coonoor Ooty Backpacking Trip from Bangalore
event_description:
**The Nilgiri Chronicles:** Where the Hills Slow You Down
Imagine this: it is an ordinary day in Bangalore, and you wake up to another day of work. But your friend calls you up and says that they have a surprise for you. They are taking you to the Nilgiris this weekend. Imagine yourself slipping away from the ordinary as your journey ascends into the Nilgiris, where the air turns crisp and alive with the invigorating scent of eucalyptus groves swaying like old friends in welcome. The chaos of the lowlands dissolves into a symphony of tumbling greens, inviting you into a world of unhurried bliss, crafted just for you by Plan the Unplanned.
This is how Ooty and Coonoor welcome you, the two hill towns that are more about feeling moments than checking off sights - misty mornings, never-ending tea gardens and views that stop you in your tracks without even trying.
**Ooty**: Living Above the Clouds
They say that Ooty is the Queen of Hill Stations, and it still bears a charm of another era, which juxtaposes with nature’s beauty on offer in the Western Ghats. The weather holds kind, the hills remain shrouded in mist, and the pace of life seems reassuringly unfast.
Tea gardens and estates as far as the eye can see colour the slopes in pale greens. For a high-altitude window on the world, climb Doddabetta Peak. From above, the hills unfurl in an endless sprawl to the horizon, and you remember that sometimes it is best just to stand still, take a deep breath in and let the view do the talking.
**Coonoor**: The Silent Heart of The Hills
If Ooty is regal and bustling, Coonoor is quiet and introspective. Its quiet roads, soft gradients and rich tradition of tea bestow upon this town its spirit. Time does seem to slow here, especially when you wander through tea estates and hillside trails that appear unspoiled by the rush.
The loveliness of Coonoor is an unassuming one, the sort that makes itself known in layers with every passing cloud and repositioned glint of sunlight.
**Itinerary**
Day 0 – Friday
‣ Depart from Bengaluru
Day 1 – Saturday

* Arrive at the homestay
*Note: Weekend check-in at Ooty stays is post 11:00 AM; washrooms will be available for freshening up*
* Freshen up & have breakfast
* Visit the Tea & Chocolate Factory for local tastings
* Head to Dolphin’s Nose viewpoint for stunning views of deep valleys and Catherine Falls *(viewed from the viewpoint)*
* Lunch at a local restaurant
* Enjoy the iconic Coonoor Toy Train ride and capture scenic moments
*Note: Toy Train tickets are subject to availability*
* Evening visit to Ooty Lake: Boating, cycling, horse riding or a relaxed lakeside walk
* Return to the stay and unwind
* Campfire evening with food, music & conversations *(subject to weather conditions)*
* Overnight stay with a peaceful night’s sleep

Day 2 – Sunday

* Wake up to a fresh mountain morning
* Breakfast at the stay
* Check out and head to the Pine Forest
* Walk amidst towering pine trees with stunning Nilgiri backdrops
* Lunch at a local restaurant
*Note: Options are limited; meals will be from available local eateries*
* Visit the famous shooting spot
* Head to the scenic Pykara Lake and relax by the lake or enjoy boating (optional)
* Evening departure towards Bangalore
* Arrival in Bangalore early morning (Monday)

\*All timings are estimates and subject to change
**Inclusion**

* Round-trip via non-AC tempo traveller or minibus (based on group size) with push-back seats

* Driver bata, interstate taxes, tolls & parking

event_title: Coorg Backpacking Trip
event_description:
Nestled in the Western Ghats, Coorg in Karnataka, also known as Kodagu, is a picturesque district often called the "Scotland of India." It’s where rolling coffee plantations, cascading waterfalls, and vibrant Kodava culture converge. We curate travel packages that make discovering Coorg feel less like checking off a tourist list and more like stepping into a living, breathing postcard.
What makes Coorg in Karnataka special is its laid-back vibe paired with an undertone of rustic luxury. Think dew-kissed mornings, rich filter coffee brewed from beans grown a few feet away, and impromptu bonfires under starry skies.
**Day 0**

* Depart from Bengaluru at 8:30 pm ( approx )

**Day 1**

* Reach Coorg in the morning, freshen up and have breakfast at the homestay
* 4×4 jeep ride to Mandalpatti viewpoint with panoramic hill views
* Lunch at a local restaurant in Madikeri
* Visit Abbey Falls surrounded by lush greenery
* Sunset experience at Raja’s Seat
* Explore Madikeri local markets for coffee, spices, honey, and tea
* Campfire evening with food, music, and conversations

**Day 2**

* Wake up to a relaxed morning at the homestay
* Breakfast and check-out
* Visit Harangi Elephant Camp (optional elephant feeding & water activities – self-sponsored)

event_title: BYOB & B*tch: The Friday Night Corporate Decompression Lounge
event_description:
### **(Banter, Venting, & Friday Night Digital Sanctuary)**

**Hosted by:** WHFL Bangalore (Wealth, Health, Friendships, Lifestyle)

**Dear Corporate Survivors,**

We know exactly how your week went. The "urgent" Friday 4 PM meetings, the passive-aggressive Slack pings, the "circular" discussions about KPIs, and the Bangalore traffic that turned your 20-minute commute into a 90-minute podcast marathon.

You don’t need another "networking" event. You don’t need to "optimize your synergy." **You just need to vent.**

On **Friday, 8th May**, we are opening the **Corporate Decompression Lounge**. It’s a 120-minute digital sanctuary where the masks come off, the mics stay unmuted, and the drinks are whatever you have in your fridge.

### **The Agenda: 2 Hours of Pure Catharsis**

We have zero keynotes and 100% honesty. This is a psychologically safe space to drop the "Professional Persona."

* **Date:** Friday, 8th May, 2026
* **Time:** 9:30 PM – 11:30 PM (The "Shut-Down-The-Laptop" Hour)
* **Format:** BYOB (Bring Your Own Beverage) Virtual Lounge
* **Investment:** **₹199** (Cheaper than a beer at a pub, 10x the fun)

**[9:30 PM – 9:50 PM] — The "Worst Meeting" Warm-up** We kick off with a rapid-fire round: Share the most ridiculous thing a manager or client said to you this week. We’re turning your workplace trauma into the night’s best entertainment.
**[9:50 PM – 10:45 PM] — Breakout Lounges: "Choose Your Industry"** We move into Zoom rooms curated by your specific corporate struggle:

* **The Dev Den:** For the software engineers dealing with "Legacy Code" and "Scope Creep."
* **The HR/Ops Hub:** For the people who spent the week managing everyone else's chaos.
* **The Marketing/Sales Suite:** For those tired of chasing leads and "pivoting" strategies.

**[10:45 PM – 11:15 PM] — The "Life-After-Login" Chat** We shift the conversation to the good stuff. What are you watching? Where are you traveling? What’s the one thing keeping you sane in Bangalore right now?
**[11:15 PM – 11:30 PM] — Closing Toasts & Weekend Launch** A final toast to surviving another week. Swap handles, make some real friends (not "connections"), and prepare for a weekend of zero notifications.

### **The Lounge Rules:**

1. **Strictly BYOB:** Whether it’s a cold beer, a glass of wine, or a strong ginger tea—bring it.
2. **No LinkedIn Posturing:** If you start talking about your "professional achievements," you’re buying the next round (metaphorically).
3. **Privacy First:** What happens in the Decompression Lounge stays in the Lounge.

### **Secure Your Spot**

We keep the groups small enough to ensure everyone actually gets to talk.
**Investment: ₹199 (All-Inclusive)**

**Buy your Ticket here: [https://forms.gle/47PZckZDJiKFtMQ8A](https://forms.gle/47PZckZDJiKFtMQ8A)**

*Stop staring at your Outlook. Start talking to humans. See you on the screen!*

Warmly,

Vishak (WhatsApp: +16722750721),
Founder - WHFL

event_title: Munnar Backpacking Trip
event_description:
Did you know that the name ‘Munnar’ comes from the two words ‘Munnu’ and ‘Aaru’, which mean ‘three rivers’ in the local languages Tamil and Malayalam? Munnar is a town that transports you to the old Bollywood charm of misty hills and undulating tea estates.
Once you enter the Idduki district in Kerala, where Munnar quietly sits, you will be greeted with a drive surrounded by the greenery and the smell of tea, umbrellad by omnipresent clouds. Munnar is not a town which you can leave, even after you have returned.

### **Trip Highlights**

* Scenic ghat drive into the misty hills of Munnar
* Stay amidst tea estates on the quieter outskirts
* Visit to iconic viewpoints surrounded by endless plantations
* Experience Kerala’s ancient martial art at a Kalaripayattu show
* Private trek to a secluded waterfall inside a tea estate
* Explore offbeat waterfalls and panoramic viewpoints
* Adventurous jeep ride to Kolukkumalai — the world’s highest tea estate
* Bonfire evening with food, music & new friendships

**Day 0 - Friday**
‣ Depart from Bengaluru at around 8:30 PM

**Day 1 – Saturday**
‣ Arrive in Munnar via scenic ghat roads
‣ Freshen up at the stay and store luggage
Note: Check-in is post 12:00 noon; washrooms will be available
‣ Breakfast
‣ Visit Photo Point overlooking lush tea plantations
‣ Explore Mattupetty Dam
‣ Lunch at a local offbeat restaurant (limited options available)
‣ Visit Echo Point for scenic views and photographs
‣ Experience the Kalaripayattu Cultural Show, Kerala’s ancient martial art
‣ Return to the stay
‣ Bonfire evening with new friends (subject to weather conditions)
**Day 2 – Sunday**
‣ Early morning wake-up
‣ Private trek to a secluded waterfall inside a tea estate
‣ Relax by the waterfall or take a dip
‣ Return to the stay to freshen up
‣ Heavy breakfast with traditional Munnar black tea
‣ Visit Periyakanal Waterfalls along the Munnar–Thekkady road
‣ Head to Lockhart Viewpoint for panoramic hill views
‣ Adventurous jeep ride to Kolukkumalai, the world’s highest tea estate
‣ Visit Jaguar Rock Viewpoint for photography
‣ Offbeat lunch stop
‣ Evening departure towards Bengaluru
‣ Arrival in Bengaluru early morning (Monday)
**Inclusion**

* Round-trip via AC tempo traveller or minibus (based on group size) with push-back seats

event_title: 🌎 (Online/Free) Free English Conversation Club with global people
event_description:
**📢IMPORTANT : CHECK THE SESSION TIME IN OUR WEBSITE(The time on the website and the meetup site may not match since we change it ocasionally)**

**Hi there! My name is Chloe.**
I'm a certified English teacher from United States.
I wanted to make an online language club where people can practice foreign languages by having real casual conversation!
I'm looking for people who want to practice conversational English by talking about interesting topics and hanging out with people from all over the world in a small online group.
**Here are some examples of what our weekly topics might be like:**
**"What would you do if you come across your crush?"**
**"Have you ever done the MBTI test? If so, what is your personality type?"**
**"Do you believe in love at first sight? Why or why not?"**
**"What are you most worried about right now?**
💎Here are some details💎
✔️ We meet via Zoom
✔️ The session will last for 1 hour and consists of 3 different rounds
✔️ Each round has different topics and discussion questions
✔️ Each round, the groups are randomly mixed, so you will have a chance to talk to different people every time
✔️ This isn't just about practicing English, but also about listening to different opinions and learning about different cultures
**✔️ The participation is FREE**
**❌ No credit card**
**❌ No Ads**

**📢IMPORTANT : CHECK THE SESSION TIME IN OUR WEBSITE(The time on the website and the meetup site may not match since we change it ocasionally)**

💎 How to Join 💎

1. [Click the link(here)](https://langclub.live/sessions)
2. [Sign up](https://langclub.live/signup)

3\. Click 'book' in the session that you want to participate\.
4\. Show up at the session time and click 'Join' in the session page
(The Zoom invitation will be sent to your e-mail once you apply too)
5\. Enjoy practicing English with people from all over the world\!

**📢IMPORTANT : CHECK THE SESSION TIME IN OUR WEBSITE(The time on the website and the meetup site may not match since we change it ocasionally)**

event_title: NEW TIME 7.00AM  START    South Perth, Narrows to Boorloo Bridge loop (10k)
event_description:
## Details

\*\*NOTE NEW START TIME 7.00 AM \*\*

Mornings are staying a bit darker so we will switch to autumn time.

A circular route taking in the South Perth and City foreshores between the Narrows and Boorloo bridges.
**Where**
Meeting near the Boatshed café and restaurant at the end of Coode Street. At the beginning of the jetty.
**Parking**
Carparks are nearby. Parking is free at the starting time of the morning but you pay for the next hour, ensure you collect a ticket and place it on your car dash.
**Distance**
10km and about 1hr 45min - 2hrs walking.
**Difficulty**
An easy paced walk but expect a decent pace and a good distance along paved paths sometimes shared with cyclists, kids and dogs. Kids and dogs are usually well behaved.
**What to bring**
Water, good walking shoes, hat & sunscreen
**Toilets**
At Betty’s Jetty and the café.
**Café after the walk**
Coffee and a snack at the Boatshed. We may need to sit outside if they are busy.
**AllTrails Map:** [Sth Perth Bridge to Bridge loop (10km)](https://www.alltrails.com/explore/map/south-perth-bridge-to-bridge-loop-9-7km-1f87a3f)
**RSVP - no shows & late arrivals:** If you are on the "Going" list and can't make it on the day please RSVP "Not Going" at the earliest convenience as a courtesy so as to allow others to join who might be on the "Waitlist".
*We aim to set-off for each walk at the advertised time and as a general rule, we set off on-time for each walk.*
**IMPORTANT**
By confirming your attendance attendees agree to "WAIVE, DISCHARGE CLAIMS, AND RELEASE FROM LIABILITY" the organisers, co-organisers, event organisers, volunteers and members of the *Walking Mates Australia* Meetup Group from any and all liability on account of, or in any way resulting from Injuries and Damages, even if caused by negligence, in any way connected with *Walking Mates Australia*.
