event_title: KL Ancient City Heritage Walkabout (1857-2026) Botanical Rainforests-Royal Club!
event_description:
**KL Ancient City Heritage Walkabout (1857-2026) Botanical Rainforests-Royal Club!**

[Meetup Location](https://maps.app.goo.gl/a6pGhhJjwxZZyFqP9)

Meet at the ABOVE coordinates SHARP 7: 30AM.

Distance 12 KM small hillocks & mostly undulating terrains, NO CLIMBS, Rain Forests with 4,000 Trees & a Botanic Park since 1890's!

Suitable for All Levels & above 10 years old Kids.

NO littering, plucking Plants, damaging anything at all only walk, take pictures, bring your own mobiles or camera. Be prepared for a 3-4 hours FULL mild Walkabout-Tons of Picture Taking Places/Opportunities.

ESSENTIALS:

1. MINIMUM 1 Liter Water or as Much as you need.
2. Dry Fit Wear & SUN Hats/ Glasses

3)Walking or Hiking Shoes/Sandals

4. Walking Stick if Required
5. Whistle
6. Mobile Phone
7. A healthy smiling attitude!- don't have?- you will Finish with one!

Walkabout History Since 1857

The founding of KL was almost an accident. In 1857, 87 Chinese prospectors in search of tin landed at the meeting point of the Klang and Gombak rivers and set up camp, naming the spot Kuala Lumpur, meaning ‘muddy confluence’.

KL botanical gardens, 160 acres was opened 13 May 1889, by Sir Frank Swettenham, 1st Resident Governor General of Federated Malay States

Now it's 3 to 4 times the size of Gasing, includes the Tugu, Bird, Butterfly & other Parks

Large variety of trees & plants from all over the world is still thriving now, some unique ones are Mahogany & Brazil Nut trees from the Amazon, 140 years old now & which lives up to a 1,000 years!

[Our Route Takes about 1 Hour of walking, However Picture Taking & Breaks makes it an hour More](https://maps.app.goo.gl/8HEcyeSKjVtJC18K6)

Our KL Heritage Hikes commences at the founding grounds, parks, sportings arena & buildings of the colonial masters, the British, since 1881.

1. Selangor Club 1884
2. Royal Lake Club
3. Dataran Merdeka
4. Railways 1886
5. Govt Printers 1890
6. Anglican Church 1894
7. Govt Buildings 1895
8. Courthouses 1896
9. Theatre 1900's
10. 1st Hotel 1910
11. Armory & Gurdwaras
12. National Monument
13. Parliament
14. KL Botanical Park 1889
15. Carcosa-Govt Generals Residence 1885
16. Birds Eye of Brickfields
17. Spice Gardens
18. Exotic Trees, Brazil Nut, Mahogany, Tembusu, Pulai, Eucalyptus, Baobab, African Sausage, Upside Down Bottle Tree
19. Tun Razak Memorial
20. Tanglin Hospital 1900
21. Methodist Girls School 1898
22. National Mosque
23. Chartered Bank 1857
24. Queen Victoria's Coronation Fountain
25. Ground ZERO Marker

I am attending the Events/Hikes/Tours at my own risk. I understand that the organizer, co-organizer and/or event hosts will try their best to assist and guide, but are no way responsible, liable or accountable for loss, damage, personal injury, death etc. By clicking the RSVP button, I acknowledge I have read, agreed and accepted the terms and conditions of this disclaimer.

event_title: AgentCamp 2026 Singapore
event_description:
AgentCamp 2026 is the flagship annual event of the Global AI Bootcamp, hosted in Singapore, dedicated to advancing the future of intelligent agents and enterprise AI. This year’s focus is on **autonomous agents, Copilot Studio, and the Azure AI Foundry ecosystem**, bringing together architects, developers, and strategists to explore how agentic AI is reshaping industries.
The event will showcase how **Copilot Studio empowers organizations to design, configure, and orchestrate custom copilots and agents** that seamlessly integrate into enterprise workflows. Combined with Azure AI Foundry, participants will gain hands-on experience in building, training, and deploying agents with governance, compliance, and lifecycle clarity.
Keynotes, workshops, and panel discussions will highlight best practices in multi-agent collaboration, operational scalability, and responsible AI adoption. Attendees will engage with real-world case studies, live demos, and community-driven innovations that demonstrate how agents and copilots can accelerate digital transformation.
AgentCamp 2026 is more than a conference—it is a collaborative platform where enterprise leaders, technologists, and AI enthusiasts converge to share insights, experiment with cutting-edge tools, and shape the next era of agent-driven solutions. Together, we will define how agents and copilots become the foundation of intelligent enterprises.

| Speaker Name | Topic | Time |
| ------------ | ----- | ---- |
| Rajesh Jayaraj | Keynote | 9.00 AM to 9.30 AM |
| Vinoth Selvam | AI Building AI: Creating a Copilot Studio Agent with Github Copilot and VS Code | 9.30 AM to 10.15 AM |
| Palash Roy | Building Intelligent Dataverse Agents with Custom AI | 10.15 AM to 11.00 AM |
| Asif Waqar | From Documents to Decisions: Multi-Agent RAG with Autonomous Workflows in Azure AI Foundry | 11.00 AM to 11.45 AM |
| Lunch Break | 11.45 AM to 12.30 PM |
| Senthamil Selvan | Teaching Agents to Use the Browser: Playwright as a Tooling Layer in MCP | 12.30 PM to 1.15 PM |
| Pavan Mani Deep Yaragini | Agentify Your Apps: Bringing Azure AI Foundry Agents into Power Apps | 1.15 PM to 2.00 PM |
| Abhilekh Verma | LLMs & Natual Language Processing - Unlocking the Future of AI Communication | 2.00 PM to 2.45 PM |

| Goloknath Mishra | MCP + Dataverse + Azure: Building unified Context Layer | 3.30 PM to 4.00 PM |

event_title: Build Your First AI Agent (No-Code Workshop to Automate Workflows)
event_description:
Most professionals are already using AI.
But very few are using it to actually reduce their workload.
Reports, follow-ups, summaries, repetitive tasks—these still take time.
What if you could automate a part of this in one session?

***

In this hands-on workshop, you’ll take a real workflow from your work and turn it into a simple AI-powered system.
You don’t need to code.
You just need to understand your workflow.

***

## What You’ll Do

You’ll break down a real task you handle regularly like reports, emails, or follow-ups—and map it into clear steps.
Using tools like Zapier, Make, or n8n, you’ll connect apps and build a working assistant.
You’ll plug in AI (Claude, Gemini, or similar) to summarise, classify, or generate outputs.
By the end of the session, you’ll have a simple system where input → processing → output is connected and working.

***

## What You’ll Walk Away With

A live AI-powered workflow you can use immediately
A clear understanding of what can (and cannot) be automated
A repeatable way to reduce manual effort in your work

***

## Who Should Attend

Professionals working in product, program, business, operations, or any role where repetitive work takes time and effort.

***

## What You Need

Your laptop
A simple workflow from your work (optional but recommended)

***

📅 Date: May 9
⏰ Time: 09:30 AM – 12:00 PM
📍 Location: IPL Campus, Koramangala, Bengaluru

***

If you’ve been exploring AI but haven’t yet used it to save real time, this session will help you get started.

**NOTE : This is a Paid Workshop**
**Price : Rs. 412**

🔗 **Trail Map:** [Causeway Bridge](https://www.alltrails.com/en-gb/trail/australia/western-australia/causeway-bridge-to-windan-bridge-via-swan-river?sh=gu0gil&utm_medium=trail_share&utm_source=alltrails_virality "https://www.alltrails.com/en-gb/trail/australia/western-australia/causeway-bridge-to-windan-bridge-via-swan-river?sh=gu0gil&utm_medium=trail_share&utm_source=alltrails_virality")
**🕗 Start Time:** 7:45 AM. Please arrive 15 minutes early at the hike/parking location (shared via a private Instagram group chat). This allows time for a quick check-in and ice breaker, and if available, a toilet break for an 8:00 AM start.
**🕚 End Time:** 11: 00 AM.
🚶‍♂️ **Pace/Difficulty:** Friendly and social / Easy.
⌛️ **Average Hike Duration:** 3 hours, depending on the group’s pace + including breaks to admire nature and a lunch break along the trail.
💬 **Community Chat Access:** You'll be added to our private Instagram group chat after booking! This is where we share final hike details, updates, and a fun space to connect before and after the hike.
**Please note:** Start location is not on Eventbrite (due to their software mapping limitation) - exact location to be shared on IG group chat.
👉 To make it easy for us to add you, please follow us on Instagram [@socialhikesau](https://www.instagram.com/socialhikesau/ "https://www.instagram.com/socialhikesau/") prior to booking.
\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-
*Looking to meet new people, explore nature, and have a good laugh along the way?*
This is your perfect Monday plan! 🎉
We’re excited to invite you to our upcoming WA Social Hike. A welcoming day out where the focus is all about fun, friendships and fresh air. Whether you come solo or bring a mate, you’ll leave with new connections and memories!
\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-
**🤩 Why Join?**
🌟 Make new friends in a relaxed, casual way.
🌟Enjoy nature and fresh air while meeting like-minded people your age
🌟Friendly vibes full of laughs, chats and good times guaranteed
🌟You never know. Today’s hiking buddy could be tomorrow’s uni mate, housemate, or travel buddy!
🌟Your safety is our priority. Equipped with a first aid kit on hand.
\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-
🎟️ **Tickets:**
**All tickets are $15 per person.**
This helps cover:

* Marketing and advertising to meet like-minded people and make friends
* Public liability insurance
* Experienced guides with a tour operator license who know the trails well
* First aid kit and safety equipment for the group

\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-
**🥾 What to Bring:**
👟 Comfy hiking shoes
💧 2–3L of water
🥪 Snacks/lunch
☀️ Sun protection (hat, sunnies, sunscreen)
🦟 Bug spray (always a good idea)
🎒 Optional: hiking poles, wet weather gear/raincoat
\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-
📌 **How It Works:**
After purchasing your ticket, you’ll receive a link to our private Instagram group chat.
All final details will be shared there: Hike route, meet-up location and any last-minute updates.
Friendly pace, friendly faces! This hike is intentionally slow–medium so everyone can enjoy and chat along the way.
\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-
**⚠️ Important:**
Must be 18+ years old to attend.
Hike at your own risk. You are responsible for your own safety and belongings.
Respectful, kind behaviour is a must. We are a positive and welcoming community.
Zero tolerance for any unwelcome behaviour. We’re here to have fun, together!
For this hike, we recommend that all participants download the AllTrails app before the event. This will help you:

* View the trail map in advance
* Track your progress during the hike
* Stay on course in case you get separated from the group

Having AllTrails ensures everyone can enjoy the hike safely and confidently.
\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-
**📜 Liability & Waiver**
By joining this hike, you acknowledge that participation is voluntary and at your own risk.
Social Hikes Australia is not liable for accidents, injuries, illnesses, theft, or transport-related issues.
🚑 If you have previous injuries, medical conditions, or health concerns, discuss with the organiser before purchasing your ticket.
Transport/carpooling is a community initiative only, not a service.
👉 Please make sure to read our [Terms & Conditions (click here)](https://drive.google.com/file/d/1tUzOL94hk5Uk5c7FOjiPRHXZz--5H9p5/view?usp=share_link "https://drive.google.com/file/d/1tUzOL94hk5Uk5c7FOjiPRHXZz--5H9p5/view?usp=share_link") before purchasing your ticket.
\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-
**Refund Policy:**
Tickets are non-refundable unless the hike is cancelled or rescheduled.
Tickets cannot be transferred to another hike unless agreed with the organisers.
\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-
**🌧 Rain Policy**
If the forecast shows over 15 mm in 6 hours or 20+ mm during the day, we may postpone or adjust the hike to keep things safe and enjoyable. Slippery trails, flooding, and poor visibility are no fun or safe.
We’ll also reschedule for thunderstorms or if trails are still soaked from heavy rain the days before.
Light rain (under 5 mm/hour) is usually fine. We’ll check each hike case-by-case. Safety first 🌿
\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-
**🌿 About Social Hikes Australia:**
Social Hikes Australia was founded in 2023 with a simple mission: to bring people together through the beauty of nature.
Since then, we’ve completed over 45 incredible hikes, from coastal trails to mountain summits, and we have explored the diverse landscapes that it has to offer, fostering connection, adventure, and a deep appreciation for the wild. 🥾🌿
We're excited to announce that fortnightly hikes have officially become part of our schedule! 🚀 Every two weeks, we'll be exploring new locations, creating more opportunities for you to connect, adventure, and be part of a growing, supportive community.
Whether you're an experienced hiker or just getting into the outdoors, Social Hikes Australia is a place where everyone is welcome. Come for the adventure, stay for the friendships. 🤝✨
👉 Follow us on Instagram for updates and upcoming hikes: [@socialhikesau](https://www.instagram.com/socialhikesau/ "https://www.instagram.com/socialhikesau/")
\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-
**💸 Where Your Contribution Goes:**
Advertising and outreach – helping us bring in new people to every event.
Public liability insurance – so we’re covered for safety and compliance.
First aid kit supplies – keeping our hikes safe and prepared.
Eco Park Pass
Tour Operator Licence.
Every ticket makes it possible to cover the essentials and keep creating safe, social, and welcoming outdoor adventures. 🌿
\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-
**📞 Organiser Contact:**
We highly encourage you to text our organiser team before purchasing your ticket if you have any concerns about transport:
Jacob 📱 [0404 870 123]() l Sebas 📱 [0435 993 237]()

Spots are limited, so grab your ticket now and let's hike, laugh and connect! 🌿🎉

event_title: Lakehouse at Scale: Doris x OLake
event_description:
**Lakehouse at Scale**
​Apache Iceberg adoption is accelerating, and with it come two operational realities data teams are running into head-on: table maintenance at scale and the demand for real-time, accurate retrieval powering AI systems.
​This meetup brings together practitioners and contributors working at both ends of that problem. Expect technical depth, real production context, and open discussion with engineers who are actively building and operating lakehouse infrastructure.
​**Agenda**
​**11:00 \- 11:30 \| Registration and Welcome**
​**11:30 \- 12:10 \| How Apache Doris Powers AI Agents with Hybrid Search and Real\-Time Analytics** *Matt Yi, Apache Doris PMC Member, Tech VP at VeloDB*

* ​Why single-method retrieval (vector-only or keyword-only) breaks down in production AI systems
* ​Hybrid search architecture: combining vector search, full-text search, and SQL for accurate, intent-aware retrieval
* ​How Apache Doris's native real-time OLAP capability extends into real-time RAG pipelines
* ​Cost and accuracy tradeoffs across retrieval strategies and what that means for context engineering at scale

​**12:10 \- 12:50 \| OLake Fusion: Solving Apache Iceberg Table Maintenance Problems at High Scale** *Ankit Sharma, Tech Lead + Badal Prasad Singh, Software Engineer, OLake*

* ​Why continuous CDC ingestion at scale creates small file accumulation and query performance degradation in Iceberg tables
* ​Compaction strategies (lite, medium, full) and how to choose the right mode based on workload and file size targets
* ​Cron-based scheduling, table enable/disable controls via Helm and Docker Compose
* ​Multi-catalog support and lessons from building maintenance systems that do not interrupt live ingestion

​**12:50 \- 1:00 \| Break**
​**1:00 \- 1:30 \| Apache Doris User Sharing** *Nilanjan Sarkar*

* ​Production experience taking Apache Doris from evaluation to live deployment
* ​Practical challenges and decisions made along the way

​**1:30 \| Lunch and Networking**

event_title: Lakehouse at Scale: Apache Doris × OLake
event_description:
Apache Iceberg adoption is accelerating, and with it come two operational realities data teams are running into head-on: table maintenance at scale and the demand for real-time, accurate retrieval powering AI systems.

This meetup brings together practitioners and contributors working at both ends of that problem. Expect technical depth, real production context, and open discussion with engineers who are actively building and operating lakehouse infrastructure.

**Location**
**Hustlehub Tech Park Building**
PWD Quarters, 1st Sector, HSR Layout, Bengaluru, Karnataka 560102, India
https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=Hustlehub%20Tech%20Park%20Building&query_place_id=ChIJ5VA9O6AUrjsRuOa1I1km1Fk

**Agenda**

* 11:00 - 11:30 Welcome!
* 11:30 - 12:10 **How Apache Doris Powers AI Agents with Hybrid Search and Real-Time Analytics** by Matt Yi, Apache Doris PMC Member, Tech VP at VeloDB
* 12:10 - 12:50 **OLake Fusion: Solving Apache Iceberg Table Maintenance Problems at High Scale** by Ankit Sharma, Tech Lead & Badal Prasad Singh, Software Engineer, OLake
* 12:50 - 13:00 Break
* 13:00 - 13:30 **Apache Doris User Sharing** by Nilanjan Sarkar
* 13:30 Lunch

**Session Briefs**

Session 1:
**How Apache Doris Powers AI Agents with Hybrid Search and Real-Time Analytics**
This talk will introduce hybrid search with Apache Doris as a next-generation retrieval solution for generative AI and context engineering. Addressing the semantic confusion problem by combining vector search, full-text search, and SQL to capture the exact-match intent with semantic similarity, resulting in a more accurate and cost-effective solution. Matt will then touch on how the native real-time capability that Apache Doris brings to OLAP can be extended to real-time RAG, helping organizations think about future challenges in this space.

Speaker Bio:
[Matt Yi, Apache Doris PMC Member, Tech VP at VeloDB](https://www.linkedin.com/in/guolei-yi-76350718/)
As Tech VP at VeloDB and an Apache Doris PMC Member, Matt Yi brings over 10 years of expertise in database kernel R&D and technical management. He has spearheaded multiple major version iterations of Apache Doris, driving significant performance breakthroughs across diverse analytical scenarios. As a core driver of the community, Matt has witnessed and propelled the evolution of Apache Doris from a technical project into one of the most active and influential open-source communities in the global big data and database landscape.

Session 2:
**OLake Fusion: Solving Apache Iceberg Table Maintenance Problems at High Scale**
Ankit Sharma, Tech Lead
Badal Prasad Singh, Software Engineer, OLake
This talk will address how continuous CDC ingestion can lead to small file accumulation and query performance degradation. The speakers will explore various compaction strategies (lite, medium, and full) and provide guidance on selecting the optimal mode based on specific workloads and file size targets. They will also cover practical implementation via Cron-based scheduling and orchestration using Helm and Docker Compose, while sharing key insights into building multi-catalog maintenance systems that ensure live ingestion remains uninterrupted.

Session 3:
**Apache Doris User Sharing**
Nilanjan Sarkar

Meet Nilanjan, an experienced Data Engineer who specializes in enterprise-grade data solutions and is a familiar, supportive face in the Apache Doris community. Over the last year, Nilanjan has gone from exploring the potential of Apache Doris to deploying it in production. We are thrilled to have him share his unique journey, offering a deep dive into his technical experiences and the milestones of bringing Apache Doris to life in a real-world enterprise setting.

event_title: In Person - PUBLIC SPEAKING/LEADERSHIP WORKSHOP - TalkMagic Toastmasters
event_description:
Contact Kailash@ +91 99452 79671 / Manish +91 95943 83692

* Guaranteed Edutainment for 2 Hours - Prepared speeches, Table Topics, Evaluation
* Snacks Served After the Workshop
* Guests (Non Members) will be charged 100 Rs/entry

About Toastmasters
Toastmasters International is a not-for-profit educational organization whose mission is to help people develop their communication skills. There are no instructors or professors and there are no tests or grading. A Toastmasters Club consists of 10 to 20 people who meet weekly throughout the year. Typically, the agenda of the club meeting has 3 major sections:

1. Prepared Speeches
The club member comes prepared to deliver his speech. This is a project speech & is towards certification.
The best speaker is chosen by the audience. 2. Speech Evaluation
An evaluator is assigned to each speaker & a 2 - 3 minute evaluation on what went well & areas of improvement is shared in a constructive manner.
2. Table Topics
This is an extempore speaking round. The table topic master will come prepared with 5-6 topics. Volunteers from the audience will come up & speak on the topic for 2-3 minutes.

Every meeting has the above sessions, as well as role players such as Grammarian, Timer, Ah-counter & General Evaluator.The members take turns to perform each of these roles which helps them enhance Leadership skills.

As a result of joining Toastmasters and actively taking part, you will: Learn to communicate more effectively.
Become a better listener.
Improve your presentation skills.
Increase your leadership potential.
Become more successful in your career.
Build your ability to motivate and persuade
Reach your professional and personal goals.
Increase your self confidence.

event_title: The Art of Tarot Spreads: From Tradition to Intuition by Nichola Snook-Shelton
event_description:
**The Art of Tarot Spreads: From Tradition to Intuition**
**Presented by Nichola Snook-Shelton**

During this interactive meeting, not only will we explore the various types tarot spreads, the category or style they fall into, their history and when you may need to create your own spread. We also have a sneak peek into Tarot Reversals and the implications of having to reverse a card in a tarot spread. The reversals are predominantly for the Rider Waite Smith based decks; Thoth and Marseilles have different views and structures when it comes to reversals.
There will be a variety of spreads available, attendees can work in groups, pairs or solo. After this we will share any insights we may have identified.
**Bio of Presenter:** Many of the members in Western Australia will know me as the 'The Tarot Lady' or 'Tarot Queen.' This developed with the Western Australian Tarot Conference where, they asked for someone to sell tarot cards. Enquiries made and 'twas arranged, what I do "differently" is offer decks that are not readily available in shops and to ensure there is product knowledge. This is very important as people need to know whether it is a beginners deck, what style of deck, does it have pip cards; this information allows people to select the appropriate deck for them.
When people started to say oh I have so many decks, I don't use them, we aren't connected, Nichola came up with the Wrap and Read and started looking into Tarot accessories to allow people to use the decks they had.
This then made a change of name to Tarot Connections.

**Socials:** [https://www.facebook.com/nichola.snookshelton/](https://www.facebook.com/nichola.snookshelton/)
**Email:** [intermezzo.no1@gmail.com](mailto:intermezzo.no1@gmail.com)

Pay cash or card at the door: Free for Professional Members, $10 for Interest members, $20 for non-members

event_title: Weekly Sharing Circle
event_description:
## **Group Structure**

* **Led by a therapist**
* **Small Cohort \| 6\-8 participants**
* **Runs Weekly on Saturday\| 75 minutes**

**Register at :** https://www.soulup.in/products/weekly-sharing-circle

## What we will explore in this session:

This circle provides a safe and gentle space to openly share what’s been on your mind—stress, frustration, confusion, or any emotions you’ve been carrying. Guided by a therapist, you’ll have time to speak without judgment, reflect on your feelings through simple prompts or activities, and gain clarity about your emotional state. It’s a space designed to help you feel seen, supported, and understood by both the facilitator and other participants

Through sharing and reflection, the circle also fosters connection and belonging, allowing you to hear others’ experiences and realise you’re not alone. By the end of the session, you’ll walk away with a sense of relief, grounding, and emotional ease, having practiced self-compassion, strengthened your emotional tools, and left the week feeling a little lighter and more connected to yourself and others.

## **Who is the circle for?**

This circle is for anyone who wants a safe, comforting space to slow down and process their week. Whether you’re feeling overwhelmed, confused, lonely, or simply in need of a pause, this session offers honest sharing, gentle guidance, and meaningful connection in a small community.

**Register here:** https://www.soulup.in/products/weekly-sharing-circle

event_title: Navigating Arranged Marriage Process
event_description:
**Group Structure:**

* **Runs weekly on every Saturday\| Every Saturday at 12:00 PM IST \| 75 minutes**
* **Confidential \| Small Group\, 6–8 Participants**
* **Led by Therapist**

**Register at:** https://www.soulup.in/products/support-groups-navigating-the-process-of-arranged-marriage

**A safe and reflective space to explore the arranged marriage process, share concerns, and make sense of emotional and family pressures while searching for a partner.**

### **Goals of the Group**

1. **Unpack Personal Doubts & Expectations**
Explore fantasies, fears, and internal narratives around love and partnership.
2. **Understand Family Influence**
Reflect on how family systems shape decisions, expectations, and roles.
3. **Navigate Pressure & Rejection**
Discuss emotional investment, rejection cycles, and boundaries in the process.
4. **Clarify Needs & Values**
Revisit your definition of compatibility, connection, and commitment.
5. **Build Perspective & Confidence**
Engage with others in similar journeys and gain tools to navigate with clarity.

***

### **Who is the Group For?**

This group is for anyone actively exploring or navigating arranged marriage and feeling confused, overwhelmed, or unsure. Whether you're dealing with family pressure, emotional uncertainty, or decision fatigue, this group offers peer connection, validation, and therapist-guided reflection.

**Register at:** https://www.soulup.in/products/support-groups-navigating-the-process-of-arranged-marriage

event_title: Coffee, Cake and Chats
event_description:
Come on down to San Churros for a simple coffee and a some interesting conversations with some like minded individuals. If you feel like a round of pool afterwards we can go next door to pot black for a few games.

We keep this Meetup small—just 7 attendees—so everyone has the chance to connect, build rapport, and get to know each other in a relaxed, welcoming setting.

event_title: Para Dance Saturday
event_description:

Join us for a fun, inclusive dance class designed specifically for wheelchair users, led by accredited DanceSport coaches.

event_title: Masked: The Unexpressed Side of Us
event_description:
**Masked: The Unexpressed side of us.**
*A session to stay with what we feel… but don’t show*
*We all wear masks.*
*Not to deceive but to protect, to belong, to cope.*

*Behind these masks live emotions we’ve never fully expressed—*
*unsaid words, hidden needs, quiet disappointments, and even unshared love.*

*Over time, what remains unexpressed doesn’t disappear…*
*it shapes how we think, react, connect, and experience life.*

*This workshop is a safe and reflective space to explore:*
*The impact of suppressed emotions on your inner and outer life*
*The needs and desires you’ve learned to silence*

*How unexpressed feelings show up in relationships, stress, and behavior*
*The gap between who you are and what you show*
*Through guided reflections, gentle exercises, and open conversation, you will:*

*Become aware of what you’ve been holding within*

*Understand your emotional patterns without judgment*

*Begin to express your truth in a way that feels safe and authentic*

*Reconnect with yourself beyond the mask*

*It’s about understanding why we live with mask and unknowingly exists unwanted … and choosing when to lower it.*
*\- Come with curiosity*
*\- Share only if you feel safe*
*\- Or simply listen and reflect*
*Because sometimes, the parts of you you’ve hidden the most…*
*are the ones waiting to be seen.*

event_title: Business & Startup Solutions Daily Workshop
event_description:
Join us for a weekly workshop where we dive deep into practical business solutions and strategies to boost your success!
🚀 Business Solution Weekly Workshop (Online)

This is NOT a webinar or a motivational session.

This is a working session for founders, co-founders & decision-makers & business owners who have real problems and want real solutions.

🎯 What makes this workshop different?

✅ Problem-first (not theory-first)
✅ Stage-aware (advice based on where your business actually is)
✅ Live problem-solving (not generic slides)
✅ Clear action plan by the end of the session

🧠 Who should attend?

Any business from any industry, founders, co-founders & decision-makers facing challenges in their business.

Founders, co-founders & decision-makers from:

Startups (Idea → Scale)

MSMEs & SMEs

D2C, B2B, SaaS, Services

Agencies, Consulting, CA, Legal, Manufacturers, etc

Tech, Non-tech, Traditional businesses

Solo founders & early-stage teams

🗂️ What we cover (Agenda highlights)

Identifying your real business bottleneck

Idea → Validation → MVP → PMF → Growth → Scale

Sales, revenue & GTM clarity

Cash flow & survival planning

Team, execution & founder bandwidth

Live founder problem-solving clinic

30–60–90 day action roadmap

🧩 Format

🖥️ Online
🗓️ Weekly
👥 Small breakout rooms (stage & problem-based)
🎤 Optional live problem discussion

event_title: Crazy Catan at Mt Claremont
event_description:
On Saturday 9 May 2026 you can play Crazy Catan in the Hakea Room at Mt Claremont.

It looks like Catan but it plays nothing like it - it's fast, noisy and a little bit unhinged!.

😎 Each player will have a list of Catan objectives they have to complete during the game (e.g. collect a brick, build a settlement, play a development card...).
😎 Meet the objective, get it signed off and start working on the next one.
😎 You'll need to work fast because you will have a limited time at one Catan island before you have to move onto the next one.
😎 Everything you have built will be left behind when you leave!

You will need to know how to play Catan and you can sign up for the craziness [here!](https://boardgamegeek.com/thread/3690396/crazy-catan-at-mt-claremont-in-may)

event_title: Morley Afternoon Tea
event_description:

event_title: Card Making
event_description:
In a fast-paced digital world, this is an opportunity to slow down, get creative, and make something meaningful by hand. Join a small, relaxed group to create 2–4 handmade cards that you can take away and send to people you care about.
This is a **self-guided** creative session and is suitable for all skill levels - no experience needed, just a willingness to get creative!

### **What’s Provided**

Basic card making materials will be supplied (thank you to the Rotary Club Northbridge for funding this activity), including:

* Blank cards
* Glue & scissors
* Coloured and patterned paper
* Lettering supplies
* Stickers and decorative elements

You’re also very welcome to bring your own materials if you’d like to personalise your creations further.

### **Before You Come**

To make the most of the session, you’re encouraged to look up ideas or inspiration beforehand, as the activity is **self-guided**.

### **Optional Activity: Send a Thank You**

You’re invited to create a thank you card for someone who has made a difference in your life.
If you finish and write a message in at least one card during the session a postage stamp will be provided so you can send your card straight away.

### **What to Expect**

* A calm, welcoming atmosphere within a small group
* Materials laid out and ready for you
* Time and space to unwind, slow down and be creative
* Please make a purchase from the cafe
* Leave with 2-4 cards you can use to give to people as occasions come up

Thank you to the Rotary Club Northbridge for funding this event.

event_title: ICIT Talk
event_description:
Do you have any emotions or behaviour patterns? Relationship issues? Fears or phobias? Unexplainable physical issues? Limiting belief systems or unexplained emotional reactions? The root cause may be your inner child(ren).

It could be that the feelings, perceptions and beliefs you have learnt or accepted as a child may now be subconsciously running your life.

Inner child work is about recovering yourself, it’s about freedom, it’s about all that you might have been and now can be. Integration Therapy is about becoming whole, finding inner harmony and balance. We are no longer at war with ourselves, reacting to old programs, old belief systems and old coping structures.

Are you a Healing Practitioner or a Therapist ?

* looking for a powerful healing tool or skill sets to deepen your practice and take you to a next level ?
* looking for advanced techniques that work with deeper issues such as trauma, dissociative disorder, abuse, archetypal distortion, shadow work etc?

Or are you an individual looking for deeper healing ?

* looking to unlock childhood experiences that have affected your behaviour and kept you feeling trapped physically, mentally, emotionally and spiritually ?
* struggling with the disharmonious parts of yourself and trying to rediscover the hidden part of yourself ?

Whether you are a therapist seeking to enhance your skills or individuals who are seeking to rediscover and heal the hidden part of yourself. Inner Child work offers integration that allows the therapist to move a client or yourself to explore deeper parts of the psyche.

Join us for an introductory talk to find out how you can deeply heal your own life or the life of your clients via Inner Child Integration Therapy. We will give you more information about how to explore Inner Child Therapy as a client for your own healing or become a certified Inner Child Regression Therapist.

This talk will cover the following topics :

* Who is my Inner Child? How do I get in touch with my inner child?
* How does going to my childhood help my current life issue?
* What is Inner Child Integration Therapy (ICIT)?
* Understanding how Inner Child Therapy can resolve physical, emotional, mental and spiritual challenges.
* One free guided healing inner child group therapy to get you started on your personal healing journey

This is the perfect preview for the Inner Child Workshops. For more information, click here : https://redefinewellness.asia/inner-child-integration-therapy/

Talk Schedule (Singapore Time) :

Timing : Saturday @ 2pm - 3.30pm

Tickets : Free

Registration : RSVP with your email and mobile to receive Zoom link

For any additional questions or information, please contact Shirley at +65-91992684 shirley.tay@redefinewellness.asia or Pooja at +65-97276181 pooja.arora@redefinewellness.asia

For more details and information on workshops and classes schedule, please visit our website : https://redefinewellness.asia/view-schedule/

By registering for this event, you give ReDefine Wellness permission to reach out to you regarding this and similar events. You can always request us not to contact you or unsubscribe from the newsletter if you so wish to do so.

event_title: Discover why it's not hard to become a skilled public speaker. You'll have fun!

Come and visit and see for yourself how we transform our members into skilled public speakers. If nothing else you will get an afternoon of free entertainment.

It will be at the Tom Dadour Centre, 363 Bagot Rd Subiaco, near the King Edward Hospital. Allow a few minutes for parking. Please arrive by 2.20pm for a 2.30 start. We start exactly on time and we need a few minutes to welcome you. The on-street parking is free but allow a few minutes for parking because of the proximity of the hospital.

We hope to meet you then.

event_title: PERTH GAY YOGA
event_description:
This is our now WEEKLY Saturday yoga class with an open invitation to join some of the guys afterwards for some social interaction.

We offer a playful, supportive and safe space for other gay men to meet and make friends. There is a real sense of community as everyone is very welcoming and friendly to each other. At the same, time taking care of your overall health and wellbeing through yoga/meditation/relaxation.

No experienced required the class is suitable for all levels including beginners and the highly experienced! ( Beginners, Bi, Trans man & Non-binary persons welcome )

BYO Mat, Water & Towel... Mats also available at the studio if you don't have one yet. Plenty of free parking available! Arrive around 5-10mins before class to set up.

You do not need to RSVP if you are not comfortable doing so and can just come along to the studio before the class starts and we will be able to fit you in and we will always respect your privacy.

$18/class or 10 week course/pass $150 ( Does not expire, use anytime )

INTRODUCTORY OFFER FOR NEW STUDENTS AND DISABILITY/CONCESSION RATE IS 10 SESSIONS FOR $99

*Class costs goes towards studio hire, guest instructors, special events and Meetup membership charges.*

event_title: The Long Table No. 1 — Borders & Belonging
event_description:
# **The Long Table No. 1 — Borders & Belonging**

I’m hosting a small supper at my place — a shared table for people who enjoy food, conversation, and being present.

This first edition is shaped by dishes I grew up with, and those I discovered while moving between places — where flavours carry memory, identity, and story.

It’s a slow evening.
We sit together, eat in courses, and let the night unfold around one table.
Not just dinner — but a space to arrive, settle, and connect.

## **About the Host**

Hi, I’m Hesti.

I grew up in Indonesia and have spent the past few years living across cultures — something that naturally shapes the way I cook.

Some dishes come from memory, some from people I’ve met along the way, and some from curiosity.

I’m also the creator of *Tahdig Bites* — a concept built around tahdig, the crispy Persian rice, shaped and filled with classic Persian stews. I’ve been running it through pop-ups in Kuala Lumpur.

This supper is a more personal extension of that — not takeaway, but a table to sit, share, and experience the food differently.

## **What is The Long Table Club?**

It’s a shared table.

A mix of strangers and familiar faces, gathered for the evening — open to conversation, connection, and the experience of being present with others.

Somewhere between a dinner at a friend’s home and a cultural exchange.
You don’t just come for the food — you come to be part of the table.

## **What to Expect**

• A multi-course menu, served throughout the evening
• 8–10 guests seated together at one table
• Shared, family-style dining
• Dishes introduced with context and story
• A slow, unhurried evening — no fixed end

The menu is prepared using halal ingredients, and vegetarian preferences can be accommodated.

Come as you are.

# **The Menu — Borders & Belonging**

This menu brings together flavours that have moved across places and stayed with me in different ways.

## 🥂 **Arrival — A First Crossing**

**Bir Pletok**
A traditional Betawi spiced drink — warm, aromatic, and rooted in history

## 🍽️ **To Begin — Shared Origins**

**Dadami Dip** — herb & labneh dip inspired by family recipes from northern Iran
**Kuku Sabzi Wrap** — aromatic herb frittata with tomato
**Noon Lavash** — Persian flatbread, made to tear and share

## 🍲 **Main — Comfort & Memory**

**Ghormeh Sabzi** — slow-cooked Persian beef herb stew
**Potato Tahdig** — saffron rice over golden potatoes, crisped into a caramelised crust
**Khiar Panir** — cucumber, feta, herbs, pomegranate & toasted almonds
**Olivieh** — Persian-style potato salad

## 🍃 **Between — From My Side**

**Risol Pisang Ijo Makassar**
A warm reinterpretation of a classic — pandan crepe, caramelised banana, rose coconut custard

## 🍰 **To End — A Gentle Return**

**Peach, Cardamom & Saffron Cake**
Soft, fragrant, lightly floral — finished with rose petals

## 🍵 **After — Lingering Conversations**

**Chai e Zafaran with Nabat**
Saffron tea with Persian rock sugar

## **Seats**

I’m keeping this small — just 8–10 seats — so it stays personal for everyone at the table.

**RM135 per seat**
(includes the full multi-course experience)

## **How to Join**

You’re welcome to mark yourself as “Going” here on Meetup — it helps me get a sense of interest and shape the table.

However, seats are confirmed through registration here:
👉 [https://luma.com/gb4d00eb](https://luma.com/gb4d00eb)

## **Location**

Kuala Lumpur
(Exact location shared after confirmation)

event_title: De-mystifying PyTorch for ASICs: When to Move Your Dev to AI Accelerators
event_description:
✨ **We’re bringing a PyTorch Conference Europe 2026 talk to the DEP AI Study Group!**

We’re honored to feature a session presented at the **PyTorch Conference Europe 2026: A Landmark Moment for Open Source AI**, held in Paris last April 15. Now, we’re sharing it with the DEP community.

**De-mystifying PyTorch for ASICs: When (and Why) to Move Your Development to AI Accelerators**
GPU availability and rising costs are pushing ML teams to explore alternatives like Google TPUs and AWS Trainium. But is the shift worth it? This session provides a practical, code-first reality check on migrating PyTorch workloads to ASICs.

🔍 **What to expect:**
• Breakdown of compiler stacks: PyTorch/XLA (TPU) and TorchNeuron (Trainium)
• Understanding the “Compiler Tax” developers often encounter
• Side-by-side code comparisons and benchmarks using Llama 4, Gemma 3, Qwen 3, CNNs, and ViTs

💡 **Key questions we will answer:**

1. How much rewriting is actually needed?
2. Which models perform well on ASICs, and which struggle with dynamic shapes?
3. What happens when debugging issues arise such as OOM or compilation hangs?

📊 Leave with a **Migration Decision Matrix** to help determine if your workload is ready for the ASIC leap.

👨‍💻 **Speaker**
**Alpha Romer Coma**
Multimodal AI Researcher \| Cloud @ Kollab \| 4x Microsoft\, 4x Google\, 2x AWS Certified

📅 **May 9, 2026**
⏰ **7:00 PM – 8:30 PM**
📍 **DEP Discord**
See you there! 💻🔥

event_title: Masterclass: Build a Real Backend System in Golang (Live Project)
event_description:
🚀 **CoderRange Production Engineering Lab**
**Masterclass: Build a Real Backend System in Golang (Live Project)**
This is not a theory session. This is a **live engineering lab** where you will build a real-world backend system used in production.

***

## 💡 Real-World Use Case

How does a system like SMS/WhatsApp delivery platforms (Complex systems) handle millions of messages reliably?
In this session, we will build a **Delivery Event Processing System**:
👉 User sends a message → API receives request
👉 Message goes to Queue (async processing)
👉 Worker processes with concurrency
👉 Data stored in Redis/DB
👉 Reporting API fetches conversation

***

## 🛠️ What You Will Build (Live in 2.5 Hours)

* REST API (Producer Service)
* Event-driven architecture (Queue-based system)
* Concurrent message processor (Goroutines)
* Storage layer (Redis / in-memory)
* Reporting API for message tracking

***

## 🧠 What You Will Learn

* How **real backend systems are designed (HLD)**
* Writing **production-level Golang code**
* **Concurrency & async processing** (critical for interviews)
* **Scaling & failure handling strategies**
* How to answer **backend/system design interview questions**

***

## 🔗 Live Project (Practice After Session)

GitHub Repo:
[https://github.com/venkatesh-db/karix-GolangDelivery](https://github.com/venkatesh-db/karix-GolangDelivery)

***

## 🎯 Who Should Attend?

* Backend Developers (2–10 years experience)
* Engineers preparing for **product companies**
* Anyone struggling with **System Design / LLD / Concurrency**

***

## ⏰ Duration

Join Zoom Meeting

[https://us02web.zoom.us/j/86936940412?pwd=OXAVWKeEJiNHAqilsWsmr9SeI7WHhg.1](https://www.google.com/url?q=https://us02web.zoom.us/j/86936940412?pwd%3DOXAVWKeEJiNHAqilsWsmr9SeI7WHhg.1&sa=D&source=calendar&usd=2&usg=AOvVaw3OLM5N8gdmfvsycaMBqXyv)

Meeting ID: 869 3694 0412
Passcode: 603247

2.5 Hours (Hands-on + Live Coding + Q&A)

***

## 💼 Outcome

By the end of this session, you will:

* Understand how **production systems actually work**
* Be able to explain system design in interviews
* Gain **hands-on backend experience (not just theory)**

***

## 🚀 Final Note

This is a **practical, no-fluff session** designed to give you real industry exposure.
Limited seats to ensure **interactive learning**.
👉 Not a webinar. This is a **live engineering lab**.

event_description:
**No Partner Needed: Beginner West Coast Swing Series \+ Guided Practice \| KL \(May 2026\)**

📍 Swango Dance Studio, Damansara Perdana
Want a fun midweek reset that helps you meet new people *and* learn a new skill — without needing a partner or experience? This is for you. 🙂

✅ **Only 9 spots left (we usually sell out).**

**To RSVP / join:** **WhatsApp us “May WCS”** and we’ll reply with the full steps (how to secure your spot, what to expect, what to wear, and how the night works). We’ll guide you through it.
[https://api.whatsapp.com/send?phone=601111023340](https://api.whatsapp.com/send?phone=601111023340)

**What is West Coast Swing?**
A modern partner dance you can do to **almost any music** — pop, R&B, acoustic, chill hits, radio songs.
(So you’re not stuck dancing to only one genre.)
It’s social, beginner-friendly, and one of the easiest ways to find a real community in KL.

**Why we don’t do free trial participation (but you can vibe-check)**
A lot of dance scenes offer free trials — but it often becomes a revolving door of people “just trying once.”

We keep our classes **capped and beginner-supported**, so first-timers get real attention and a consistent group to learn with.

**Not sure yet? Come observe for FREE anytime.**

You can watch the beginner class + guided practice, get a feel for the vibe, and chat with us after. **No pressure, no awkward hard sell.**

**Dates & Schedule (May 2026)**
📅 **Saturdays:** 9th, 16th, 23rd & 30th May (4 Weeks)
🕗 **7:30–8:30PM** Beginner Class (for first-timers)
🕺 **8:30–12:00 Midnight** Social Dance Party

**What you get (RM160 monthly package)**

* **4 beginner classes + 4 socials/ practice sessions**
* **Recap videos** so you can revise anytime
* **Private group chat** so you’re not “doing it alone”
* A warm community that actually remembers what it’s like to be new

✨ **Leads / guys especially welcome** — no experience required. Most people start solo.

**First-timer comfort rules**

* **No partner needed** (most people come alone)
* **Rotation is optional** (you can always sit out)
* **What to wear:** comfy casual
* **Shoes:** comfortable (sneakers are fine)

**Our vibe + community standards**
We’re building the **largest social dance community in Malaysia** — sustainably.
That means: welcoming, respectful, and safe for everyone.

* **Consent matters:** you can say “no thanks” anytime

event_title: Opensource Stories :A Networking Day for Contributors
event_description:
## **Opensource Stories: A Networking Evening for Contributors**

https://luma.com/xt0fuk1c (RSVP are only accepted via luma not in meetup.com)
Open source isn't just code; it’s the infrastructure of the modern world, often built by people in their spare time. **Opensource Stories** is a community-focused evening in Bangalore designed for those who build, maintain, and care about the future of open software.

Forget the polished pitches and sponsor booths. We’re gathering 50–80 developers, designers, and curious minds for honest conversations about the real open-source experience—from the "first-contribution jitters" to the realities of maintainer burnout.

***

### **Why You Should Join Us**

* **Real Journeys:** Hear honest stories from speakers about moving from "lurker" to active contributor.
* **Collective Purpose:** Join a panel discussing why open source matters for equity, knowledge, and how we build things together.
* **Low Pressure, High Connection:** Whether you’re a long-time maintainer or a student looking for your first project, you’ll find a space to share interests and grab a lightning talk slot.
* **Local Community:** Meet the people behind the projects in Bangalore.

***

### **The Agenda**

* **10:00 AM:** Informal mingling & contributor interest mapping.
* **10:30 AM:** Setting the stage: Why open source?.
* **11:00 AM:** Talk: From lurker to contributor—overcoming the fear.
* **11:30 AM:** Talk: The real cost of sustaining a project.
* **12:00 PM:** Talk: How open source changes career trajectories.
* **12:30 PM:** Open conversations and networking.

***

### **Who is this for?**

* **Aspiring Contributors:** If you want to start but don't know where to begin.
* **Active Maintainers:** If you’re looking to connect with the local ecosystem.
* **Tech Professionals:** If you’re curious about licensing, sustainability, and culture.
* **Believers:** Anyone who views open software as a public good worth investing in.

**Ready to share your story?**
https://luma.com/xt0fuk1c (RSVP ARE ONLY ACCEPTED FROM HERE)
If you contribute to open source, thank you. If you don’t yet, this is a good place to start.

event_title: Write Together, KL - Sunday @ Yap Ah Shak, Chow Kit
event_description:
**\# Introduction and Purpose:**

**Write Together** is a two-hour focused writing session that brings writers together to work on whatever project, idea, draft, or creative work they choose. This is **not a workshop**; there is no instruction, curriculum, or prescribed topic. Participants work independently on whatever they are currently writing, or thinking of writing, for the full duration of the session.

The purpose of Write Together is to provide a regular, scheduled space for writers to carve out intentional time to write, strengthen their writing habits, and connect informally with other writers. Sessions take place **fortnightly on Sundays**, typically on the **1st and 3rd Sundays of each month**. Attendance is open to all levels and forms of writing/art.

**\# Agenda:**

**Arrival & Orientation:** Participants are encouraged to arrive a few minutes early. Look for **Shankar or Dilshod** for a brief orientation to the space and session flow. Venue staff can also direct you to the Write Together area if needed.

**Focused Writing Session (2 hours):** Writers work quietly and independently on their own chosen projects throughout the session.

**Optional Social:** The session is typically followed by an informal social at a nearby café or pub. Participation is entirely optional and provides space to connect and converse after the writing period.

**\# Venue:**

We are typically seated at **The Botanical Arts Society at Yap Ah Shak**. It is an elegant space with greenery and, often, soft classical or jazz music. At times, exhibitions are hosted on the same floor, contributing to a calm and creatively stimulating atmosphere.

**\# Requirements:**

1. **You must write** — something, anything. Other forms of creative or expressive work are also welcome.
2. **Please purchase at least one drink** from the venue to support the space hosting the session.

*— Shankar & Dilshod*

event_title: "Arabesques" by Anton Shammas
event_description:
Chosen by *The New York Times* as one of the best books of 1988, *Arabesques* is a luminous novel that engages with history and politics not as propaganda but as literature. That engagement begins with the language in which the book is written: Anton Shammas, from a Palestinian Christian family and raised in Israel, wrote in Hebrew, as no Arab novelist had before. The choice was provocative to both Arab and Jewish readers.

*Arabesques* is divided into two sections: “The Tale” and “The Teller.” “The Tale” tells of several generations of family life in a rural village, of the interplay of past and present, of how memory intersects with history in a part of the world where different people have both lived together and struggled against each other for centuries. “The Teller” is about the writer’s voyage out of that world to Paris and the United States, as he comes into his vocation as a writer, and raises questions about the authority of the storyteller and the nature of the self. Shammas’s tour de force is both a personal and a political narrative—a reinvention of the novel as a way of envisioning and responding to historical and cultural legacies and conflicts.

event_title: The Sunday 6PM Singles Syndicate
event_description:
### **(Curated Proximity & Platonic Connection for Professionals)**

Calling all the singles out there!

Ready to ditch the dating apps and make some real-life connections? Join Bangalore Singles Network (BSN) for a night of Speed Friending and Partying virtually with other singles from the comfort of your own home. We look forward to providing you with opportunities to network, to make new contacts, and to explore the possibility of working together harmoniously to achieve extraordinary results. Let’s come together, learn together, and succeed together!

Let’s be honest: Being single in India as a driven professional is a double-edged sword. You have the freedom, the career, and the lifestyle—but your social circle is likely 90% "coupled up" or 100% "work-focused."
Apps are for scrolling. Bars are for noise. **The Syndicate is for connection.**

On **Sunday, 10th May**, we are hosting a high-intent virtual gathering designed for single men and women who are looking to expand their **platonic** inner circle. This isn't about awkward "matchmaking" or forced romance; it’s about finding your tribe of high-performing peers who happen to be in the same life stage as you.

### **The Agenda: 3 Hours of Curated Synergy**

We use structure to eliminate the "awkward silence." No one is here to be "hit on"—we are here to be heard.

* **Date:** Sunday, 10th May, 2026
* **Time:** 6:00 PM – 9:00 PM (The "Sunday Reset" Hour)
* **Format:** Structured Virtual Breakouts
* **Investment:** **₹349** (The price of a Sunday snack, with 100x the social ROI)

**[6:00 PM – 6:30 PM] — The Syndicate Orientation** A sharp opening. We align our vision for the night. You’ll see the "grid"—a room full of ambitious, single professionals from Tech, Finance, Arts, Business and other fields.

**[6:30 PM – 7:45 PM] — The "Life-Stage" Breakouts** We move into curated Zoom rooms of 4-5 people. The goal? **Speed-Friending.** You’ll rotate through groups to discuss things that actually matter:

* *How do you maintain work-life boundaries?*
* *What are the best "Solo-Travel" hacks for 2026?*
* *The Indian lifestyle: What’s missing from your social calendar?*

**[7:45 PM – 8:30 PM] — The Hobbyist Exchange** Are you a fitness enthusiast? A closet philosopher? A Sunday cook? We facilitate "Interest-Based" rooms where you connect with other singles—men and women alike—who share your exact passions.

**[8:30 PM – 9:00 PM] — Closing & Profile Exchange** The "Syndicate" doesn't end when the Zoom call closes. We facilitate a voluntary exchange of Instagram handles and LinkedIn profiles to turn digital interactions into real-world Sunday brunches or gym buddies.

### **Why Join the Syndicate?**

* **Platonic First:** We focus on building deep, high-value friendships. If a romantic connection happens naturally later, that’s your business—but our mission is your **Social Infrastructure.**
* **No "Balance" Guarantees:** We don't play the "gender ratio" game. This is about meeting **high-quality humans**, regardless of gender. You are here to find friends, allies, and future co-adventurers.
* **The Sunday Solution:** Replace the "Sunday Scaries" with 3 hours of stimulating, high-status conversation.

### **Secure Your Digital Seat**

To ensure everyone gets a chance to speak and connect, capacity is strictly managed.

**Investment: ₹349 (All-Inclusive)**

**Buy your Ticket here: [https://forms.gle/F7pfL2CQrk6VBcQR6](https://forms.gle/F7pfL2CQrk6VBcQR6)**

*After registering, you will receive a confirmation email containing information to join the virtual meeting.*

***NOTE:*** *We are not a dating group. Our events are not organised to manage your dating life. Our group is created to help ladies and gents meet other like minded ladies and gents. Please do not attend our event if a perfect gender balance is important for your enjoyment of our meet-up.*

**NOTE:** *The Syndicate is a professional, respectful environment. We uphold a zero-tolerance policy for solicitation or harassment. Come as you are, leave with a network.*

***About BSN:*** *We're all about creating fun social events for singles in Bangalore. We don't guarantee you'll find your soulmate, a boyfriend, a girlfriend, or a spouse at our meetups. Finding a romantic partner depends on a whole bunch of factors, like mutual attraction, shared interests, compatible personalities, looks, profession, academic qualifications, family background, and all that jazz. We simply provide the platform – the rest is up to you and the chemistry you create! Think of this as a chance to meet new friends, and if something more happens, that's a fantastic bonus!*

RSVP Now and let the FUN begin!

**We look forward to seeing you there. God Bless All!**

Best regards,

Vishak (WhatsApp: +16722750721),
Founder - BSN (Bangalore Singles Network)

event_title: Mini-Talk & Discussion: "Past Life Inquiry as a Healing Tool"
event_description:
Hi everyone,

We’re excited to host a relaxed and engaging parapsychology Mini‑Talk and discussion series as part of our APRA meetups.

At each session, we’ll present a short talk exploring different areas of parapsychology — from psychic perception, mediumship, consciousness and anomalous experiences, through to research, theory and lived practice.

These Mini‑Talks are designed to be accessible, thought‑provoking, and grounded in both experience and inquiry.

After each talk, we’ll open the floor for informal group discussion, questions, reflections, and shared perspectives.

The aim is to create a respectful space where curiosity is welcomed and diverse viewpoints can be explored without judgment.
Whether you’re new to parapsychology, quietly curious, or deeply immersed in the field, you’re very welcome to join us for an evening of learning, conversation, and open‑minded exploration.

We appreciate your support as we continue evolving APRA and updating our online presence.

To learn more about APRA or to donate, visit:
🌐 https://www.parapsychology.org.au

Stay connected by visiting:
📘 https://www.facebook.com/AustralianPRA/

APRA is a not-for-profit group, run by a committee and registered with the ACNC.

Your donations and contributions help us continue providing support in paranormal research, education and advice to you and your community.

For enquiries or support requests, feel free to contact us at:
✉️ apra.parapsychology@gmail.com

We look forward to seeing you at the next event!

Warmly,
Cherie & Vlad

event_title: AWS Core Services Tutorial
event_description: Join us for a hands-on introduction to two of AWS’s most essential services—**EC2** (Elastic Compute Cloud) and **S3** (Simple Storage Service). In this session, you’ll learn how to launch and connect to virtual servers with EC2, and store/retrieve data securely using S3 buckets. The tutorial includes live demonstrations and guided hands-on exercises, so you can follow along and build real cloud skills. Perfect for beginners looking to move from theory to practice.

event_title: Metal Monday
event_description:
An evening for all things metal - machining, welding, fabrication, whatever.

Free for Artifactory Members. Non-members are very welcome, we just ask for $20 to help us keep the lights on.

Come along and:

* work on your projects
* use the machines
* show off your cool stuff
* share ideas
* contribute to group projects
* learn techniques and equipment
* hang out and socialize

Wear enclosed shoes, and consider long sleeves / pants if you're planning on using equipment.

event_title: Let’s Practice Japanese Conversation🗣️
event_description:
【Lesson Overview】
A session designed for those who have already mastered the fundamentals — and now aim to speak truly naturally. In this advanced conversation class, there are no fixed topics — we discuss current events, cultural nuances, and complex ideas freely, just like native speakers do. Through spontaneous discussion and instructor feedback, you’ll refine your phrasing, rhythm, and fluency to sound more authentic and confident in Japanese.
Whether you’re preparing for a professional setting or simply want to express your thoughts with precision and personality, this is the space to challenge yourself.

【Schedule】
90-minute session

【Lesson Style】
・Small group, conversation-focused lesson
・Dynamic, discussion-based learning
・Instructor feedback for fluency and expression
・Relaxed and friendly atmosphere

【This Lesson Is Perfect For】
✔ Learners taking their final steps toward near-native fluency
✔ Those who want to refine nuance, tone, and cultural expression
✔ Anyone who enjoys open-ended, natural conversation

【Target Level】
Expert / Advanced

**\*\*To attend this event please register at [https://community.welearn.co.jp/register](https://community.welearn.co.jp/register) for a 7 day free trial\*\***

【Important Notes】
・Registration is required to attend classes.
⇒[Start7-dayFree Trial](https://community.welearn.co.jp/)
・Camera Off is OK! 💻
If you prefer not to be on camera, you can keep it off during the session.
・Join from Any Device! 📱💻
Participate using your smartphone, tablet, or PC—whatever works best for you! Participate from home, a café, or even while on the go.
・Original Lesson Materials:
The class will use custom materials provided by the instructor. Be sure to have a notebook and pen ready to jot down important points!
・Ask Lots of Questions! 🧑‍🏫
Don't hesitate to ask questions during the lesson.
The instructor will provide clear explanations and encourage communication!
Join us for a fun, supportive, and engaging way to practice your Japanese! Looking forward to your participation! 😊

event_title: Let’s Talk Money! 💰🤩
event_description:
Why is this a Laugh Factory event? Because money follows joy. 😄

What you seek… seeks you too. And what you talk about... expands...

How’s your relationship with money?
Do you want it?
Do you like it?
Do you avoid talking about it?

The wealthiest people I know ***love*** money.
They’re not shy about it. They talk about it, think about it, and they have a great time talking about what money can buy....

Why do you love money?
What would you do with more of it?
What would you buy… and why?

This is about how we think, speak, and feel about money.
Let’s make money our friend.
It’s hard to be “money poor” when your best friend is money 😄

This is a light-hearted session.
Bring your positivity, your ambition, and your imaginative inner child.
Let’s see how far (and how wild) we can go.
Let's flash some imaginative cash!😄

**Applying but hearing nothing back?**

**This is why — and how to fix it fast.**

**Stop Getting Overlooked. Start Getting Shortlisted.**
If you're applying to jobs but not landing interviews, something's blocking you. It's not your experience. It's your positioning.
In this high-impact session, you'll discover exactly what's going wrong — and the simple shifts that change everything.

**This isn't about applying to more roles. It's about fixing what's not working.**

**Who This Is For:**

* Professionals stuck in the application cycle
* Career changers struggling to translate their skills
* Anyone applying but not getting interviews
* People ready to stop guessing and start getting results

**Meet Your Coach:**
**Fi Wainrit** — Career Coach, Founder of Career Mojo, and featured career expert in *The Age* and other leading publications.
With 25+ years in recruitment, talent strategy, and career coaching, I've seen exactly why strong candidates get overlooked — and what actually gets people hired.
I've worked with organisations including ANZ, Just Group, and Swinburne University, helping hundreds of professionals land roles with $10K-$15K salary increases.

**Why This Works:**
This isn't theory. These are the same strategies my clients use to go from:

* No responses → Multiple interviews within weeks
* Confused → Clear and confident
* Stuck → Securing offers (often with significant salary bumps)

**⚠️ Limited Spots Available**

event_title: The "Side-Hustle & Passive Income" Jam
event_description:
### **Hello Builders,**

The single-income stream is a 20th-century relic. In 2026, relying on a salary isn't just "safe"—it’s a bottleneck.

We know the feeling: You have the skills, you have the capital, and you have the itch to build something "on the side." But between the office hours and the Bangalore traffic, you’re missing the most important ingredient: **Proven Frameworks.**

On **Tuesday, 12th May**, we are hosting the **Side-Hustle & Passive Income Jam**. This is a 120-minute, high-intensity deep dive into how modern professionals are building "Revenue Engines" without quitting their day jobs.

### **The Agenda: 2 Hours of Cash-Flow Logic**

No motivational fluff. No "get rich quick" scams. Just raw data, execution strategies, and collective brainstorming.

* **Date:** Tuesday, 12th May, 2026
* **Time:** 9:30 PM – 11:30 PM (Mid-Week Momentum)
* **Format:** Structured "Sprint" Sessions
* **Investment:** **₹499** (An investment in your 2nd and beyond income streams)

**[9:30 PM – 10:00 PM] — The "Sovereign Creator" Protocol** I’ll kick things off by breaking down the 2026 landscape of **Asset-Light Businesses**. We’ll discuss Equity and Asset Ownership, Digital Real Estate, and why "Trust" is the ultimate currency for side-hustlers.

**[10:00 PM – 10:50 PM] — The "Idea-Stage" Breakouts** We move into Zoom rooms curated by your current progress:

* **The Launchers:** For those with zero ideas but high hunger. We brainstorm low-friction entry points.
* **The Scalers:** For those already making ₹20k-₹50k/month and looking to automate the operations.
* **The Passive Investors:** Focusing on REITs, Index Funds, and Fractional Real Estate for the "Set and Forget" crowd.

**[10:50 PM – 11:15 PM] — The Tech Stack Audit** We swap the tools of the trade.

* *Which AI tools are building landing pages in 5 minutes?*
* *What are the best low-cost automated outreach engines?*
* *How to manage a side-hustle in under 4 hours a week.*

**[11:15 PM – 11:30 PM] — Accountability & Alliances** Ideas are worth nothing; execution is everything. Find an accountability partner in the room, swap handles, and commit to one "Revenue-Generating Activity" for the rest of the week.

### **Why Join This Jam?**

* **Practicality:** We focus on businesses that don't require VC funding or a 40-hour work week.
* **The Proximity Effect:** Network with other high-performing 9-to-5ers who are building their empires after hours.
* **Zero Posturing:** We talk real numbers, real margins, and real failures.

### **Secure Your Digital Seat**

We are strictly limiting capacity to ensure the breakout sessions remain high-value and focused.

**Investment: ₹499 (All-Inclusive)**

**Buy your Ticket here: [https://forms.gle/89fUTdxjvTZQymYj9](https://forms.gle/89fUTdxjvTZQymYj9)**

**Note:** *This is an execution-focused meetup. Come prepared to share your ideas and have them stress-tested by a room of ambitious operators. See you in the Jam.*

**Glory To The Supreme God - The Most Merciful and The Most Benevolent. God Bless All.**

Warmly,

Vishak (WhatsApp: +16722750721),
Founder - Business Factory

event_title: Applied AI Ethics MonthlyMixer - Where Innovation Meets Responsibility
event_description:
Join a global, open-format gathering for those building and questioning AI — from real-world use cases to critical conversations around ethics, bias, and transparency.
Every month, we create space for developers, researchers, and policymakers to connect in meaningful, unmoderated dialogue — with flexible chats, peer learning, and 1-on-1 video networking.
Watch how it works: **[https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=bOYkxBjrAYY](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=bOYkxBjrAYY)**
**What Is the Applied AI & Ethics #MonthlyMixer?**
The Applied AI & Ethics MonthlyMixer is a self-directed online networking event where professionals from across the AI spectrum come together to collaborate, question, and connect.
This isn’t a webinar or panel — it’s a structured-free, chat-first environment hosted on a Slack-like virtual platform, featuring topic-specific channels and private video or text chat options.
Whether you're designing algorithms, drafting policy, or advocating for responsible tech, this is your space to exchange ideas, showcase projects, or meet others who care about building AI with integrity and impact.
**Who Should Join:**

* AI/ML developers working on real-world deployments
* Policy makers and legal experts exploring AI governance
* Data scientists interested in ethical model design
* Academics and researchers studying AI and society
* Ethicists, community leaders, and social impact advocates
* Product leaders balancing innovation with responsibility

Whether you're launching applied AI tools or questioning their societal effects, this mixer welcomes your perspective.
**Explore These Channels Inside:**
The platform provides themed group discussions to help you navigate the topics that matter most:

* #general – Start with introductions, announcements, and cross-topic conversation
* #networking – Share your portfolio, white papers, startups, or ethical AI tools
* #intros – Tell your story: what you work on and why it matters
* #help-wanted – Request or offer guidance around data ethics, fairness, or responsible deployment
* #industry-room-tech – Dive deep into real-world cases, AI misuse, explainability, and bias mitigation

You’ll also be able to chat directly and launch 1-on-1 video calls for deeper conversations.
**When & Where?**

* Second Wednesday of Every month
* 5:00 PM – 7:00 PM (your local time)
* Register from the platform here - [https://events.tao.ai/pod/analytics.club/cont-r8tfbvicrqsh](https://events.tao.ai/pod/analytics.club/cont-r8tfbvicrqsh)

No login hurdles. No presentations. Just people-driven dialogue.
**Why Join?**

* Global gathering of AI professionals shaping the future responsibly
* Self-led chats & 1-on-1 networking with values-aligned peers
* Exchange insights, frameworks, and use cases for applied AI
* Build your ethical AI toolkit and collaborative network
* Reconnect monthly to deepen the conversation

**Questions or Suggestions?**
We’re always listening.
Contact us anytime at: [https://noworkerleftbehind.org/event_support](https://noworkerleftbehind.org/event_support)
**Quick Links:**

* Explainer Video: [https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=bOYkxBjrAYY](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=bOYkxBjrAYY)
* Support Page: [https://noworkerleftbehind.org/event_support](https://noworkerleftbehind.org/event_support)

**Hashtags for Discovery & Engagement**
#AppliedAI #EthicalAI #AIEthics #ResponsibleTech #MonthlyMixer #AIGovernance #AIandSociety #FairAI #BiasInAI #TechForGood #AINetworking #AIProfessionals #GlobalAICommunity

event_title: Founder Sundowner
event_description:
**DATE CHANGE!!!**
**This Founder Sundowner has been moved to the following Wednesday (May 13th) as Otherside of Nowhere have an event on and need to shift us!**

Calling all startup founders!

On the first Wednesday of each month (but note the date change above for this one!!), let's get together for a tasty beverage or two, celebrate and/or commiserate the month that was, and share plans and ideas for the month yet to be!

You might even meet your future Co-founder, Engineering Lead, or Senior Vice President of Vibes & Alignment.

As Elon Musk could have once said, "All my success is due to drinking with other startup founders as often as possible".

This is a very casual, monthly, after-work event, in addition to our fortnightly Morning Startup meetups at Spacecubed. So if you're not a morning person, this is for you!

There will be no speakers or formalities. Just a bunch of startups, in a pub, having fun, and learning from each other.

Pizza will be provided courtesy of our amazing Morning Startup sponsors (see below), but make sure you save up ahead of time to cover the cost of your own exotic cocktails and Perth-priced-drinks.

To find us, ask the bar staff for the Founder Sundowner table and they'll point you in the right direction.

Look forward to seeing you there!

**Get around our amazing sponsors!**

Thanks to [Ammo Marketing](https://ammo.marketing/), [Beacham Group](https://beachamgroup.com/), [Purpose Ventures](https://www.purpose.ventures/), and [Everest Engineering](https://everest.engineering/) for your support of Morning Startup and the Perth startup community! If you need help with [marketing and growth](https://ammo.marketing/), [hiring awesome people](https://beachamgroup.com/), [VC funding](https://www.purpose.ventures/), or [app development](https://everest.engineering/) check them out and tell them Morning Startup sent you!

event_title: Perth Rainbow Toastmasters - LGBTIQA+ Public Speaking Club
event_description:
Welcome to Perth Rainbow Toastmasters.

We meet on 2nd and 4th Wednesday of the month.

Perth Rainbow Toastmasters Club is specifically for members, friends, family, and supporters of the LGBTIQA+ communities. Come join us on a journey as we develop our member’s communication and leadership skills with pride.

The meeting is scheduled to run from 6:45pm - 8:30pm. Please arrive 10-15 minutes early to be greeted and seated.

The meetings are in person at Blackburn Accounting: Level 1 (upstairs), 931 Albany Highway, East Victoria Park, Western Australia.

Please note that the meeting room is accessible via a staircase. Unfortunately, there is no lift in the venue.

There will be members delivering speeches and evaluators who will be providing constructive feedback to the speakers. There is an opportunity for visitors/guests and members to practice impromptu speaking in the Table Topics segment. However, there's no obligation to speak if you don't want to. Some people prefer to be observers for the first meeting.

Visitors are welcome to visit free of charge for up to 2 meetings.

Check out our website for more information: https://perthrainbow.toastmastersclubs.org/

Find us on Facebook: https://www.facebook.com/perthrainbowtoastmasters/

event_title: Get Leads Online with AI (Live Workshop Preview)
event_description:
**Curious how people are using AI to generate content, videos, and leads online?**

event_title: Resume Chess: Beat ATS + Win Humans With 3 Clean Moves
event_description:
There are many groups promoting this event.

Our meetup group is participating by promoting this event.

This event is organized by Albert's List.

This is an online event.

Attendees can be located anywhere in the world!

There is a cost to attend this event.

In order to attend, please register here:
[https://www.eventbrite.com/e/resume-chess-beat-ats-win-humans-with-3-clean-moves-tickets-1986287239603?aff=ideatoipo](https://www.eventbrite.com/e/resume-chess-beat-ats-win-humans-with-3-clean-moves-tickets-1986287239603?aff=ideatoipo)

Most resumes fail for one simple reason: they read like a biography, not a targeted pitch.
The resume is a marketing document. Hiring teams don’t want “everything you’ve done.”

They want proof that you can solve the problems highlighted in the job description.
In this live session, you’ll learn a clean, repeatable way to build a strong resume fast using a 3-step AI prompting workflow that stays aligned to the role.
This webinar is built for anyone who wants a resume that feels clear, credible, and directly relevant.
**Move 1: Past Experiences (Proof)**

* Pick the right 3–5 bullets per role and cut the noise
* Turn responsibilities into impact using a simple storytelling structure
* Mirror the job description without keyword stuffing or fluff

**Move 2: Present Skills (Fit)**

* Build a skills section that supports your target role (not a tools list)
* Pull the right skill keywords straight from the job description
* Organize skills so recruiters can scan them in 10 seconds

**Move 3: Future Goals (Direction)**

* Write a focused mission statement that doesn’t sound generic
* Align your “next move” with the role so you feel like an obvious hire
* Keep your resume, LinkedIn, and interview story consistent

No fluff. No guessing. Just a clean system you can run in **15 minutes!**
Reserve your spot and bring a job description if you want to follow along live.
**Meet Your Host:** [Nader Mowlaee](https://www.linkedin.com/in/engineeringcareercoach/ "https://www.linkedin.com/in/engineeringcareercoach/")
Hi, I’m Nader. I’m an engineer with 10 years of recruiting engineering experience before shifting into career coaching.
I help people get hired faster by building a clear system for targeting, outreach, and personal branding. My style is direct, structured, and built for analytical minds.
Why Work With Me?

* 200+ public testimonials on LinkedIn
* Resume + LinkedIn positioning built for hiring managers (not job boards)
* Practical workflows you can repeat without overthinking

Client Note:
"*Nader transformed my resume and LinkedIn profile, and elevated my marketability. I got my dream job!*" Meredith Meyer - Software Engineer, Apple
