event_title: Malaysia Entrepreneurs Network (Kuala Lumpur) Monthly Meetup
event_description:
Good day everyone!

We're meeting at Padi Malaya, Gourmet Street, Ground Floor, G 12b, Lalaport, BBCC.

Same relaxed crowd, same great drinks and food, do feel free to bring your friends along for a night of good conversation and good food. I do look forward to seeing you all there!

For your benefit, here are some Frequently Asked Questions:

Q. What is the agenda of this monthly Malaysia Entrepreneur gathering?

A. Being an entrepreneur can be a lonely journey so this is meetup where you can bounce ideas and get feedback from your peers. Try not to bore the other party with the history of your business. Focus on educating each other and building relationships.

Q. Is this a free to attend event?

A. Attendees have to pay the venue for the food/drinks you consume. You won't be allowed to join us if you don't order anything.

Q. I am not an entrepreneur/I am a student/I am thinking of becoming an entrepreneur/I am a government worker/I am a corporate worker/etc. Am I still allowed/able to come for the event?

A. Sure!

Q. I have this marvelous new method of making money the easy and fast way via Multi-Level Marketing/Personal Franchising/insert-other-similar-names...

A. PLEASE DO NOT TURN UP AND HARASS OUR ATTENDEES. And NO, I do not intend to answer your questions "why/what is wrong/why are you so closed minded/why are you etc etc".

Q. I have this great idea! But why don't you do it for me?

A. Malaysia Entrepreneurs Network is a volunteer driven initiative. We are under no obligation to do anything for you, and neither are my fellow co-organizers. That said, we welcome whoever wishes to organize an event series as long as you have commitment in running the events.

EXTRA NOTE

1. No hard selling, don't pester or force people to buy/join
2. Don't over sell yourself, company or product. Ex: Introducing your company or product yes for 2-3 minutes is acceptable, but not if you keep going on about it for 1 hour+
3. Not welcomed: Snake oil (Miracle) schemes not allowed (ex: xxx product that can cure AIDS, Cancer, makes you immortal/god, product that can guarantee to make you a millionaire if you invest xxx)
4. Not welcomed: All investment `schemes` are not allowed (ex: I represent a marketing company in Singapore selling Sugar investment by this firm in UK who is own by a group of VC in Hong Kong). Finding investors for YOUR company, or looking to invest = ok
5. Not welcomed: Medically insane people
6. You WILL be asked to leave if you fall into #3-#5

event_title: Singapore Power BI & Fabric User Group May 2026 Event (Virtual)
event_description:
Singapore Power BI & Fabric User Group May Event will be a virtual event on May 14th 7pm Singapore time (GMT+8).

Agenda:
7:00--8:00pm [Gregor Brunner](https://www.linkedin.com/in/gregorbrunner/)
Column-level lineage in Power BI with Measure Killer

Measure Killer has been around since 2023, what started as a fun project now became a full governance and lineage solution for Power BI and Fabric, get a deep dive into all its features in this session.

8:00-9:00pm [Pragati Jain](https://www.linkedin.com/in/pragatijain1187/?lipi=urn%3Ali%3Apage%3Ad_flagship3_profile_view_base%3B%2FQf1D3WcTbKLSdSzF5RAwA%3D%3D)
Signal Over Schedule: Engineering Efficient Event-Driven Pipelines in Microsoft Fabric

Is your Microsoft Fabric Capacity being drained by "ghost" pipeline runs? In many traditional data architectures, we schedule pipelines to run every hour or every 15 minutes; only for them to find no new data to process. This "polling" approach is not just inefficient; it’s a silent killer of your compute budget.

In this session, we move from a passive, schedule-based mindset to a reactive, event-driven architecture. Using a real-world use case, I will demonstrate how to transform Fabric SQL Tables into active event sources. By leveraging Fabric Activator to monitor data inserts and updates, we can trigger Data Factory Pipelines and Notebooks only when there is actual work to do.
We will walk through the end-to-end implementation: from configuring the SQL monitoring layer to passing dynamic parameters directly into your downstream workflows.
Whether you are looking to reduce latency for your business users or optimize your Fabric Capacity consumption, this session provides the blueprint for building a truly "active" data platform.

**\# Introduction and Purpose:**

**Write Together** is a two-hour focused writing session that brings writers together to work on whatever project, idea, draft, or creative work they choose. This is **not a workshop**; there is no instruction, curriculum, or prescribed topic. Participants work independently on whatever they are currently writing, or thinking of writing, for the full duration of the session.

The **Thursday session is our flagship** Write Together gathering and has been running consistently for **three years**. It takes place **every Thursday**.

**\# Agenda:**

**Arrival & Orientation:** Participants are encouraged to arrive a few minutes early. Look for **Shankar near the entrance of Plump** for a brief orientation to the space and session flow.

**Focused Writing Session (2 hours):** Writers work quietly and independently on their own chosen projects throughout the session.

**Optional Social:** The session is typically capped with an informal social at the **pub downstairs**. Participation is entirely optional and offers space to connect and converse after the writing period.

**\# Venue:**

Write Together (Thursday) is hosted at **Plump Bangsar**, a cafe lined with books, references to literature, and writing. It has a lovely indoor section with a balcony with additional tables with greenery.

**\# Requirements:**

1. You must write or create — **something, anything**. Other forms of creative or expressive work are also welcome.
2. Please purchase **at least one drink** from the venue to support the space hosting the session.

— **Shankar**

event_title: How to watch the watcher: Investigating vulnerability scanner reports 101
event_description:
While teams are overwhelmed by vulnerability assessments, they rarely understand how popular vulnerability scanners arrive at these determinations. Using popular container scanning tools for illustration, we will break down how your tooling goes from beginning a scan to delivering findings. From that foundation, we'll work through how to read bulletins for a particular CVE and understand what the technical language means. Finally, we'll work through how teams can ensure that the results are accurate and applicable.

This webinar will help you:
- Understand how vulnerability scanners work
- Read CVE bulletins like a pro
- Evaluate vulnerability scans to address concerns

Speaker: Neil Carpenter
Field CTO, Minimus

Neil also helped build the future of cloud-native security at Twistlock, StackRox, Torq, and Orca Security before joining Minimus. When he's not in front of a computer, Neil is an avid NYC-based street photographer.

event_title: Securing the Invisible Thread: Threat Modeling and Defending MCP in Agentic AI
event_description:
Talk Title (MCP / Agentic AI)
“Securing the Invisible Thread: Threat Modeling and Defending Model Context Protocol (MCP) in Agentic AI Systems”

Talk Abstract
As organizations rapidly adopt Agentic AI, the Model Context Protocol (MCP) has emerged as a powerful enabler—connecting LLMs to tools, data sources, APIs, and systems with unprecedented ease. But this same abstraction layer is quietly becoming a security force multiplier, expanding attack surfaces in ways traditional AppSec models were never designed to handle.

In this talk, Vandana Verma Sehgal breaks down how MCP fundamentally changes trust boundaries in AI systems. She explores real-world threat scenarios including context poisoning, token and secret leakage, privilege escalation via scope creep, tool misuse, and shadow MCP servers, showing how seemingly “helpful” agents can become highly effective attack orchestrators.

Drawing from the OWASP MCP Top 10, community research, and field observations, this session provides:

A clear mental model for threat modeling MCP-based architectures

Key anti-patterns and failure modes seen in early MCP deployments

Practical security controls, governance strategies, and runtime defenses for MCP and agentic workflows

Attendees will leave with actionable guidance to design, deploy, and govern MCP-enabled systems securely—without slowing innovation.

short bio :
Vandana Verma Sehgal is a globally recognized cybersecurity leader, Staff Security Advocate at Snyk, and a former OWASP Global Board Member. She is deeply involved in shaping the future of application security, software supply chain security, and AI-native security, with a strong focus on Agentic AI, LLM security, and Model Context Protocol (MCP) risks.

Vandana is a founding contributor and active leader across multiple OWASP initiatives, including the OWASP LLM Top 10, OWASP MCP Top 10, and OWASP GenAI Security Project. She regularly speaks at global conferences such as Black Hat, OWASP AppSec events, BSides, and Nullcon, and is known for translating complex security concepts into practical, human-centric guidance for builders, security teams, and leadership.

Beyond her technical work, Vandana is a passionate community builder, mentor, and educator, driving inclusive security communities and next-generation AI security education worldwide.
when : 15 th May 2026, 14:30 pm- 15:30 pm Jakarta time.
where : online by zoom, this session will deliver in english

event_title: JUNG’S PRIMAL DREAM AND ITS EFFECTS ON HIS LIFE
event_description:
**Lecture: Friday 15 May, 2026**
**7:00pm- 8:30 pm**
***JUNG’S PRIMAL DREAM AND ITS EFFECTS ON HIS LIFE***
**Dr Sally Kester**
In *Memories, Dreams, Reflections* Jung records his primal dream which he had at three years of age. In it he descends into an underground chamber and encounters an enthroned phallus, and he gives both associations and interpretation. The talk explores further aspects of the dream to show its lifelong implication for both his inner and outer life.
**Dr** **Sally Kester** has had two parallel careers, each of them for over forty years. She has been a Jungian analyst in private practice for forty-six years, twenty of them as a training analyst for The Australian and New Zealand Society of Jungian Analysts. She has lectured extensively to the CG Jung Society of WA as well as to Jung Societies around Australia. She was also a part-time lecturer in the School of Music at The University of WA for forty-two years, and for ten years was a professional music critic. For some twenty-five years she presented pre-concert talks for the West Australian Symphony Orchestra. Her doctoral thesis was on Wagner’s Ring Cycle and she has presented lectures and workshops to Wagner Societies in five states\*\*.\*\*
**Venue: Cottesloe Civic Centre Lesser Hall**
**Corner of Napier Street and Broome Street, Cottesloe**

event_title: Ask a Woman: What Men (or Women) Want to Know
event_description:
What does a woman want?
What should you do when she’s upset?
How do you show you care?
What actually matters, and what doesn’t?

These are questions many men (women too) have… but don’t always know who to ask.
This is a space to ask those questions openly, and to meet others who are figuring it out too.

We’re here to better understand each other and learn how to navigate relationships between genders, starting with something simple but often missing: **having a healthy conversation about it**.

This is an **interactive discussion, not a seminar**.

Come with your questions, your experiences, lessons or just your curiosity.

Whether it’s about a partner, someone you’re dating, your mother, a friend, a colleague or an ex - if she’s a woman, I have an opinion!😅 I will also share from a neuro-wiring, cultural upbringing and conditioning perspective😊

At the same time, we’ll learn from each other.

No judgment. Just honest conversations that help you understand a little more.

Come curious. Ask what you’ve been wondering.
You’ll likely leave with more clarity, and better conversations ahead.😊

Meet your host:
Anna, a certified therapist who encourages people to use time-off intentionally - not just to escape, but to heal, reflect, and grow. Having lived across multiple continents and speaking several languages, Anna brings a global perspective to how she understands people and life.

Let's connect on Instagram! @TherapistOnVacation

event_title: Munnar Backpacking Trip
event_description:
Did you know that the name ‘Munnar’ comes from the two words ‘Munnu’ and ‘Aaru’, which mean ‘three rivers’ in the local languages Tamil and Malayalam? Munnar is a town that transports you to the old Bollywood charm of misty hills and undulating tea estates.
Once you enter the Idduki district in Kerala, where Munnar quietly sits, you will be greeted with a drive surrounded by the greenery and the smell of tea, umbrellad by omnipresent clouds. Munnar is not a town which you can leave, even after you have returned.

### **Trip Highlights**

* Scenic ghat drive into the misty hills of Munnar
* Stay amidst tea estates on the quieter outskirts
* Visit to iconic viewpoints surrounded by endless plantations
* Experience Kerala’s ancient martial art at a Kalaripayattu show
* Private trek to a secluded waterfall inside a tea estate
* Explore offbeat waterfalls and panoramic viewpoints
* Adventurous jeep ride to Kolukkumalai — the world’s highest tea estate
* Bonfire evening with food, music & new friendships

**Day 0 - Friday**
‣ Depart from Bengaluru at around 8:30 PM

**Day 1 – Saturday**
‣ Arrive in Munnar via scenic ghat roads
‣ Freshen up at the stay and store luggage
Note: Check-in is post 12:00 noon; washrooms will be available
‣ Breakfast
‣ Visit Photo Point overlooking lush tea plantations
‣ Explore Mattupetty Dam
‣ Lunch at a local offbeat restaurant (limited options available)
‣ Visit Echo Point for scenic views and photographs
‣ Experience the Kalaripayattu Cultural Show, Kerala’s ancient martial art
‣ Return to the stay
‣ Bonfire evening with new friends (subject to weather conditions)
**Day 2 – Sunday**
‣ Early morning wake-up
‣ Private trek to a secluded waterfall inside a tea estate
‣ Relax by the waterfall or take a dip
‣ Return to the stay to freshen up
‣ Heavy breakfast with traditional Munnar black tea
‣ Visit Periyakanal Waterfalls along the Munnar–Thekkady road
‣ Head to Lockhart Viewpoint for panoramic hill views
‣ Adventurous jeep ride to Kolukkumalai, the world’s highest tea estate
‣ Visit Jaguar Rock Viewpoint for photography
‣ Offbeat lunch stop
‣ Evening departure towards Bengaluru
‣ Arrival in Bengaluru early morning (Monday)
**Inclusion**

* Round-trip via AC tempo traveller or minibus (based on group size) with push-back seats
* Accommodation in Cabin Frame Cottage tents.
* Private Waterfalls Experience (Water level subject to weather conditions)
* 2× Breakfast • 1× Dinner
* Drive bata, toll, parking, & interstate parking

event_title: Tadiandamol Coorg Trek
event_description:
As part of its never-ending beauty and wonders, Coorg brings to you its highest point: **Tadiandamol**, a trek at an **altitude of 5735 feet**. This stunning peak is surrounded by the greenery and expanses of the Shola forests, which are broken by the beautiful blueness of the water bodies that cut through it. The word Tadiandamol in Kannada means a big mountain, which is exactly what it is.
The starting point of this trek is **Nalakanad Palace**, which will take you through a wide expanse of colours, sights and smells that you have never experienced before. Once you make it all the 5735 feet to the top, you are going to be rewarded by the company of nature, which will include flowers, snails, trees, streams, and butterflies. Following this gorgeous walk amidst nature, you will return to the homestay and can enjoy the company of your new trek family!
Some memories linger a little longer than others; some leave an esoteric imprint, whilst only a precious few warm you up from the inside and turn you into a storyteller. The mind-blowing adventure to ascend the peak of Tadiandamol, a mountain of such majestic beauty enveloped by an imposing atmosphere of serenity, is one such memory to gain while you enter into the New Year 2024.
Notice that this New Year, seats are limited and will fill out on a First Come, First Served basis. Also, be informed that this New Year is preceded by a weekend, so kindly check out the itinerary and book your seats at the earliest.
**Brief Itinerary**
**Day 0**

* Leave Bangalore by 8:30 PM (approx)

**Day 1**

* Reach the campsite and freshen up
* Leave for Nalaknad Palace (Trek Base)
* Start the Tadiandamol Trek
* Reach Big Rock
* Reach False Peak
* Summit Tadiandamol Peak
* Have a packed lunch during the trek
* Traditional Coorg-style Dinner and Campfire

**Day 2**

* Freshen up and Breakfast
* Visit Abbe Falls
* Visit Golden Temple, Kushalnagar
* Depart for Bangalore and reach by 10:30 PM (approx)

**INCLUSIONS:**

* To and fro Transport from Bangalore ( Non A/c )
* Accommodation in a homestay on a multiple-sharing basis ( separate for men & women )
* 2 Breakfast, 1 Lunch, 1 Dinner
* Karnataka Forest Department Entry Fees
* Local Guide & Outdoor Leader from Plan The Unplanned
* The experience that you gain

**Exclusions**

* Any meals not included above
* Any kind of personal expenses
* Any kind of insurance (health, medical, life, accidental, etc)
* Anything not included above

event_title: 合一气功。免费（随喜乐捐）。在线。
event_description:
免费（随喜乐捐）的在线合一气功课！

“合一气功”是一套独特的、简单易学的，由CordyKet发现的气功，能释放负能量、提升积极能量，并激活所有的三个能量系统（生命力、气和普拉纳）在体内顺畅流动。

合一气功与其他类型的气功的不同之处在于：

人体中有两个不同的能量系统：
普拉纳系统——普拉纳的能量在脉（nadis）和脉轮（chakras）中流动；
气的系统——气在三个丹田和经络中流动。
大部分气功仅仅以增强“气的系统”为主。
但合一气功可以净化、疗愈、修复并增强这两个系统。

事实上，合一气功是启动能量自我疗愈及自我保护的一种形式。

合一气功能疏通和疗愈脉和脉轮、丹田和经络以及身体的其他能量系统。

练习合一气功有助于排出身体里气的系统、普拉纳/脉络系统和思维中的负能量，能同时增强气的系统和普拉纳系统。

合一气功也能提升练习者的振动的频率（维度）。您可以在练习后立即感受到区别。它也能降低您的脑波，是非常好的冥想前的放松练习。

它既可以站着练习也可以坐着，甚至身体无需做任何动作。

合一气功始于2015年年底，是在CordyKet冥想的过程中获得的灵感。起初只是CordyKet及其徒弟的内部习练，2016年7月，CordyKet认为好的东西应该与更多人分享，于是开始传授给大众。

期待在线上见到你！

线上课程的链接及登录密码将会在课前发到CordyKet 线上冥想&合一气功微信群里。https://chat.whatsapp.com/GeswexVw4oODPXbGlJGIXV

### **Why Join the Global Citizen Mixer?**

* **Curated Proximity:** You aren't just "networking"; you are entering a room of people who understand your unique journey of navigating multiple geographies.
* **Operational Intelligence:** Leverage the collective experience of a group that understands international partnerships, foreign exchange, and global market entry.
* **Zero Small Talk:** We focus on the "Global Reset"—how to thrive in Bangalore using the world-class systems you’ve learned abroad.

4. Walking Stick if Required
5. Whistle
6. Mobile Phone
7. A healthy smiling attitude!- don't have?- you will Finish with one!

Walkabout History Since 1857

The founding of KL was almost an accident. In 1857, 87 Chinese prospectors in search of tin landed at the meeting point of the Klang and Gombak rivers and set up camp, naming the spot Kuala Lumpur, meaning ‘muddy confluence’.

KL botanical gardens, 160 acres was opened 13 May 1889, by Sir Frank Swettenham, 1st Resident Governor General of Federated Malay States

Now it's 3 to 4 times the size of Gasing, includes the Tugu, Bird, Butterfly & other Parks

Large variety of trees & plants from all over the world is still thriving now, some unique ones are Mahogany & Brazil Nut trees from the Amazon, 140 years old now & which lives up to a 1,000 years!

[Our Route Takes about 1 Hour of walking, However Picture Taking & Breaks makes it an hour More](https://maps.app.goo.gl/8HEcyeSKjVtJC18K6)

Our KL Heritage Hikes commences at the founding grounds, parks, sportings arena & buildings of the colonial masters, the British, since 1881.

1. Selangor Club 1884
2. Royal Lake Club
3. Dataran Merdeka
4. Railways 1886
5. Govt Printers 1890
6. Anglican Church 1894
7. Govt Buildings 1895
8. Courthouses 1896
9. Theatre 1900's
10. 1st Hotel 1910
11. Armory & Gurdwaras
12. National Monument
13. Parliament
14. KL Botanical Park 1889
15. Carcosa-Govt Generals Residence 1885
16. Birds Eye of Brickfields
17. Spice Gardens
18. Exotic Trees, Brazil Nut, Mahogany, Tembusu, Pulai, Eucalyptus, Baobab, African Sausage, Upside Down Bottle Tree
19. Tun Razak Memorial
20. Tanglin Hospital 1900
21. Methodist Girls School 1898
22. National Mosque
23. Chartered Bank 1857
24. Queen Victoria's Coronation Fountain
25. Ground ZERO Marker

I am attending the Events/Hikes/Tours at my own risk. I understand that the organizer, co-organizer and/or event hosts will try their best to assist and guide, but are no way responsible, liable or accountable for loss, damage, personal injury, death etc. By clicking the RSVP button, I acknowledge I have read, agreed and accepted the terms and conditions of this disclaimer.

event_title: Updated - WMA - Introductory Walk  (East Perth - 7kms)
event_description:
**Introduction to WMA**

We'll continue hosting a series of introductory walks aimed at WMA members who are returning to walking, building up distance/fitness, on a recovery plan following injury and/or want to walk and learn more about the WMA team and hosts. It's probably an ideal opportunity to see if the group is for you before launching into our 10k+ City Park walks and/or Bush walks as we near the Perth hiking season.

Walks will start from the Partisan Cafe in Claisebrook Cove, East Perth. We'll take in the Cove, Matagarup Bridge, the outside of Optus Stadium, Windan Bridge, Heiresson Island/Boorloo Bridge before returning to the Partisan for an optional coffee and catch-up.

**Meet: Partisan Cafe, Claisbrook Cove, EAST PERTH** (Click on map for the **exact** meeting point)

**Parking:** There is plenty of free parking on Wittenoom St and paid parking on Royal Street

**Distance:** 7 km. However, there is the ability to do a shorter walk.

**Difficulty:** Flat terrain & mainly bitumen paths. Expect an easy walk but at a reasonable pace (5km/h) We should complete the walk in under 90 mins.

**What to bring:** Water, yourself and a good sense of humour

**Toilets:** At the Partisan or Toast cafe afterwards and along the way.

**About the Walk**
[Matagarup Bridge](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Matagarup_Bridge)
Optus Stadium
[Windan Bridge](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Windan_Bridge)
Causeway bridge
Heiresson island

**RSVP - no shows & late arrivals:** If you are on the "Going" list and can't make it on the day please RSVP "Not Going" at the earliest convenience as a courtesy so to allow others to join who might be on the "Waitlist".
*We aim to set-off for each walk at the advertised time and as a general rule, we set off on-time for each walk.*

**IMPORTANT**

Powered by: \[CAST AI\]\(https:// https://cast\.ai/?ref_id=267\) & [ClickHouse](https://clickhouse.com) \| In Association with: [Cloud Computing Circle](https://www.linkedin.com/company/cloud-computing-circle/) \| Community Partner: [Docker](https://www.docker.com/) and [Collabnix](https://collabnix.com/)

***

Everyone's talking about AI agents. Most of the conversation is hype decks and Twitter threads. This event is the other thing , engineers in a room, showing what they've actually built, what broke, and what they learned.
Agentic AI Unplugged is a full day meetup focused on AI Agents as they exist in practice: tool calling, orchestration, failure modes, and the infra that holds it all together. If you're building with agents or thinking about it, this is where you come to compare notes with people doing the same.

event_title: Use AI to Simulate Decisions & Stakeholder Thinking — Hands-On Session
event_description:
Hey Bengaluru 👋
We're bringing back the AI Playbook hands-on series for its 4th edition — and this is Session 3.

This one is specifically about decision-making and stakeholder simulation — which is the thing most AI workshops never touch.

Here's the honest pitch:
Most of us use AI for writing and research. The people who've genuinely changed how they work use AI to think. To simulate. To anticipate what's coming before they're in the room.
That's what we're building in this session.

**In 2.5 hours you'll:**
🔹 Use AI to roleplay your manager, client, finance team, or any stakeholder — and generate the actual objections they're likely to raise before your next meeting

🔹 Test your decisions across scenarios — what happens if the budget gets cut? What if the timeline shifts? What if the key person in the room disagrees?

🔹 Build a simulation template you can reuse before any high-stakes conversation — board meetings, client pitches, performance reviews, project sign-offs

**Tools:** Claude, Perplexity, Gemini — bring whichever you use. No setup needed beyond a laptop.

**Who shows up to these sessions:**
Product managers, ops leads, senior engineers, founders, consultants, analysts — anyone who makes decisions and needs to bring other people along. The room is small (30 people max) and intentionally mixed so the conversations are as useful as the content.

**This is part of a 4-session series:** April 25 · May 9 · May 16 · May 23
All sessions at IPL Campus, Koramangala · 9:30 AM – 12 PM

You can join just this session or the full series.
Single session: ₹399
Full series: ₹1,499

NOTE : This is a Paid Workshop

event_title: Stop Applying to Jobs Like It's 2010 - What actually works - Recruiter Advice
event_description:
## Details

Most people are still applying to jobs the same way they did 10–15 years ago and it’s no longer working.
I’m a Tech Recruiter , and I’ve interviewed thousands of candidates and extended hundreds of offers. I can tell you firsthand: **the job market has completely changed.**
In today’s AI-driven economy, simply submitting applications is not enough.
In this session, I’ll break down:

* Why applying online is no longer the most effective strategy
* What recruiters actually look for today
* How to stand out in a competitive, AI-influenced job market
* The strategies top candidates use to get noticed and hired

This is not theory, this is **real recruiter insight** you can apply immediately.
You’ll also have the opportunity to ask questions, connect with others, and start building a smarter approach to your career.

event_description:
𝗙𝗥𝗘𝗘 (𝗱𝗼𝗻𝗮𝘁𝗶𝗼𝗻-𝗯𝗮𝘀𝗶𝘀) session

𝗦𝗺𝗶𝗹𝗶𝗻𝗴 𝗠𝗲𝗱𝗶𝘁𝗮𝘁𝗶𝗼𝗻

Cordelia shall guide you on simple and easy-to-follow steps to free the tension and stress from within your body by breathing exercises, visualizations and using our voice. If you have yet to experience connecting with your body through smiling to your body organs, these exercises will assist you to be more aware of your body state and evoke positivity in self. A non-religious session open to all.

𝗙𝗮𝗰𝗶𝗹𝗶𝘁𝗮𝘁𝗲𝗱 𝗯𝘆 𝗖𝗼𝗿𝗱𝗲𝗹𝗶𝗮 𝗟𝗲𝗲

Cordelia Lee teaches non-religious meditation and facilitates vocal sound toning techniques which focuses on the power of our voice since 2010. She is the co-founder of CordyKet and co-writer of The Spunky, The Rebel & A Mama.

During her journey in seeking solutions for managing mental and emotional health, she discovered her gift in vocal toning. She has conducted Wellness Sounds Sessions and Heartsong sessions in personal and group settings.

She has helped many in her pursuit of growth and empowerment in Malaysia and beyond, such as China, Singapore, Australia and more. She has also collaborated with different organizations in providing mental-emotional health wellness workshops, with communities in mind.

In addition, she is an inspirational speaker, of which her speaking engagements were on fearlessness and empowerment at Miss Plus World Malaysia’s online seminar, Intel Malaysia WLDSC Leadership Development Working Group, among others.

In her spare time, she designs and makes sock dolls by hand to give to the children in hospitals.

Cordelia lives by her motto, which she has coined since 2020: “Clarity, Calmness & Centeredness to manage Chaos & Challenges.”, and “From Light to Ignite, To Infinite Possibilities.”

event_title: Uttari Betta Sunrise Trek
event_description:
When the daily grind starts to get to you, it is time for a well-deserved break. You need to take just a day or two to get in touch with yourself and hit that reset button.
But when you are in a bustling city like Bangalore, where do you escape to?
Simple – you come to Plan The Unplanned and book a Kodachadri trek from Bangalore.
This weekend, the choice is yours. You could sleep in and check out PTU’s ‘gram updates - or be in it. Seriously, you could be walking through dense green forests, gushing waterfalls and landscapes that will wash away the chaos lingering in your mind.
Located within the Mookambika Wildlife Sanctuary, this is one of those treks which is truly magical – the perfect blend of mythology, legends and drop-dead gorgeous sights!
Although there are 3 different trek routes, the one we will take you through might prove to be slightly tough for first-timers. But fret not - if you are ready for a challenge, then this could be the adventure of your lifetime!
Trek highlights:

* Spot every shade of green as you trek through the region’s greenest forests
* Climb up the magnificent Hidlumane Waterfalls
* Visit Mookambika temple (where Goddess Mookambika destroyed the demon Mookasura)
* A pit stop at the Sarvajna Peetha, where Adi Sankaracharya once meditated
* Walk through Nagara fort and stop a while at Devagange Temple as well
* Explore the stunning beaches of Udipi before heading back to Bangalore
* If you’re lucky, we could witness the most spectacular sunset over the Arabian Sea from the peak

When you embark on this trek with the Plan The Unplanned tribe, you get a chance to let your hair down and let it all go – take this trek to reset and reconnect with yourself and nature!
**Brief Itinerary**
Day 0 - Friday
‣ Depart from Bangalore at 9:00pm
Day 1 - Saturday
‣ Reach the homestay and freshen up
‣ Finish breakfast by 6:45am
When the days start getting heavier and harder, you just need an escape.
What if we tell you that there is an escape waiting for you, just 65 kms or so from your office desk?
Uttari Betta has to be one of the best kept secrets of Karnataka - also known as Hutridurga, this is an adorable little hamlet, which is where you get to start this amazing trek. And it doesn’t matter if you are a newcomer to the world of trekking or are a solo female trekker, at Plan The Unplanned, we are here for you. When you choose to embark on the Uttari Betta sunrise trek, you are choosing to take that “me” time that you truly deserve and need - a quick escape from the daily grind and a chance to reconnect with yourself.
**Trek Highlight**

* We promise you the most spectacular sunrise and an amazing early morning adventure
* Guaranteed spectacular views that will leave you spellbound
* Lean in to listen to the stories of a lost past, that still hide in the ancient fort walls
* Make your way up the rock-cut stairs that offer cardio as well as captivating sights

**Itinerary**
**Day 0**
‣ Depart from Bangalore at 10:30 pm
**Day 1**
‣ Reach the base and rest for a while
‣ Start the trek at 4:00 am
‣ Reach the peak and explore
‣ Descend and reach the base by 8:00 am
‣ Head to the local town for a sumptuous breakfast
‣ Reach Bangalore by 12:00 noon
\*All timings are estimates and subject to change
**Included**

* Round trip non-AC transport from Bangalore
* 1× Breakfast
* Forest Department permits
* Trained outdoor leader from Plan The Unplanned & a local guide
* The overall experience that you gain during the trek

**Excluded**

* Any meals beyond those listed
* Any kind of personal expenses
* Any kind of insurance (health, medical, life and accidental)
* Anything not expressly mentioned in the inclusions

**For a detailed itinerary,**
**visit here:** [https://plantheunplanned.com/tours/uttari-betta-trek](https://plantheunplanned.com/tours/uttari-betta-trek)
**For any queries, please WhatsApp us at 7760710005 or 70199 33728.**
Do not message on the Meetup app.
*Follow Plan The Unplanned on Facebook, Instagram, LinkedIn, Twitter, Youtube, and Pinterest to stay up-to-date on their latest adventures, travel tips, and wanderlust-inspiring content.*

event_title: Create High-Converting Marketing Videos Using AI
event_description:
In today’s market, attention is expensive — and content is your biggest growth lever.
But most founders struggle with video:

* It’s slow
* It’s expensive
* It requires too many dependencies

This masterclass is designed to give you control.
In just 3 hours, you’ll learn how to **create high-quality marketing videos using AI — on your own, on demand.**

## 🚀 What You Will Walk Away With

-== About SAGE ==-
  SAGE is a social group for people who identify as being on the autistic spectrum. You do not need a formal diagnosis to come along to events and support people are welcome. Our core values are respect, acceptance, and community, and by holding to these values we aim to create a friendly and safe place for members to relate to others and have some fun. New members are always welcome and we understand that it can take courage to attend events if you don’t get out often. Feel free to message event hosts for details or assurance, we want to meet you. We want all our events be psychological safe spaces and do not tolerate aggressive or inappropriate behaviour. SAGE is entirely volunteer operated and was established in February 2020. If you would like to support SAGE donations are welcome at:

https://gofund.me/187847

event_title: All the Fun of the Fayre - Photojournalism
event_description:
Photojournalism is a form of journalism which tells a news story through powerful photography. Guildford is hosting a Medieval Fayre with reenactment of Medieval Combat, Wandering Minstrels plus normal market stalls etc. We attended this fun event last year and there were so many photo opportunities. We will learn how to create emotive photographs and ones which clearly tell the story of the event.
This small group session is lead by Anna-Mair, a formally trained professional photographer with many years experience of teaching people from beginners to advance to become better photographers. We welcome all cameras. Our meetups start with a brief of how to set the camera for the topic. They are fun and informative with likeminded people and the professional photographer on hand to assist throughout the session.
Anna-Mair Francis is a professional fine art photographer having held several successful exhibitions which were held in high regard with many positive reviews. She has images displayed all over the world. She is a very successful teacher having had many of her former pupils going on to achieve awards and be highly respected photographers in there own right. She is highly regarded by the people she has taught and the methods she uses to teach them photography. She is very patient and understanding and works on the philosophy that there is no such thing as a silly question.

event_title: "A Private Life"
event_description:
----------------------------------------------------

**Film Link**

https://www.lunapalace.com.au/films/a-private-life

----------------------------------------------------

**Event Notes**

-Event start time tbc once film session times are released, currently estimated as 1pm based on typical past session times.

-We recommend booking tickets in advance to avoid missing out.

-The film is in french with english subtitles.

----------------------------------------------------

**Event Schedule**

-Watch film which is 103 min in length.

-Have a post-film chat nearby over drinks and snacks at a bar or cafe.

----------------------------------------------------

**Film Description**

"Paris. A shocking death, a determined psychiatrist, and a truth no one else wants to see. When Paula Cohen-Solal is found dead, the authorities rule it a suicide. But her psychiatrist, Lilian Steiner (Jodie Foster in her first major French-language role), refuses to accept it.

Convinced that Paula’s husband is involved, she decides to investigate on her own – even if it means venturing far beyond her professional limits. With the help of her ex-husband Gabriel and a hypnotist whose sessions trigger unsettling visions, Lilian is plunged into a labyrinth where memory, intuition, and doubt collide. Each vision brings her closer to the truth… or deeper into danger.

Jodie Foster delivers a riveting performance worthy of her two Oscars and four Golden Globes, and showcases her sensational linguistic and emotional precision. Directed by Rebecca Zlotowski the film reimagines the investigative thriller with a bold twist – blending tension, dark humour, and witty couple dynamics within a charged psychological setting.

Festival de Cannes 2025 | Official Selectio
Sydney Film Festival 2025 | Official Selectio
Melbourne International Film Festival 2025 | Official Selection"

---------------------------------------------------

event_title: The Virtual Boardroom: High-Stakes Networking for Bangalore’s Elite
event_description:
Hello Visionaries,

We know the feeling: you want the high-level connections and the strategic breakt oughs of a BHSC meetup, but sometimes you want to do it from the comfort of your home office.

On **Sunday, 17th May,** we are breaking geographical barriers within the city. No traffic, no parking hassles—just pure, unfiltered business growth. We are taking the **Bangalore High Society Club (BHSC)** online for a 3-hour intensive session designed to audit your business, expand your network, and set up your "Revenue Engines" for the month ahead.

Whether you are a student looking for a mentor, a working professional looking for a new role or additional new streams of income, an entrepreneur scaling a startup, or an investor looking for the next big deal, this virtual summit is where your next breakthrough begins.

### The Agenda: 3 Hours of Virtual Velocity

We have adapted our signature high-impact format specifically for Zoom, utilizing breakout rooms and digital whiteboards to ensure maximum interaction.

**Date:** Sunday, 17th May, 2026
**Time:** 6:00 PM – 9:00 PM
**Platform:** Private Zoom Link (Sent upon registration)

**6:00 PM – 6:30 PM] — The Digital Handshake & Vision Alignment**
We start sharp. A quick orientation on how to use the session for maximum ROI. We’ll kick off with rapid-fire introductions in the main room to get a pulse on the industries present—from Tech and Real Estate to Finance and Creative Services.

**6:30 PM – 7:30 PM] — The Referral & Commission Exchange (The "Money" Session)**
his is the core of BHSC. In structured intervals, members will present:
**What they offer:** Your product or service.
**The Reward:** The specific commission or referral fee you pay to partners.
**The Target:** Exactly who you want to be introduced to. By the end of this hour, you will have a digital directory of partners ready to cross-promote your business to their respective networks.

**7:30 PM – 8:15 PM] — Virtual Mastermind: Peer-to-Peer Problem Solving**
We will move into **Zoom Breakout Rooms** curated by industry and interest. Bring one specific "Blocker" you are facing.

***Struggling with digital ad spend? Need a reliable vendor in Bangalore? Can't figure out your pricing strategy?*** Your peers in the room will act as your "Board of Advisors," offering instant, diverse solutions and "Peer-to-Peer" learning that would otherwise cost thousands in consulting fees.

**8:15 PM – 8:45 PM] — The Barter Bazaar: Skill-Swapping for Scalability**
In this session, we facilitate "Cashless Growth."

* I’m a developer who needs legal advice."
"I’m a CA who needs social media management." We will use the chat and digital boards to match members for skill bartering, allowing you to save your capital while still getting top-tier professional work done for your business.

**8:45 PM – 9:00 PM] — Closing Keynote & Long-Term Alliances**
We wrap up with a focus on "Relationship ROI." We’ll share the BHSC framework for turning a one-time Zoom meeting into a long-term strategic partnership. Final exchange of LinkedIn & Instagram profiles and digital business cards. You will also be given a link to join the WhatsApp Group meant only for the members who attend this Meetup.

### Why Join This Virtual Edition?

* **Focus:** Without the distractions of a physical venue, we dive deeper into the data and the deals.
**Convenience:** Log in from anywhere. No Sunday evening commute.
**Accessibility:** A lower entry price means more diverse talent and more potential referral partners in the "room."
**Digital Directory:** All attendees will receive a digital list of the connections made during the session.

## Investment in Your Network:

To keep this session exclusive to serious players while acknowledging the online format, the investment is just:
**349 per head (Early Bird, All-Inclusive)**
**499 per head ( Non-Early Bird, All-Inclusive)**

***Note***\*: To ensure everyone gets a chance to speak and participate in the Mastermind sessions, we are strictly limiting the Zoom capacity. This link will expire once we hit our limit.\*

## How to Register:

Secure your digital seat and prepare your "Elevator Pitch." The connections you make this Sunday could be the catalyst for your most profitable quarter yet. We will stay connected with you via Email or WhatsApp to keep you updated about the Meetup. Buy your ticket(s) from this page: ***[https://www.bangaloremeetups.fun/event-details/the-virtual-boardroom-high-stakes-networking-for-bangalores-elite](https://www.bangaloremeetups.fun/event-details/the-virtual-boardroom-high-stakes-networking-for-bangalores-elite)***

**Host**: Bangalore High Society Club (BHSC).
**Platform**: Zoom Video Communications.

Let’s leverage the power of technology to build the wealth of the future. See you on the screen!

***BHSC WhatsApp Group: [https://chat.whatsapp.com/CDMoYry7AhGId4FDBUNcpC](https://chat.whatsapp.com/CDMoYry7AhGId4FDBUNcpC)* \***

We are incredibly excited to host you and witness the powerful synergies that will emerge. We are confident that this event will be a game-changer for your business. **Join us at the Bangalore High Society Club (BHSC) for an evening of strategic networking, collaborative growth, and unparalleled opportunities**. Let's build something great together.

**Best Regards and God Bless,**

Vishak Ganti (**WhatsApp:** +16722750721), Founder - Bangalore High Society Club (BHSC)

event_title: May - Her Wealthy Circle Book Club 📚
event_description:
Ready to take control of your financial future and dive deep into some of the minds of the financially independent? Then join us for our monthly book club, where we’ll unpack some ideas around mindset, financial education and wealth creation.

**This month's book is:** The Wardrobe Project: A Year of Buying Less and Liking Yourself More

**About the book**
One year. A whole new perspective on style, spending and self-image.

Have you ever stared at a wardrobe full of clothes thinking 'I have nothing to wear'? You're not alone. Millions of women are stuck in the frustrating cycle of shopping for the perfect outfit, only to find themselves overwhelmed and confused as to why no matter what they buy, they never look the way they want to.
This expensive obsession drains our wallets and leaves us feeling empty, especially when we face new cost-of-living pressures.

Emma Edwards, financial behaviour specialist and founder of The Broke Generation, broke free from this cycle with a radical experiment: one full year without buying any clothes. No new clothes, no second-hand finds, not even rentals.

In The Wardrobe Project, Emma shares her raw, honest journey of stepping away from what she calls the 'hamster wheel' of spending. She confronts our culture of consumption and the impossible standards women are forced to chase ― and reveals the empowering transformation that followed.
What you'll discover:

* How to understand your buying habits and ways you might be outsourcing your identity to consumption
* Real-world strategies for surviving weddings, work events, and ‘nothing to wear’ days ― all without buying something new
* The link between body image and fashion consumption, through Emma's raw month-by-month insights into changing the way she saw herself
* The unexpected power that lies within your existing wardrobe and how wearing what you can have can change everything
* Practical techniques for saying no to impulse buying, whether you're in the store or shopping online
* Ways to find confidence beyond the perfect outfit and rediscover what brings you joy

The Wardrobe Project is your guide to gently shifting your spending habits and finding confidence within yourself instead of searching for it on a rack. It might even tempt you to take on the no-buy challenge as you discover your inner brilliance, without the need to add to cart.

[Amazon link](https://www.amazon.com.au/Wardrobe-Project-Buying-Liking-Yourself/dp/1394376812)
[BookTopia link](https://www.booktopia.com.au/the-wardrobe-project-emma-edwards/book/9781394376810.html)

**Mission:** A welcoming space for financial independence enthusiasts to connect, discuss, and explore diverse perspectives through literature.

**Goals:**

* Foster engaging discussions about a wide range of books.
* Build a supportive community of readers with shared interests.
* Encourage personal growth, open-minded thinking, and creativity through reading.

Members are encouraged to read the monthly selection and come prepared to share their thoughts.

Participate using your smartphone, tablet, or PC—whatever works best for you!
Participate from home, a café, or even while on the go.
・Original Lesson Materials:
The class will use custom materials provided by the instructor. Be sure to have a notebook and pen ready to jot down important points!
・Ask Lots of Questions! 🧑‍🏫
Don't hesitate to ask questions during the lesson.
The instructor will provide clear explanations and encourage communication!
Looking forward to your participation! 😊

→ Add us as a friend here: [ [https://lin.ee/q0qHhDS](https://lin.ee/q0qHhDS) ]

event_title: EmpowerUs Well-Being Meditation
event_description:
**Find Your Balance. Rediscover Yourself.**
If you’ve been feeling restless, unable to relax, or struggling to find your footing in life — you’re not alone. EmpowerUs is here to help you reconnect, rebalance, and remember who you truly are.

* You try to relax, but your mind never stops racing.
* You long for balance, but stress and emotions keep pulling you away.
* You feel tired even after rest — as if something deeper is out of sync.
* You sense you’re meant for more, but can’t quite see the path forward.

**Other struggles we address:**

* Feeling “stuck” in repeating life patterns.
* Living on autopilot, disconnected from joy and meaning.
* Overthinking, overwhelm, and burnout.

**What if you could…**
✔️ Finally **relax deeply**, without guilt or distraction.
✔️ Find a natural **balance** between work, family, relationships, and you.
✔️ Feel emotionally lighter, mentally clearer, and physically recharged.
✔️ **Become whole again — simply by being yourself.**

**Our Space, Your Transformation**
At EmpowerUs, we offer more than wellness sessions.
We offer a safe space for transformation — where you can breathe, reflect, and gently rediscover yourself.
Through universal, non-religious guided meditation, holistic healing (mental, emotional, and physical), and a supportive community, you’ll experience the clarity, balance, and confidence to become more of who you are.

\- On\-site guided meditation
\- Monthly Membership subscription \(30\-day pass\)
\- 8 classes for 4 weeks
\- Every Tuesday 8 \- 9pm & Saturday 11am \- 12pm

More details: [https://thegoldenspace.com.my/empowerus-well-being/](https://thegoldenspace.com.my/empowerus-well-being/)
Or WhatsApp us +6011 4058 2178

There's also a one-time trial class you can experience. Talk to us!

event_title: Founders Networking: Connect & Help Each Other
event_description:
**Founders helping founders – not a slogan, a weekly session.** Stuck on the same problem for weeks, need some help or advice? **Join a live builders' networking – casual format, real connections, actual progress.**

* Meet builders at your stage or one step ahead
* Get honest feedback on what you're building
* Challenge your messaging, strategy, or any idea

📆 Tuesdays · 20:30-21:30 IST · Zoom

**This is your weekly founder check-in.** No pitches, no performance – just builders helping builders sort out what's slowing them down.
\*\*\*

A community of builders working on real traction. Here we're sharing real feedback, marketing that actually works, and hands-on builder experience.

event_title: AWS CendekiAwan 2026: Showcast Event
The Future of Search in Umbraco Starts Here
event_description:
Umbraco Search is on its way, and it’s coming fast.

**But what exactly is it, and why should you care?**

In this session, we’ll break down what Umbraco Search is all about, what problems it aims to solve, and how it fits into the evolving Umbraco ecosystem. You’ll also get practical insights on how to start preparing for it, so you’re ready to take full advantage when it lands.
Whether you're working with content-heavy platforms or looking to improve search experiences, this session will give you a solid starting point.

event_title: Agentic AI Multimodal MonthlyMixer - Where Agents Minds Meet
event_description:
Join the global gathering of AI builders, researchers, and enthusiasts pushing the boundaries of agentic systems and multimodal intelligence.
Connect every month for organic networking, 1-on-1 video chats, and powerful peer-to-peer exchanges across a flexible, open chat-based platform.
**Watch how it works**: [https://youtu.be/trmf6Gqs6FM](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=trmf6Gqs6FM)
What Is the **Agentic AI Multimodal** #MonthlyMixer?
The **Agentic AI Multimodal MonthlyMixer** is a self-led, virtual networking event built for the ever-evolving AI community. Held on a Slack-like platform, this event is **unmoderated and open-format**, giving you full control to explore conversations, meet new minds, and share your work with others who “get it.”
From autonomous agents to multimodal AI, this mixer is the go-to online space for researchers, engineers, founders, and curious learners working at the frontier of machine intelligence.
**Who It’s For:**

* AI & ML researchers building agent-based systems
* Engineers and developers experimenting with LLMs and multimodal models
* Founders and startup teams innovating with AI tools
* Students, hobbyists, and professionals exploring applied AI
* AI community managers, mentors, and tech evangelists

Whether you're here to **collaborate, hire, share, or simply learn** — this mixer offers a space to grow your AI network month by month.
Channels You'll Explore:
Once inside, you’ll find themed group chats designed to make it easy to dive in:

* **[#general](https://teamtaoindia.slack.com/archives/C04SYEPNHH6)** – For intros, casual chat, and major community announcements
* **#networking** – Drop your portfolio, project, startup, or agent demo
* **#intros** – Share your background, interests, and goals
* **#help-wanted** – Ask for or offer help with AI, code, design, research & more
* **#industry-room-tech** – Deep discussions on the latest tools, models, and frameworks in AI

And yes — you can instantly jump into **1-on-1 video or text conversations** with anyone you meet.
When & Where?

* **Every third Wednesday of the month**
* **5:00 PM – 7:00 PM (your local time)**
* **Register from the platform here - [https://events.tao.ai/pod/analytics.club/cont-4d55c8eg78pg](https://events.tao.ai/pod/analytics.club/cont-4d55c8eg78pg)**

No Zoom. No formal agenda. Just real people, real tech, real connections.
**Why You Shouldn’t Miss It:**

* Global network of agentic AI practitioners and researchers
* Self-guided 1-on-1 connections and peer-led topic chats
* Showcase your work, explore partnerships, or ask for feedback
* Return monthly to reconnect and keep the conversation going
* A low-pressure space to think out loud with fellow innovators

Questions or Feedback?
Reach out any time at: [https://noworkerleftbehind.org/event_support](https://noworkerleftbehind.org/event_support)
**Useful Links**

* **Explainer Video**: [https://youtu.be/trmf6Gqs6FM](https://youtu.be/trmf6Gqs6FM)
* **Need help?** [https://noworkerleftbehind.org/event_support](https://noworkerleftbehind.org/event_support)

**Hashtags:**
#AgenticAI #MultimodalAI #MonthlyMixer #AICommunity #AIMeetup #LLMNetworking #AutonomousAgents #AIBuilderMeetup #MachineLearningNetworking #VirtualAIMixer #AIIndustryEvents #ConnectWithAI \*\*#\*\*thefutureofAI—together.

event_title: In-Person Meetup: AI in 2026, From Predictive to Proactive
event_description:
**In-Person Meetup: AI in 2026 - From Predictive to Proactive**

AI has moved from dashboards to decisions. This in‑person meetup is for Perth leaders who want to understand what **proactive AI systems** mean for revenue, risk, and workforce, without vendor spin. You get short, sharp talks from WA operators, technologists, and policymakers on how AI is actually being used in Perth plus where it is failing. Then we open the floor for questions you cannot ask in a sales pitch.

**Learning outcomes**
Participants will:

* See real WA case examples of AI lifting efficiency and decision quality, and where it stalled.
* Understand the difference between predictive analytics and emerging agentic AI systems.
* Identify one or two candidate areas in their own business for exploration.
* Learn which WA AI Hub workshops or cohorts best match their maturity.

event_title: Outer Join - An Industry Inner Join Event
event_description:
***Outer Join - An Industry Inner Join 'Spin-off' Event***

*We’re going a little bigger for this one.*

**Outer Join** is a one-time spin-off from Industry Inner Join - built for the Perth data community.

This is a **mini-conference-style evening** for people working across **data, analytics, AI, engineering and leadership**. We're bringing together senior-level professionals, sharp thinking and broader industry conversations under one roof.

***What to Expect***

**4:00PM** Doors open. Drinks & snacks to kick things off.
**5:00PM** Speaker Sessions begin:

🎤 Ash Smith - Manager, Data @ South32
🎤 Andy Maher - Manager, Digital and Data @ IGO
🎤 Yulia Merrill - President @ DAMA Perth
🎤 TO BE ANNOUNCED

**Panel Discussion** \- An interactive conversation with industry leaders\, open to audience questions\. Introducing the panel:

🎤 Karen Stewart - Governance Manager, Data & AI @ P&N Bank
🎤 Jonathan Dingle - Head of Data @ FinTech Services Australia
🎤 James Wright - Chief Strategy Officer @ InterWorks

And your host [Douglas Robertson](https://www.linkedin.com/in/douglas--robertson/) \- Director @ DR Analytics Recruitment

**6:30PM** Wrap up, networking, food & drinks
**8:00PM** Close

***The Conversations***
Expect conversations around things like:

* Where AI is actually heading
* What’s changing across data teams and businesses
* How technical people can create more influence and impact
* The bigger shifts happening across the industry right now

***The People***
As always, a big part of **Industry Inner Join** is who ends up in the room.
***Outer Join*** is designed to bring together:

* Analysts
* Engineers
* Data scientists
* Consultants
* Leaders

***Who Should Come***
This event is for senior-level professionals working in or around:

* Data & Analytics
* Business Intelligence
* Data Engineering
* AI/ Machine Learning
* Technology
* Product
* Strategy
* Transformation

***Why We’re Running It***
Industry Inner Join has always been about helping uplift **data literacy, connection and community** in Perth. **Outer Join** is an extension of that.

This is a chance to experiment with a different format, bring together a strong room and create space for the bigger conversations happening across Perth and the industry right now.

***Spots are limited***
This one will be curated comparatively to our usual events, so registrations are limited. *If you want to be there, jump in early.*

**Event Details:**

* **Time:** 4:00PM – 8:00pm *(Speakers from 5:00pm sharp)*
* **Venue:** Central Park Conference Centre
* **Address:** Level P1, 152 St Georges Terrace, Perth
* **Dress Code:** smart casual
* **Food:** food provided
* **Drinks**: drinks provided (non-alc. & alcoholic)

**Contact:**
F﻿or more event information please contact Douglas Robertson:
[Linkedin ](https://www.linkedin.com/in/douglas--robertson/)\| Email: douglas@analyticsrecruitment\.com\.au

**Sponsored by:**

* [DR Analytics Recruitment](www.analyticsrecruitment.com.au) – helping businesses secure top data & analytics talent - building people, building community, building businesses.

event_title: The Perth AI Roundtable
event_description:
Welcome to the Perth AI Roundtable Meetup!

**MONTHLY EVENTS**

\* Rocky Ridge Brewery, Burswood 6:00pm
\* 3rd Thursday of the Month
\* Kick off 21st May 2026 @ 6pm

The Perth AI Roundtable is a structured, facilitated conversation where the room collectively discusses and learns about AI and how to benefit from it.

This meetup is dedicated to exploring the latest developments in AI Agents and associated systems.

Submit topics at: https://socratic.otherstuff.ai/

On the 3rd Thursday of the month, we will have a community curated list of topics which will form the agenda for the evening, we will pick out topics to focus on and discuss and learn in a open manner for everyone in the room.

Whether you are a seasoned AI professional or you’re just starting out, this is the perfect place to network, share ideas, and learn from each other.

**WHAT TO EXPECT**

6:00 – 6:30: Arrive & connect
Drinks, introductions
6:30 – 7:45 - Facilitated Socratic discussion on the topics in the list. Open floor, no wrong answers
7:45 – 8:00 - Nominate next month's questions

**NOMINATE A TOPIC**

Topics can be nominated on an open community list prior to the session and will be curated by the Organisers and our handy AI assistant Wingman.

Join us for discussions, workshops, and presentations on the latest trends and developments in the world of AI and Agents.

event_title: 线上成人口才课程 | Tangkak | Yong Peng | Batu Pahat
event_description:
表达，不只是说话，而是让机会看见你。

你有没有发现——
会表达的人，更容易被理解；
会沟通的人，更容易赢得信任；
会讲话的人，往往拥有更多机会。

无论是在职场、家庭、社交，还是公众场合，口才，早已不是加分项，而是竞争力。

M5分区主办线上成人口才课程，特别为想提升表达能力、突破自我、增强影响力的你而设。

✨ 三堂实用课程，带你学习：
✅ 克服紧张与演讲恐惧
✅ 掌握即兴演讲技巧
✅ 提升临场反应能力
✅ 学习高效沟通与领袖表达
✅ 运用肢体语言与声音魅力，让表达更有感染力

这不是一堂单纯“教你讲话”的课程，
而是一场帮助你成长、自信蜕变、提升个人影响力的学习旅程。

event_title: NEW TIME 7.00AM  START    South Perth, Narrows to Boorloo Bridge loop (10k)
event_description:
## Details

\*\*NOTE NEW START TIME 7.00 AM \*\*

Mornings are staying a bit darker so we will switch to autumn time.

A circular route taking in the South Perth and City foreshores between the Narrows and Boorloo bridges.
**Where**
Meeting near the Boatshed café and restaurant at the end of Coode Street. At the beginning of the jetty.
**Parking**
Carparks are nearby. Parking is free at the starting time of the morning but you pay for the next hour, ensure you collect a ticket and place it on your car dash.
**Distance**
10km and about 1hr 45min - 2hrs walking.
**Difficulty**
An easy paced walk but expect a decent pace and a good distance along paved paths sometimes shared with cyclists, kids and dogs. Kids and dogs are usually well behaved.
**What to bring**
Water, good walking shoes, hat & sunscreen
**Toilets**
At Betty’s Jetty and the café.
**Café after the walk**
Coffee and a snack at the Boatshed. We may need to sit outside if they are busy.
**AllTrails Map:** [Sth Perth Bridge to Bridge loop (10km)](https://www.alltrails.com/explore/map/south-perth-bridge-to-bridge-loop-9-7km-1f87a3f)

*We aim to set-off for each walk at the advertised time and as a general rule, we set off on-time for each walk.*

By confirming your attendance attendees agree to "WAIVE, DISCHARGE CLAIMS, AND RELEASE FROM LIABILITY" the organisers, co-organisers, event organisers, volunteers and members of the *Walking Mates Australia* Meetup Group from any and all liability on account of, or in any way resulting from Injuries and Damages, even if caused by negligence, in any way connected with *Walking Mates Australia*.

event_title: Use AI to Build Your Professional Edge
event_description:
Hey Bengaluru 👋
Let's talk about the question most professionals are quietly asking but nobody says out loud:
*"If AI can do half of what I do — what exactly am I still here for?"*

It's not a comfortable question. But it's the right one. And the professionals who answer it clearly right now are the ones who stay ahead of everyone who's still avoiding it.
This session is built around that answer.
Not in a theoretical "here's where humans beat AI" way. In a practical, build-it-in-the-room way — using tools you already have access to.

***

**Here's what you'll actually do in 2.5 hours:**
You'll come in with your work history, your projects, your patterns — and leave with something concrete that captures exactly what you bring that no AI in the world can replicate.
→ Use Notion AI, Mem AI, or NotebookLM to reflect on your projects and surface the patterns of thinking and decision-making that define how you work — things you've never written down because nobody asked

→ Identify precisely where you create irreplaceable value when AI is in the loop — not vague strengths, but specific moments where your judgment, context, and relationships change outcomes

→ Build a personal positioning statement grounded in how you actually think, decide, and use AI at work — not a LinkedIn buzzword salad, a genuine articulation of what makes you worth the room

→ Use writing tools like Grammarly, Jasper, or Claude to polish your LinkedIn, CV, or portfolio so it reflects who you actually are — sharper, clearer, without losing the voice that makes it yours

***

**Why this matters right now specifically:**
AI is not going to replace you. But someone who knows how to use AI — and can articulate clearly why they're still the most valuable person in the room — might.
The professionals who thrive in the next five years won't be the ones who know the most about AI. They'll be the ones who know the most about themselves in the context of AI.
That's what this session is designed to give you.

***

**Bring to the session:**
Your laptop. A rough sense of your last 2-3 major projects or wins at work. An honest 5 minutes thinking about where you feel most valuable — and where you feel most at risk.
No prep needed beyond that. The session does the rest.

***

**Who this is for:**
Senior professionals, product managers, engineers, consultants, ops leads, analysts — anyone who is good at their work and wants a clear, confident answer to "what do I do that AI can't?" that they can actually use in a room, on a CV, or in a conversation with their next employer or client.
Also for people mid-career transition, freelancers building their positioning, and anyone who has felt the ground shift a little under their feet in the last 12 months and wants to stand on it more firmly.

***

**This is Session 4 of the AI Playbook for Decision Makers series:**
A 4-Saturday hands-on workshop running at IPL Campus, Koramangala.
April 25 · May 9 · May 16 · **May 23**

Each session is standalone you can join just this one or the full series.

See you on May 23.

event_title: From Chat to Agents: Five AI Engineering Paradigms
event_description:
# Description

Software development with AI is no longer just prompting. It spans everything from quick chat prompts to autonomous agents and AI-infused features. In this live-coding session, a simple game evolves through five AI engineering paradigms:

* Chat-Oriented Programming:

Explore ideas and approaches through conversational prompts

* AI-Assisted Programming

Use inline code completions to speed up development

* Prompt-Powered Programming

Generate, test, and refactor code using structured prompts

* Agentic Workflows

Delegate multi-step tasks to agents with minimal intervention

* AI-Infused Products

Build user-facing features powered by AI capabilities

Participants see how work shifts between humans and AI across these paradigms, and leave with a practical framework to choose the right paradigm for their context.

Most teams are experimenting with AI, but lack a clear way to decide how to use it effectively. This talk cuts through the AI hype and clarifies how to actually use AI in day-to-day software development. This is not a tools talk. It is about ways of working with AI. These paradigms are not theoretical. They are grounded in how our teams are already applying AI in real-world software delivery.

# Outline

\- Introduction and overview: 5 min
\- Live coding of a simple game across 5 AI engineering paradigms: 30 min
\- Principles and practices for choosing the right paradigm: 15 min
\- Q&A: 10 min

event_title: Vic Park Community Games Night [Free event thanks to the Town of Vic Park]
event_description:
**Get ready for a night of fun 🎉, friends 👫, and friendly competition! 💪**

Join us for our ever-popular monthly board games night, where you can meet new people, try new games, and enjoy some relaxed socialising.

This event is now **Free** after we received funding from the Town of Victoria Park to run these events.

**About the 🎲 board games night 🌃**

Just two weeks after launching Activate Mental Health, founder Rahul Seth took the stage at a Vic Park Collective crowdfunding dinner in Victoria Park. His passionate pitch paid off, securing $890 in funding.

Demonstrating his commitment to the broader mental health community, Rahul donated 25% of the funds to another local charity. With the remaining amount, he purchased a collection of board games, laying the foundation for Activate's monthly board game nights.

Since August 2016, these nights have been a staple at the Victoria Park Community Centre, fostering connection and community among attendees.

**Our games 🎲**

The board games featured in the monthly games nights have all been crowdfunded and sourced from donations by the community. Our Complete games list is here:

event_title: Role of a Architect in coming Age of AI
event_description:
Artificial Intelligence is reshaping industries at lightning speed, and nowhere is the impact felt more profoundly than in IT.
This raises bold questions: What is the future of the Architect’s role?
Will AI diminish their importance—or will it elevate them to new heights?Join me in person as we explore how Architects have historically been the backbone of IT transformation, and why their role will become even more pivotal as AI takes over entry-level tasks.
Far from being replaced, Architects will be the visionaries who design, orchestrate, and guide the intelligent systems of tomorrow.You’ll also discover the SPARKS Framework, a powerful model to cultivate the mindset of an Architect—one that thrives in complexity, embraces innovation, and leads with clarity in the age of AI.
This is not just a talk; it’s a call to action for every aspiring leader who wants to shape the future rather than be shaped by it.✨ Come ready to be inspired, challenged, and equipped for the next era of technology.I look forward to seeing you in person!

event_title: Romance Writers of Australia – Perth Write-in
event_description:
**NOTE:**

* **THIS EVENT STARTS AT 10am PERTH TIME.**
* **You'll find us in the meeting room, which we have booked till 6pm. You're welcome to come whenever it's convenient for you.**
* **We'll break at 12.30pm for a 45-minute lunch and chat break.**

Are you a romance writer looking for some dedicated writing time in a cosy, supportive space? Join us for our monthly write-in at Dôme Manning, where words and coffee flow in equal measure!

WHAT'S A WRITE-IN?
A write-in is simply a chance to gather with fellow writers, settle in with your laptop or notebook, and focus on your writing projects. Whether you’re drafting swoony meet-cutes, editing that all-is-lost moment, or brainstorming your next happy-ever-after, this is your time to write.

WHAT TO EXPECT:
\- A relaxed\, welcoming vibe \(no pressure\, just pages\!\)
\- Friendly romance writers who “get it”
\- Time to focus on writing\, with a dedicated lunch break to chat and connect
\- Goodies from the Dôme menu available for you to purchase

WHO'S IT FOR?
While our sessions are hosted by the Romance Writers of Australia, all writers—new or experienced—of romance or romantic elements are welcome. Whether you’re writing short stories, novellas, or novels, come along and write in good company.

WHAT TO BRING:
Bring your laptop, notebook, headphones (if that's your vibe) or whatever else you need to write productively and let’s fill the café with creativity, community, and romance-writing magic.

event_description:
Hello Everyone!

Tom LW

event_title: RESILIENCY PLUS WORKSHOP
event_description:
RESILIENCY PLUS is an interactive 1-day workshop that provides strategies to help you bounce back when life presents challenging situations. It is designed to develop a plan of action for a more courageous and optimistic growth mindset, and patterns of inertia and helplessness.

Conducted in an atmosphere of joyful participation and creative activities, this course will provide you with invaluable tools to place in your mental and emotional toolkit.

A workbook will be provided that can be an ongoing resource throughout life.

Facilitated by Raelene Newall, a qualified counsellor.

Please RSVP with a call to 9313 1600 to ensure you have a place.

event_title: Prime Midlife May Monthly Reset Workshop
event_description:
In the midst of constant decision-making and responsibility, it's rare to find space to pause, process, and recalibrate, unless we intentionally create it.

​The Prime Midlife Monthly Reset is a guided 60-minute session designed to help you close the month with clarity, acknowledge your progress, and set deliberate direction for what comes next.

​Using the **Plus, Minus, Next** framework, a structured yet reflective approach that asks what worked, what didn't, and what to carry forward, you'll move from a state of constant doing to one of deliberate action.
​**In this session, you will:**

* ​Close open loops and create space to exhale
* ​Reconnect with your priorities and direction for the year
* ​Leave with one clear, tangible next step for the month ahead

​**What to bring:** your calendar, a to-do list or planner, any existing notes and a willingness to step away from distractions for one hour. A guided worksheet will be provided.

​**EVENT DETAILS**
​Date: 27 April 2026 (Monday)
​Time: 7:00 PM SGT
​Venue: Zoom *(link sent upon registration)*

​**Prime Midlife member: Complimentary**
​**Non-member: SGD 10**
Company Name: Pivotus Pte Ltd
Bank Name: ANEXT Bank
Paynow: 202514133R
Bank Account Number: 11086849111
**(💡 *Not a member yet? Joining Prime Midlife free and unlocks member benefits and the member rate today. Sign up [here](https://forms.gle/HXk2PRacVKPkn5mr6?utm_source=luma) before you register.)***

**​**Your Facilitator: Wai Ling Fong, PhD Wai Ling specialises in adult learning, learning design, and reflective practice. With a background in higher education and organisational development, she creates intentional spaces for clarity, alignment, and growth helping individuals move from reactive busyness to purposeful momentum.
